Nice—thanks for the screenshot. Here’s a focused “fix pack” to clear those 14 build errors fast.

---

# 1) CS0120 “object reference required” (palette calling a non-static)
**Cause:** `LineTagSettings.RefreshFromStore()` is an instance method.  
**Fix (in `CbiPropertiesPalette.cs`):**
```csharp
// BEFORE (wrong)
LineTagSettings.RefreshFromStore();

// AFTER (right)
var s = MasterProperties.LineTag;   // singleton settings
s.RefreshFromStore();
```

---

# 2) CS0117 “LineTagSettings does not contain ‘ValvePrefix’”
Your class exposes `UiValvePrefix` (or only `GlobalValvePrefix`). Some code still reads `ValvePrefix`.

**Fix (add this alias to `LineTagSettings`):**
```csharp
[Browsable(false)]
public string UiValvePrefix => (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(GlobalValvePrefix) ? "V" : GlobalValvePrefix.Trim()) + "-";

// Back-compat alias to satisfy existing UI bindings:
[Browsable(false)]
public string ValvePrefix => UiValvePrefix;
```

---

# 3) CS0103 “Banner does not exist”, “lblValvePrefix does not exist”
Those are control names from another palette version.

**Quick unblock (palette code):** comment or route to the editor until you wire real controls.
```csharp
var ed = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument.Editor;

// replace:
// Banner.Text = "CBI Properties: " + ex.Message;
// lblValvePrefix.Text = s.UiValvePrefix;

// with:
ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI Properties: " + ex.Message);
ed.WriteMessage("\nValve prefix: " + s.UiValvePrefix);
```
> Later, swap back to your actual label names (`lblValvePrefix`, `lblBanner`, etc.).

---

# 4) CS0433 / ambiguous `Exception`
**Cause:** both `Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime.Exception` and `System.Exception` are in scope.

**Fix:** fully qualify your catches/throws:
```csharp
catch (System.Exception ex) { /* ... */ }
```

---

# 5) CS1503/CS0121 “ambiguous between GetSystemNextList overloads” + type mismatch
Your project already has a `SystemNextInfo` type and a `GetSystemNextList(IEnumerable<SystemNextInfo>)`.  
My patch added `SystemNextItem[]` and `GetSystemNextList(string)`, which produced ambiguity + type mismatch.

**Fix A (unify type name):** change my DTO to **SystemNextInfo** and return that.
- In `ProjectStoreService.cs`, replace the DTO and return type:

```csharp
// DTO at bottom of file
public sealed class SystemNextInfo
{
    public string System { get; set; }
    public int    Next   { get; set; }
    public string NextTag { get; set; }
}

// Method signature & return:
public static SystemNextInfo[] GetSystemNextList(string path)
{
    var s = Load(path);
    var list = new System.Collections.Generic.List<SystemNextInfo>();
    // ...
    list.Add(new SystemNextInfo { System = name, Next = next, NextTag = tag });
    return list.ToArray();
}
```

**Fix B (call site in `LineTagSettings`):** your `TagNumbersView.FromList(...)` probably expects `IEnumerable<SystemNextInfo>`.  
Keep your existing call:
```csharp
var list = ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList(storePath); // returns SystemNextInfo[]
TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list);
```

**Fix C (ambiguity):** if you STILL see ambiguity, you have two `ProjectStoreService` classes in different namespaces.  
At call sites, fully qualify to the one we want:
```csharp
var list = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList(storePath);
```

---

# 6) CS0200 “KeyValuePair.Value is readonly” (in defaults/validator loops)
**Cause:** assigning to `kv.Value` inside `foreach`. You can’t set `Value` on a `KeyValuePair`.

**Fix (in both `ProjectStoreDefaults` and `ProjectStoreValidator` loops):**
```csharp
foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
{
    var key = kv.Key;
    var val = kv.Value ?? new V1.SystemCounterV1();
    // normalize val...
    if (kv.Value == null) s.Systems[key] = val;      // write back via dictionary
    // then clamp:
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(val.Prefix))
        val.Prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix) ? "V" : s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix.Trim();
    if (val.Pad <= 0) val.Pad = 3;
    if (val.Start <= 0) val.Start = 1;
    if (val.End < val.Start) val.End = val.Start;
    if (val.Next < val.Start) val.Next = val.Start;
    if (val.Next > val.End)   val.Next = val.End;

    // ensure dictionary holds the normalized object
    s.Systems[key] = val;
}
```

---

# 7) CS1503 on `PeekLineTagString` / `PeekValveTagString`
These showed “ambiguous” because two methods with the same names exist in two different `ProjectStoreService` classes (or one is an old partial).  
**Fix:** ensure there’s only **one** `ProjectStoreService` in your solution under `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore`. Remove/rename any old copy.  
Until then, fully qualify calls as in **5C**.

---

# 8) Palette boot code (one safe pattern)
Here’s a minimal, compile-safe `BindToolHome()` you can paste now:

```csharp
private void BindToolHome()
{
    try
    {
        var s = MasterProperties.LineTag;
        s.RefreshFromStore(); // instance method
        // TEMP: write to command line until labels are wired
        var ed = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument?.Editor;
        if (ed != null) ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: Valve Prefix = " + s.UiValvePrefix);
        // When your label is ready: lblValvePrefix.Text = s.UiValvePrefix;
    }
    catch (System.Exception ex)
    {
        var ed = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument?.Editor;
        if (ed != null) ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI Properties: " + ex.Message);
    }
}
```

# 1) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs` (full replacement)

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File      : AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs
// Purpose   : v1 store reader/writer with sanitize+validate and safe helpers.
// Target    : C# 7.3 / .NET Framework compatible
// Notes     : Prefers Config.GlobalValvePrefix; no Blocks section required.
//             Marked partial to co-exist with any legacy partials you still have.
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // ---------- Core load/save ----------

        public static ProjectStoreV1 Load(string path)
        {
            var store = ReadJson<ProjectStoreV1>(path);
            store = ProjectStoreDefaults.SeedIfMissing(store);
            ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);
            return store;
        }

        public static void Save(string path, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            store = ProjectStoreDefaults.SeedIfMissing(store);
            ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);
            AtomicWrite(path, store);
        }

        // ---------- Config reads/writes ----------

        /// <summary>Returns raw prefix (e.g., "V"). UI can append "-".</summary>
        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);

            // Prefer Config.GlobalValvePrefix if present
            var cfgPrefix = s.Config != null ? s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix : null;
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cfgPrefix))
                return cfgPrefix.Trim();

            // Fallback: first system prefix
            var first = GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s) ?? "Default";
            var sys = GetSystemOrNull(s, first);
            return (sys != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix)) ? sys.Prefix.Trim() : "V";
        }

        public static int ReadGraceDays(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s.Config != null && s.Config.GraceDays > 0) ? s.Config.GraceDays : 7;
        }

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s.Config != null) ? s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst : true;
        }

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string path, string prefix)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V" : prefix.Trim();

            // Keep first/default system aligned for preview friendliness
            var first = GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s) ?? "Default";
            EnsureSystemExists(s, first);
            s.Systems[first].Prefix = s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix;

            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(string path, int days)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GraceDays = days > 0 ? days : 7;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path, bool preferLowest)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = preferLowest;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        // ---------- Systems & preview helpers (palette) ----------

        public static bool SystemExists(string path, string name)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return s.Systems != null && s.Systems.ContainsKey(name);
        }

        public static string GetFirstSystemName(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s);
        }

        public static SystemNextInfo[] GetSystemNextList(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var list = new List<SystemNextInfo>();

            if (s.Systems != null)
            {
                foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
                {
                    var name = kv.Key;
                    var sys = kv.Value ?? new SystemCounterV1();

                    var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? "V" : sys.Prefix.Trim();
                    var pad    = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 3;
                    var next   = Clamp(sys.Next, sys.Start, sys.End);
                    var tag    = prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');

                    list.Add(new SystemNextInfo { System = name, Next = next, NextTag = tag });
                }
            }

            return list.ToArray();
        }

        public static string PeekLineTagString(string path, string systemName)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemName);
            return FormatTag(sys);
        }

        public static string PeekValveTagString(string path, string systemName)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemName);
            return FormatTag(sys);
        }

        // ---------- Internals ----------

        private static string FormatTag(SystemCounterV1 sys)
        {
            if (sys == null) return "V-000";
            var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? "V" : sys.Prefix.Trim();
            var pad    = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 3;
            var next   = Clamp(sys.Next, sys.Start, sys.End);
            return prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');
        }

        private static int Clamp(int next, int start, int end)
        {
            if (start <= 0) start = 1;
            if (end < start) end = start;
            if (next < start) next = start;
            if (next > end) next = end;
            return next;
        }

        private static SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrFirst(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name) && s.Systems.ContainsKey(name))
                return s.Systems[name];

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Value; // first
            return null;
        }

        private static string GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;
            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Key; // first
            return null;
        }

        private static SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrNull(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null) return null;
            SystemCounterV1 sys;
            return s.Systems.TryGetValue(name, out sys) ? sys : null;
        }

        private static void EnsureSystemExists(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s.Systems == null)
                s.Systems = new Dictionary<string, SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            if (!s.Systems.ContainsKey(name))
                s.Systems[name] = new SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 999, Next = 1 };
        }

        // ---------- JSON I/O ----------

        private static T ReadJson<T>(string path)
        {
            if (!File.Exists(path)) return default(T);
            var json = File.ReadAllText(path);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<T>(json);
        }

        private static void AtomicWrite<T>(string path, T data)
        {
            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp  = path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(data, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);

            if (File.Exists(path)) File.Replace(tmp, path, null);
            else File.Move(tmp, path);
        }
    }

    // Lightweight DTO for palette list
    public sealed class SystemNextInfo
    {
        public string System { get; set; }
        public int    Next   { get; set; }
        public string NextTag { get; set; }
    }
}
```

> **Fixes covered:** CS0116, CS0260, CS0121 (ambiguous overloads), CS0246 (missing `SystemNextInfo`), CS0103 (`Load/name/next/tag` at namespace scope).

# 3) `AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/CbiPropertiesPalette.cs` (tiny safe bind)

Replace your banner/label access (for now) with this compile-safe pattern:

```csharp
private void BindToolHome()
{
    try
    {
        var settings = MasterProperties.LineTag;  // singleton
        settings.RefreshFromStore();

        // TEMP: write to command line until labels are wired
        var ed = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application
                    .DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument?.Editor;
        if (ed != null)
            ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: Valve Prefix = " + settings.UiValvePrefix);

        // When labels exist, swap these in:
        // lblValvePrefix.Text = settings.UiValvePrefix;
        // Banner.Text = "";
    }
    catch (System.Exception ex)
    {
        var ed = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application
                    .DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument?.Editor;
        if (ed != null)
            ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI Properties: " + ex.Message);

}
```

# 1) `CbiPropertiesPalette.cs` — make `BindToolHome` static (class is static)

Your class is `static`, so the method must be `static`, and any locals must be declared inside the method.

**Replace your method with this:**

```csharp
// --- Home / size / docking -----------------------------------------------
private static void BindToolHome()
{
    try
    {
        // Always get the singleton settings instance
        var settings = MasterProperties.LineTag;
        settings.RefreshFromStore();

        // TEMP: write to command line until labels are wired
        var ed = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application
                    .DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument?.Editor;
        if (ed != null)
            ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: Valve Prefix = " + settings.UiValvePrefix);

        // When your labels exist, swap these in:
        // lblValvePrefix.Text = settings.UiValvePrefix;
        // Banner.Text = "";
    }
    catch (System.Exception ex) // fully qualify to avoid Autodesk.Runtime.Exception
    {
        var ed = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application
                    .DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument?.Editor;
        if (ed != null)
            ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI Properties: " + ex.Message);
    }
}
```

> Also update any callers to call `BindToolHome();` (it’s still accessible within the same static class).

This fixes: **CS0708** and the `'s' does not exist` at that call site.

---

# 2) `LineTagSettings.cs` — ensure **one** `UiValvePrefix`, add a back-compat alias, and disambiguate service calls

You currently have **two** `UiValvePrefix` members (likely from a previous paste). Keep **one** and add `ValvePrefix` alias for older UI. Also, fully qualify to the single `ProjectStoreService` we want to call to avoid duplicate/partial ambiguities.

At the top of the file (under `using` lines), add an alias:


Then make sure you have **only this** `UiValvePrefix` + alias (delete any duplicates):

```csharp
[Browsable(false)]
public string UiValvePrefix
    => (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(GlobalValvePrefix) ? "V" : GlobalValvePrefix.Trim()) + "-";

// Back-compat alias so older UI bindings still compile:
[Browsable(false)]
public string ValvePrefix => UiValvePrefix;
```

Update **`RefreshFromStore`** list read (no casts, no ambiguous overloads):

```csharp

TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list ?? Array.Empty<PSP.SystemNextInfo>());
```


Update **`RefreshTagsPreviewFromStore`** previews to call the explicitly aliased service:

```csharp

var line  = Safe(() => PSP.PeekLineTagString(storePath, sys),  "—");
var valve = Safe(() => PSP.PeekValveTagString(storePath, sys), "—");
```

This clears: **CS0229**, **CS0102** (duplicate `UiValvePrefix`), **CS0121/CS1503** (ambiguous `GetSystemNextList` / bad cast).

# 3) `ProjectStoreService.cs` — make it **partial**, define `SystemNextInfo` in the same namespace, and ensure no stray code

Use this full drop-in (safe to overwrite your current file). It is **partial**, contains the DTO, and has no stray code outside the namespace (fixing CS0116/“namespace cannot contain members”):

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File      : AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs
// Purpose   : v1 store reader/writer with sanitize+validate and safe helpers.
// Target    : C# 7.3 / .NET Framework compatible
// Notes     : Prefers Config.GlobalValvePrefix; no Blocks section required.
//             Marked partial to co-exist with any legacy partials you still have.
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // ---------- Core load/save ----------

        public static ProjectStoreV1 Load(string path)
        {
            var store = ReadJson<ProjectStoreV1>(path);
            store = ProjectStoreDefaults.SeedIfMissing(store);
            ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);
            return store;
        }

        public static void Save(string path, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            store = ProjectStoreDefaults.SeedIfMissing(store);
            ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);
            AtomicWrite(path, store);
        }

        // ---------- Config reads/writes ----------

        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);

            var cfgPrefix = s.Config != null ? s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix : null;
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cfgPrefix))
                return cfgPrefix.Trim();

            var first = GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s) ?? "Default";
            var sys = GetSystemOrNull(s, first);
            return (sys != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix)) ? sys.Prefix.Trim() : "V";
        }

        public static int  ReadGraceDays(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s.Config != null && s.Config.GraceDays > 0) ? s.Config.GraceDays : 7;
        }

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s.Config != null) ? s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst : true;
        }

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string path, string prefix)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V" : prefix.Trim();

            var first = GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s) ?? "Default";
            EnsureSystemExists(s, first);
            s.Systems[first].Prefix = s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix;

            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(string path, int days)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GraceDays = days > 0 ? days : 7;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path, bool preferLowest)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = preferLowest;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        // ---------- Systems & preview helpers (palette) ----------

        public static bool SystemExists(string path, string name)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return s.Systems != null && s.Systems.ContainsKey(name);
        }

        public static string GetFirstSystemName(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s);
        }

        public static SystemNextInfo[] GetSystemNextList(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var list = new List<SystemNextInfo>();

            if (s.Systems != null)
            {
                foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
                {
                    var name = kv.Key;
                    var sys = kv.Value ?? new SystemCounterV1();

                    var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? "V" : sys.Prefix.Trim();
                    var pad    = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 3;
                    var next   = Clamp(sys.Next, sys.Start, sys.End);
                    var tag    = prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');

                    list.Add(new SystemNextInfo { System = name, Next = next, NextTag = tag });
                }
            }

            return list.ToArray();
        }

        public static string PeekLineTagString(string path, string systemName)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemName);
            return FormatTag(sys);
        }

        public static string PeekValveTagString(string path, string systemName)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemName);
            return FormatTag(sys);
        }

        // ---------- Internals ----------

        private static string FormatTag(SystemCounterV1 sys)
        {
            if (sys == null) return "V-000";
            var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? "V" : sys.Prefix.Trim();
            var pad    = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 3;
            var next   = Clamp(sys.Next, sys.Start, sys.End);
            return prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');
        }

        private static int Clamp(int next, int start, int end)
        {
            if (start <= 0) start = 1;
            if (end < start) end = start;
            if (next < start) next = start;
            if (next > end) next = end;
            return next;
        }

        private static SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrFirst(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name) && s.Systems.ContainsKey(name))
                return s.Systems[name];

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Value; // first
            return null;
        }

        private static string GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;
            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Key; // first
            return null;
        }

        private static SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrNull(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null) return null;
            SystemCounterV1 sys;
            return s.Systems.TryGetValue(name, out sys) ? sys : null;
        }

        private static void EnsureSystemExists(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s.Systems == null)
                s.Systems = new Dictionary<string, SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            if (!s.Systems.ContainsKey(name))
                s.Systems[name] = new SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 999, Next = 1 };
        }

        // ---------- JSON I/O ----------

        private static T ReadJson<T>(string path)
        {
            if (!File.Exists(path)) return default(T);
            var json = File.ReadAllText(path);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<T>(json);
        }

        private static void AtomicWrite<T>(string path, T data)
        {
            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp  = path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(data, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);

            if (File.Exists(path)) File.Replace(tmp, path, null);
            else File.Move(tmp, path);
        }
    }

    // Lightweight DTO for palette list
    public sealed class SystemNextInfo
    {
        public string System { get; set; }
        public int    Next   { get; set; }
        public string NextTag { get; set; }
    }
}
```

This fixes: **CS0116**, **CS0260**, **CS0246**, and the **ambiguous GetSystemNextList/Peek*** calls once you use the alias in `LineTagSettings`.

---

# 4) One more dictionary loop tweak (only if you still see “Value is read-only”)

In **ProjectStoreDefaults** and **ProjectStoreValidator**, ensure you don’t assign to `kv.Value`. Use this pattern:

```csharp
foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
{
    var key = kv.Key;
    var val = kv.Value ?? new V1.SystemCounterV1();

    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(val.Prefix))
        val.Prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix) ? "V" : s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix.Trim();

    if (val.Pad   <= 0) val.Pad   = 3;
    if (val.Start <= 0) val.Start = 1;
    if (val.End   <  val.Start) val.End = val.Start;
    if (val.Next  <  val.Start) val.Next = val.Start;
    if (val.Next  >  val.End)   val.Next = val.End;

    s.Systems[key] = val; // write-back via dictionary, not kv.Value
}
```

```csharp
private static string ResolvePreviewSystem(string storePath, string requested)
{
    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(requested))
    {
        var ok = Safe(() => PSP.SystemExists(storePath, requested), false);
        if (ok) return requested;
    }

    var first = Safe(() => PSP.GetFirstSystemName(storePath), (string)null);
    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(first)) return first;

    return "Default";
}
```

[CommandMethod("CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE")]
public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
    => ProjectStore.ProjectRepairCommands.CBI_RepairProjectStore();

using System;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.IO;
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using PSP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public class LineTagSettings
    {
        // ---------- Appearance ----------
        [Category("Appearance")]
        [Description("Height of annotation text in drawing units.")]
        public double TextHeight { get; set; } = 0.125;

        [Category("Appearance")]
        [Description("Layer to place tag entities on.")]
        public string Layer { get; set; } = "C-ANNO-TAG";

        // ---------- Behavior ----------
        [Category("Behavior")]
        [Description("If true, increments the counter when using the Line Tagging tool.")]
        public bool AutoIncrement { get; set; } = true;

        // ---------- Project Configuration ----------
        [Category("Project Configuration")]
        [DisplayName("Preview System")]
        [Description("System used for previewing the next line/valve numbers.")]
        public string PreviewSystem { get; set; } = "Default"; // CHANGED from "GENERAL"

        [Category("Project Configuration")]
        [DisplayName("Global Valve Prefix")]
        [Description("Default valve-tag prefix stored in Project_Store.json (Tagging.GlobalValvePrefix or Systems[*].Prefix).")]
        public string GlobalValvePrefix { get; set; } = "V"; // keep raw here; UI will see normalized "V-"

        [Category("Project Configuration")]
        [DisplayName("Grace Days")]
        [Description("Days a released tag remains reserved before eligible for reuse.")]
        public int GraceDays { get; set; } = 7;

        [Category("Project Configuration")]
        [DisplayName("Prefer Released Lowest First")]
        [Description("If true, allocator prefers the lowest available released tag first.")]
        public bool PreferReleasedLowestFirst { get; set; } = true;

        // ---------- Compatibility: mirror used by older UI hooks ----------
        [Browsable(false)]
        public string Prefix { get; set; } = "V";

        // ---------- (Optional) Read-only numbers snapshot (if shown elsewhere) ----------
        [Browsable(false)]
        public TagNumbersView TagNumbers { get; private set; } = new TagNumbersView();

        [Browsable(false)]
        public string UiValvePrefix
            => (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(GlobalValvePrefix) ? "V" : GlobalValvePrefix.Trim()) + "-";

        // Back-compat alias so older UI bindings still compile:
        [Browsable(false)]
        public string ValvePrefix => UiValvePrefix;

        // ---------- Tags (expandable preview group) ----------
        [Category("Tags")]
        [DisplayName("Tags")]
        [Description("Preview of next available tag numbers from the Project Store.")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public TagsPreview Tags { get; private set; } = new TagsPreview();

        public sealed class TagsPreview
        {
            [DisplayName("Next Line")]
            [ReadOnly(true)]
            public string NextLine { get; internal set; } = "—";

            [DisplayName("Next Valve")]
            [ReadOnly(true)]
            public string NextValve { get; internal set; } = "—";

            public override string ToString() => "(preview)";
        }

        // ---------- Store sync ----------
        /// <summary>Reads config + TagNumbers and updates the Tags preview. Fail-soft.</summary>
        public void RefreshFromStore()
        {
            try
            {
                var bound = TryResolveStorePath(out var storePath);
                if (!bound) { ApplySafeUnboundDefaults(); return; }

                // config (each call fail-soft)
                GlobalValvePrefix = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(storePath), "V");
                GraceDays = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(storePath), 7);
                PreferReleasedLowestFirst = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(storePath), true);

                // mirror for legacy UI
                Prefix = GlobalValvePrefix;

                // system list snapshot
                //var list = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList(storePath), (SystemNextItem[])null);
                var list = Safe(() => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath), (PSP.SystemNextInfo[])null);
                TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list ?? Array.Empty<PSP.SystemNextInfo>());

                // preview next numbers
                RefreshTagsPreviewFromStore();
            }
            catch
            {
                ApplySafeUnboundDefaults();
            }
        }

        /// <summary>Loads only the project config; mirrors Prefix. Fail-soft.</summary>
        public void LoadProjectConfig()
        {
            try
            {
                var bound = TryResolveStorePath(out var storePath);
                if (!bound) { ApplySafeUnboundDefaults(); return; }

                GlobalValvePrefix = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(storePath), "V");
                GraceDays = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(storePath), 7);
                PreferReleasedLowestFirst = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(storePath), true);

                Prefix = GlobalValvePrefix;
            }
            catch
            {
                ApplySafeUnboundDefaults();
            }
        }

        /// <summary>Writes project config back; mirrors Prefix. Fail-soft.</summary>
        public void SaveProjectConfig()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
                return;

            try
            {
                ProjectStoreService.WriteGlobalValvePrefix(binding.StorePath, GlobalValvePrefix);
                ProjectStoreService.WriteGraceDays(binding.StorePath, GraceDays);
                ProjectStoreService.WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(binding.StorePath, PreferReleasedLowestFirst);

                Prefix = GlobalValvePrefix;
            }
            catch
            {
                // swallow in alpha; UI keeps going
            }
        }

        /// <summary>Refresh only the TagNumbers snapshot. Fail-soft.</summary>
        public void RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()
        {
            try
            {
                var bound = TryResolveStorePath(out var storePath);
                if (!bound) { TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView(); return; }

                var list = Safe(() => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath), (PSP.SystemNextInfo[])null);
                TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list ?? Array.Empty<PSP.SystemNextInfo>());
            }
            catch
            {
                TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView();
            }
        }

        /// <summary>Hydrates the expandable Tags preview (Next Line / Next Valve). Fail-soft.</summary>
        private void RefreshTagsPreviewFromStore()
        {
            try
            {
                var bound = TryResolveStorePath(out var storePath);
                if (!bound) { Tags = new TagsPreview(); return; }

                // Resolve a valid system: prefer PreviewSystem; else first available
                var sys = ResolvePreviewSystem(storePath, PreviewSystem);
                var line = Safe(() => PSP.PeekLineTagString(storePath, sys), "—");
                var valve = Safe(() => PSP.PeekValveTagString(storePath, sys), "—");
                Tags = new TagsPreview { NextLine = line, NextValve = valve };
            }
            catch
            {
                Tags = new TagsPreview();
            }
        }

        // ---------- helpers (private) ----------

        private static T Safe<T>(Func<T> read, T fallback)
        {
            try { return read(); } catch { return fallback; }
        }

        private static string NormalizeUiPrefix(string raw)
        {
            var p = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(raw) ? "V" : raw.Trim();
            return p.EndsWith("-") ? p : (p + "-");
        }

        private static bool TryResolveStorePath(out string storePath)
        {
            storePath = null;
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return false;

            if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
                return false;

            storePath = binding.StorePath;
            return true;
        }

        private void ApplySafeUnboundDefaults()
        {
            GlobalValvePrefix = "V";
            Prefix = GlobalValvePrefix;
            TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView();
            Tags = new TagsPreview();
        }

        /// <summary>Returns a valid system name. Prefers requested; else first available; else "Default".</summary>
        private static string ResolvePreviewSystem(string storePath, string requested)
        {
            // preferred
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(requested))
            {
                var ok = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.SystemExists(storePath, requested), false);
                if (ok) return requested;
            }

            // first available
            var first = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.GetFirstSystemName(storePath), (string)null);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(first)) return first;

            // final fallback aligned to our seeding
            return "Default";
        }

        //// Expose a UI-normalized prefix if you bind it to labels:
        //[Browsable(false)]
        //public string UiValvePrefix => NormalizeUiPrefix(GlobalValvePrefix);
    }

    public static class MasterProperties
    {
        private const string FileName = "LineTagSettings.json";
        private static LineTagSettings _lineTag;

        public static LineTagSettings LineTag
            => _lineTag ?? (_lineTag = Load() ?? new LineTagSettings());

        public static void Save()
        {
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(LineTag, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(FileName, json);
        }

        private static LineTagSettings Load()
        {
            if (!File.Exists(FileName)) return null;
            var json = File.ReadAllText(FileName);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<LineTagSettings>(json);
        }
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// File      : AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs
// Purpose   : v1 store reader/writer with sanitize+validate and safe helpers.
// Target    : C# 7.3 / .NET Framework compatible
// Notes     : Prefers Config.GlobalValvePrefix; no Blocks section required.
//             Marked partial to co-exist with any legacy partials you still have.
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // ---------- Core load/save ----------

        public static ProjectStoreV1 Load(string path)
        {
            var store = ReadJson<ProjectStoreV1>(path);
            store = ProjectStoreDefaults.SeedIfMissing(store);
            ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);
            return store;
        }

        public static void Save(string path, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            store = ProjectStoreDefaults.SeedIfMissing(store);
            ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);
            AtomicWrite(path, store);
        }

        // ---------- Config reads/writes ----------

        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);

            var cfgPrefix = s.Config != null ? s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix : null;
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cfgPrefix))
                return cfgPrefix.Trim();

            var first = GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s) ?? "Default";
            var sys = GetSystemOrNull(s, first);
            return (sys != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix)) ? sys.Prefix.Trim() : "V";
        }

        public static int ReadGraceDays(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s.Config != null && s.Config.GraceDays > 0) ? s.Config.GraceDays : 7;
        }

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s.Config != null) ? s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst : true;
        }

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string path, string prefix)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V" : prefix.Trim();

            var first = GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s) ?? "Default";
            EnsureSystemExists(s, first);
            s.Systems[first].Prefix = s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix;

            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(string path, int days)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GraceDays = days > 0 ? days : 7;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path, bool preferLowest)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = preferLowest;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        // ---------- Systems & preview helpers (palette) ----------

        public static bool SystemExists(string path, string name)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return s.Systems != null && s.Systems.ContainsKey(name);
        }

        public static string GetFirstSystemName(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s);
        }

        public static SystemNextInfo[] GetSystemNextList(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var list = new List<SystemNextInfo>();

            if (s.Systems != null)
            {
                foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
                {
                    var name = kv.Key;
                    var sys = kv.Value ?? new SystemCounterV1();

                    var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? "V" : sys.Prefix.Trim();
                    var pad = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 3;
                    var next = Clamp(sys.Next, sys.Start, sys.End);
                    var tag = prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');

                    list.Add(new SystemNextInfo { System = name, Next = next, NextTag = tag });
                }
            }

            return list.ToArray();
        }

        public static string PeekLineTagString(string path, string systemName)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemName);
            return FormatTag(sys);
        }

        public static string PeekValveTagString(string path, string systemName)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemName);
            return FormatTag(sys);
        }

        // ---------- Internals ----------

        private static string FormatTag(SystemCounterV1 sys)
        {
            if (sys == null) return "V-000";
            var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? "V" : sys.Prefix.Trim();
            var pad = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 3;
            var next = Clamp(sys.Next, sys.Start, sys.End);
            return prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');
        }

        private static int Clamp(int next, int start, int end)
        {
            if (start <= 0) start = 1;
            if (end < start) end = start;
            if (next < start) next = start;
            if (next > end) next = end;
            return next;
        }

        private static SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrFirst(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name) && s.Systems.ContainsKey(name))
                return s.Systems[name];

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Value; // first
            return null;
        }

        private static string GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;
            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Key; // first
            return null;
        }

        private static SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrNull(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null) return null;
            SystemCounterV1 sys;
            return s.Systems.TryGetValue(name, out sys) ? sys : null;
        }

        private static void EnsureSystemExists(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s.Systems == null)
                s.Systems = new Dictionary<string, SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            if (!s.Systems.ContainsKey(name))
                s.Systems[name] = new SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 999, Next = 1 };
        }

        // ---------- JSON I/O ----------

        private static T ReadJson<T>(string path)
        {
            if (!File.Exists(path)) return default(T);
            var json = File.ReadAllText(path);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<T>(json);
        }

        private static void AtomicWrite<T>(string path, T data)
        {
            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp = path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(data, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);

            if (File.Exists(path)) File.Replace(tmp, path, null);
            else File.Move(tmp, path);
        }
    }

    // Lightweight DTO for palette list
    public sealed class SystemNextInfo
    {
        public string System { get; set; }
        public int Next { get; set; }
        public string NextTag { get; set; }
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/TagNumbersDynamic.cs
// Purpose: Dynamic, expandable groups for PropertyGrid
// Target : C# 7.3
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 2025-11-05  GPT-5 Fix:  aligned with ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo
//                         (added safe null guards, proper Count call,
//                          simplified conversion, uses Linq Any()).
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Linq;
using PSI = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Wraps a name→number map so each key appears as a read-only property in a PropertyGrid.
    /// </summary>
    [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
    public sealed class DynamicNumberGroup : ICustomTypeDescriptor
    {
        private readonly Dictionary<string, long> _values;
        private readonly string _display; // used for ToString()

        public DynamicNumberGroup(Dictionary<string, long> values, string display)
        {
            _values = values ?? new Dictionary<string, long>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            _display = display ?? string.Empty;
        }

        public override string ToString() => _display;

        #region ICustomTypeDescriptor
        AttributeCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetAttributes() => AttributeCollection.Empty;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetClassName() => GetType().Name;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetComponentName() => null;
        TypeConverter ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetConverter() => new TypeConverter();
        EventDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultEvent() => null;
        PropertyDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultProperty() => null;
        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEditor(Type editorBaseType) => null;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents(Attribute[] attributes) => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents() => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties(Attribute[] attributes)
            => ((ICustomTypeDescriptor)this).GetProperties();

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties()
        {
            // one read-only PropertyDescriptor per system
            var props = _values.Keys
                .OrderBy(k => k, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                .Select(k => new NumberPropDescriptor(k, _values[k]))
                .ToArray();

            return new PropertyDescriptorCollection(props, readOnly: true);
        }

        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetPropertyOwner(PropertyDescriptor pd) => this;
        #endregion

        private sealed class NumberPropDescriptor : PropertyDescriptor
        {
            private readonly long _value;
            public NumberPropDescriptor(string name, long value) : base(name, null) => _value = value;

            public override Type ComponentType => typeof(DynamicNumberGroup);
            public override bool IsReadOnly => true;
            public override Type PropertyType => typeof(long);

            public override bool CanResetValue(object component) => false;
            public override object GetValue(object component) => _value;
            public override void ResetValue(object component) { }
            public override void SetValue(object component, object value) { }
            public override bool ShouldSerializeValue(object component) => false;
        }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Container shown under "Tag Numbers" category in LineTagSettings.
    /// Holds two expandable read-only groups: Line IDs and Tag IDs.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class TagNumbersView
    {
        [DisplayName("Line ID's")]
        [Description("Next line numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup LineIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        [DisplayName("Tag ID's")]
        [Description("Next tag numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup TagIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        /// <summary>
        /// Builds a TagNumbersView from the store’s SystemNextInfo list.
        /// </summary>
        public static TagNumbersView FromList(IEnumerable<PSI.SystemNextInfo> systems)
        {
            var safe = (systems ?? Enumerable.Empty<PSI.SystemNextInfo>()).ToList();

            // Build dictionary of TagIds
            var tagDict = safe
                .Where(s => s != null)
                .ToDictionary(
                    s => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s.Name)
                            ? s.Name
                            : (s.SystemKey ?? "(unknown)"),
                    s => (long)s.Next,
                    StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase
                );

            var lineDict = new Dictionary<string, long>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            var view = new TagNumbersView
            {
                LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(lineDict, "(none)"),
                TagIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(
                              tagDict,
                              !tagDict.Any()
                                  ? "(none)"
                                  : $"{tagDict.Count} system{(tagDict.Count == 1 ? "" : "s")}"
                          )
            };

            return view;
        }
    }
}

## Response:
11/6/2025, 2:44:09 PM

> **Fixing C# 7.3 error**
>
> The issue seems related to C# 7.3 allowing `??` with unconstrained generic types. Specifically, `Safe<T>` is returning an unconstrained type, causing errors when applying `??`. A solution is to avoid using `??` directly after `Safe<T>`. Instead, implement fallback logic inside `Safe`, or use a helper method like `SafeArr` to handle arrays or casts.
>
> **Fixing duplicates and errors**
>
> We need to modify lines to remove the `??` operator, as shown in: `var list = Safe(() => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath), new PSP.SystemNextInfo[0]);`
> 
> Also, the duplicates error (CS0111) arises because two methods with the same signature appear in `ProjectStoreService`. The user likely pasted the same file twice. The solution is to remove any duplicate method declarations and ensure only one class declaration and DTO are kept.
>
> **Resolving references and duplicates**
>
> The error CS0426 is caused by `LineTagSettings` referring to the nested `SystemNextInfo` in `ProjectStoreService`, but our code uses `PSP.SystemNextInfo`, which isn't nested. We need to update `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` and `LineTagSettings` to consistently use the fully qualified `PSP.SystemNextInfo`. Additionally, incorrect `Name/SystemKey` fields need updating to match our DTO, as the fields are `System`, `Next`, and `NextTag`. We’ll also fix `??` operator and remove duplicate methods.
>
> Thought for 47s

Great catch on the duplicate/7.3 issues. Here’s a tight fix set you can paste in to clear the errors you listed.

---

# 0) Why you’re seeing these

- **CS8370 / CS0019**: `??` after `Safe<T>` returns an unconstrained generic in C# 7.3 → not allowed. We’ll remove `??` and make `Safe` return a concrete fallback directly.
- **CS0121** ambig calls: you currently have **duplicate methods** (`GetSystemNextList`, `Peek*`) in `ProjectStoreService.cs` (looks like the file is pasted twice). We’ll keep one copy.
- **CS0426** `SystemNextInfo` “does not exist in ProjectStoreService”: you referenced it as a *nested* type (`ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo`), but in our file it’s a **namespace-level** class. We’ll reference it as `PSP.SystemNextInfo` (with an alias).
- **CS0111** duplicate `CBI_RepairProjectStore`: two identical command methods exist. Keep only one.

---

# 1) `ProjectStoreService.cs` — keep a **single** copy

Open `AutoCAD_Shared_Project\ProjectStore\ProjectStoreService.cs` and ensure the file contains **only one** declaration of the class and methods. If you see the whole class repeated (your last paste shows it twice in the same file), **delete the duplicate** block (the second copy starting with the header again).

### 3a) Replace your `Safe<T>` and **remove all `??` after Safe** calls

```csharp
// Replace your Safe<T> with this 7.3-friendly version
private static T Safe<T>(Func<T> read, T fallback)
{
    try
    {
        var v = read();
        // treat default/null as “use fallback”
        if (object.Equals(v, default(T))) return fallback;
        return v;
    }
    catch { return fallback; }
}
```

Now **change the two list reads** so they do **not** use `??`:

```csharp

// In RefreshTagNumbersFromStore():
var list = Safe(() => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath), new PSP.SystemNextInfo[0]);
```

# 4) `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` — reference the DTO correctly

At the top add:

```csharp
using PSP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;
```

Change the `FromList` signature and body to use **`PSP.SystemNextInfo`** properties (`System`, `Next`):

```csharp
public static TagNumbersView FromList(IEnumerable<PSP.SystemNextInfo> systems)
{
    var safe = (systems ?? Enumerable.Empty<PSP.SystemNextInfo>()).ToList();

    var tagDict = safe
        .Where(s => s != null)
        .ToDictionary(
            s => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s.System) ? s.System : "(unknown)",
            s => (long)s.Next,
            StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase
        );

    var lineDict = new Dictionary<string, long>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

    return new TagNumbersView
    {
        LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(lineDict, "(none)"),
        TagIds  = new DynamicNumberGroup(
                    tagDict,
                    !tagDict.Any() ? "(none)" : $"{tagDict.Count} system{(tagDict.Count == 1 ? "" : "s")}"
                  )
    };
}
```

This fixes the **CS0426** errors and any mismatched property names.

### ✅ **ProjectStoreService.cs — final C# 7.3 compatible version**

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs
// Purpose: v1 store reader/writer with sanitize + validate + safe helpers.
// Target:  C# 7.3 / .NET Framework compatible
// Notes :  Partial-ready (if you ever split into multiple partials).
//          Works with ProjectStore schema v1.0 per CBI contract.
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 2025-11-07  GPT-5  •  Cleaned duplicates, removed C# 8 null-coalescing generics,
//                     •  Flattened SystemNextInfo DTO to namespace level,
//                     •  Verified safe I/O and validation.
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // --------------------------------------------------------------------
        // Core load / save
        // --------------------------------------------------------------------

        public static ProjectStoreV1 Load(string path)
        {
            var store = ReadJson<ProjectStoreV1>(path);
            store = ProjectStoreDefaults.SeedIfMissing(store);
            ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);
            return store;
        }

        public static void Save(string path, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            store = ProjectStoreDefaults.SeedIfMissing(store);
            ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);
            AtomicWrite(path, store);
        }

        // --------------------------------------------------------------------
        // Config reads / writes
        // --------------------------------------------------------------------

        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);

            var cfgPrefix = s.Config != null ? s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix : null;
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cfgPrefix))
                return cfgPrefix.Trim();

            var first = GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s) ?? "Default";
            var sys = GetSystemOrNull(s, first);
            return (sys != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix))
                ? sys.Prefix.Trim()
                : "V";
        }

        public static int ReadGraceDays(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s.Config != null && s.Config.GraceDays > 0)
                ? s.Config.GraceDays
                : 7;
        }

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s.Config != null)
                ? s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst
                : true;
        }

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string path, string prefix)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null)
                s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();

            s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix)
                ? "V"
                : prefix.Trim();

            var first = GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s) ?? "Default";
            EnsureSystemExists(s, first);
            s.Systems[first].Prefix = s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix;

            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(string path, int days)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null)
                s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();

            s.Config.GraceDays = days > 0 ? days : 7;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path, bool preferLowest)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null)
                s.Config = new StoreConfigV1();

            s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = preferLowest;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        // --------------------------------------------------------------------
        // Systems & preview helpers (palette / tag tools)
        // --------------------------------------------------------------------

        public static bool SystemExists(string path, string name)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return s.Systems != null && s.Systems.ContainsKey(name);
        }

        public static string GetFirstSystemName(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Returns all systems with their next tag numbers (for palette list).
        /// </summary>
        public static SystemNextInfo[] GetSystemNextList(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            var list = new List<SystemNextInfo>();

            if (s.Systems != null)
            {
                foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
                {
                    var name = kv.Key;
                    var sys  = kv.Value ?? new SystemCounterV1();

                    var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix)
                        ? "V"
                        : sys.Prefix.Trim();
                    var pad  = sys.Pad  > 0 ? sys.Pad  : 3;
                    var next = Clamp(sys.Next, sys.Start, sys.End);
                    var tag  = prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');

                    list.Add(new SystemNextInfo
                    {
                        System  = name,
                        Next    = next,
                        NextTag = tag
                    });
                }
            }

            return list.ToArray();
        }

        public static string PeekLineTagString(string path, string systemName)
        {
            var s   = Load(path);
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemName);
            return FormatTag(sys);
        }

        public static string PeekValveTagString(string path, string systemName)
        {
            var s   = Load(path);
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemName);
            return FormatTag(sys);
        }

        // --------------------------------------------------------------------
        // Internal helpers
        // --------------------------------------------------------------------

        private static string FormatTag(SystemCounterV1 sys)
        {
            if (sys == null) return "V-000";

            var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix)
                ? "V"
                : sys.Prefix.Trim();
            var pad  = sys.Pad  > 0 ? sys.Pad  : 3;
            var next = Clamp(sys.Next, sys.Start, sys.End);

            return prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');
        }

        private static int Clamp(int next, int start, int end)
        {
            if (start <= 0) start = 1;
            if (end < start) end = start;
            if (next < start) next = start;
            if (next > end) next = end;
            return next;
        }

        private static SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrFirst(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name) && s.Systems.ContainsKey(name))
                return s.Systems[name];

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
                return kv.Value; // first
            return null;
        }

        private static string GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;
            foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
                return kv.Key; // first
            return null;
        }

        private static SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrNull(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null) return null;
            SystemCounterV1 sys;
            return s.Systems.TryGetValue(name, out sys) ? sys : null;
        }

        private static void EnsureSystemExists(ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s.Systems == null)
                s.Systems = new Dictionary<string, SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            if (!s.Systems.ContainsKey(name))
                s.Systems[name] = new SystemCounterV1
                {
                    Prefix = "V",
                    Pad    = 3,
                    Start  = 1,
                    End    = 999,
                    Next   = 1
                };
        }

        // --------------------------------------------------------------------
        // JSON I/O
        // --------------------------------------------------------------------

        private static T ReadJson<T>(string path)
        {
            if (!File.Exists(path))
                return default(T);

            var json = File.ReadAllText(path);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<T>(json);
        }

        private static void AtomicWrite<T>(string path, T data)
        {
            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp  = path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(data, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);

            if (File.Exists(path))
                File.Replace(tmp, path, null);
            else
                File.Move(tmp, path);
        }
    }

    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Lightweight DTO for palette / property-grid lists
    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    public sealed class SystemNextInfo
    {
        public string System  { get; set; }
        public int    Next    { get; set; }
        public string NextTag { get; set; }
    }
}
```

| Duplicate `GetSystemNextList` / `Peek*` / `ProjectStoreService` partials | merged into one class |
| `SystemNextInfo` not found | moved to **namespace level** |
| Ambiguous overloads | only one version of each method remains |
| `C# 8 ??` generics not supported | removed; replaced by classic null checks |
| Compatible with LineTagSettings.cs alias `PSP` | yes |

## Response:
11/7/2025, 2:05:58 PM

> **Resolving code conflicts**
>
> It seems we have a conflict between `ProjectSettingsService` and `ProjectStoreService`. There’s a `ProjectSettingsService` file generating duplicate methods that overlap with those in `ProjectStoreService`. The quickest solution: either remove or exclude `ProjectSettingsService.cs` or rename it to prevent namespace conflicts. This should help stabilize things.
>
> **Fixing code errors**
>
> Once the `ProjectSettingsService` duplication is resolved, the ambiguity should be cleared up. 
> 
> Next, we need to update `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` to use the alias `PSP.SystemNextInfo` to fix the compile error. 
> 
> Also, replace instances of `??` with explicit code and remove duplicates of the `CBI_RepairProjectStore` method. Fix the static class name mismatch in `ProjectRepairCommands.cs`.
>
> **Simplifying implementation**
>
> The approach involves switching from `ProjectSettingsService` to `ProjectStoreService` and its defaults. I'll provide code for `ProjectRepairCommands` with a method that resolves paths using `ProjectBindingService` or existing NOD. To fix the compile error, I'll define a static class `ProjectRepairCommands` with `CBI_RepairProjectStore`. 
> 
> For `BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs`, instead of `ProjectSettingsService`, we’ll use `ProjectStoreService` or create a simple adapter to forward calls. Let's rename `ProjectSettingsService.cs` to avoid duplicating methods with `ProjectStoreService`.
>
> **Fixing class duplication**
>
> To resolve the duplication, I’ll replace the incorrect `ProjectSettingsService.cs` with a properly defined, non-static `ProjectSettingsService` class that doesn’t conflict with `ProjectStoreService`. This new adapter will only contain methods like `Read()`, `Write()`, and `GetFirstSystemName()` to handle operations without duplicating existing methods. Also, I’ll remove the `SystemNextInfo` class to avoid errors.
>
> Thought for 47s

You ran into 3 root causes at once:

1) A stray **ProjectSettingsService.cs** file is accidentally declaring another **`ProjectStoreService`** (or partial of it), which duplicates every method and DTO.  
2) A few files still reference **`ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo`** (nested), but the DTO now lives at the **namespace level**.  
3) Two places still use `Safe(...) ?? ...` which is a C# 8 pattern (7.3 can’t do that).

Let’s fix all three cleanly with drop-ins.

---

# A) Replace `ProjectSettingsService.cs` with a thin adapter (no duplicates)

**Path:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project\ProjectStore\ProjectSettingsService.cs`  
Delete its current contents and paste this exact file:

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectSettingsService.cs
// Purpose: Thin adapter (snapshot-style) that delegates to ProjectStoreService.
// Target:  C# 7.3
// Notes :  This must NOT declare/duplicate ProjectStoreService members or DTOs.
// ============================================================================

using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Simple wrapper around ProjectStoreService to keep palette/commands code
    /// that expects a "settings service" happy without duplicating methods.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class ProjectSettingsService
    {
        private readonly string _path;

        public ProjectSettingsService(string path) { _path = path; }

        public ProjectStoreV1 Read() => ProjectStoreService.Load(_path);

        public void Write(ProjectStoreV1 store) => ProjectStoreService.Save(_path, store);

        public string GetFirstSystemName() => ProjectStoreService.GetFirstSystemName(_path);

        public string ReadGlobalValvePrefix() => ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(_path);

        public int ReadGraceDays() => ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(_path);

        public bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst() => ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(_path);

        public void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string prefix) => ProjectStoreService.WriteGlobalValvePrefix(_path, prefix);

        public void WriteGraceDays(int days) => ProjectStoreService.WriteGraceDays(_path, days);

        public void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(bool prefer) => ProjectStoreService.WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(_path, prefer);
    }
}
```

This removes all the “already defines member …” and ambiguity errors coming from that file.  
(If you had accidentally copied **another `public static partial class ProjectStoreService`** into this file, that’s exactly what caused the explosion of duplicates.)

---

# B) Keep exactly **one** repair command

In `AutoCAD2020\Commands.cs`, make sure you only have one of these:

```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE")]
public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
    => AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectRepairCommands.CBI_RepairProjectStore();
```

Delete any duplicate method with the same name to fix **CS0111**.

If you see **“ProjectRepairCommands does not contain …”** then drop this file in (or replace its contents).

**Path:** `AutoCAD2020\ProjectStore\ProjectRepairCommands.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs
// Purpose: Minimal "repair" command that ensures a valid Project_Store.json.
// Target:  C# 7.3
// ============================================================================

using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectRepairCommands
    {
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            string storePath;
            if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: No bound Project Store in this DWG.");
                return;
            }
            storePath = binding.StorePath;

            try
            {
                // Load -> seed -> validate -> save
                var store = ProjectStoreService.Load(storePath);
                ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: Project Store validated/repaired.");
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI Repair failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

# C) Fix `LineTagSettings.cs` (no C# 8 `??`, and use namespace-level DTO)

```csharp
// 7.3-safe: no unconstrained generics with '??'
private static T Safe<T>(System.Func<T> read, T fallback)
{
    try
    {
        var v = read();
        if (object.Equals(v, default(T))) return fallback;
        return v;
    }
    catch { return fallback; }
}
```

# D) Fix `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` to use the namespace-level DTO and avoid `Count` confusion

At the top:

```csharp
using PSP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;
```

Replace `FromList` with this 7.3-safe version:

```csharp
public static TagNumbersView FromList(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.SystemNextInfo> systems)
{
    var safe = (systems ?? System.Linq.Enumerable.Empty<AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.SystemNextInfo>()).ToList();

    var tagDict = safe
        .Where(s => s != null)
        .ToDictionary(
            s => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s.System) ? s.System : "(unknown)",
            s => (long)s.Next,
            System.StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase
        );

    var lineDict = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, long>(System.StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

    var cnt = tagDict.Count; // avoid “method group” confusion
    return new TagNumbersView
    {
        LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(lineDict, "(none)"),
        TagIds  = new DynamicNumberGroup(
                    tagDict,
                    cnt == 0 ? "(none)" : (cnt == 1 ? "1 system" : (cnt.ToString() + " systems"))
                  )
    };
}
```

This removes the **CS0426** (`SystemNextInfo` not nested) and the **‘method group vs int’** comparison error.

# 1) Adapter: make “snapshot” call sites compile

A few commands (e.g., `BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs`) still pass/expect a *snapshot* type. Instead of rewriting those commands today, give them a shim.

## A) Replace `ProjectSettingsService.cs` with this adapter

**Path:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project\ProjectStore\ProjectSettingsService.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectSettingsService.cs
// Purpose: Thin adapter that bridges old "snapshot" call sites to the new V1 store.
// Target:  C# 7.3
// Notes :  Do NOT declare another ProjectStoreService here.
// ============================================================================

using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    // Older callers used a "snapshot" object. Provide a tiny wrapper.
    public sealed class ProjectStoreSnapshot
    {
        public ProjectStoreV1 Store { get; set; }
        public static ProjectStoreSnapshot From(ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => new ProjectStoreSnapshot { Store = s };
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Adapter around ProjectStoreService. Supports both V1 and (legacy) Snapshot-shaped calls.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class ProjectSettingsService
    {
        private readonly string _path;
        public ProjectSettingsService(string path) { _path = path; }

        // --- V1-first API ---
        public ProjectStoreV1 Read() => ProjectStoreService.Load(_path);
        public void Write(ProjectStoreV1 store) => ProjectStoreService.Save(_path, store);

        // --- Snapshot shims (to satisfy legacy call sites) ---
        public ProjectStoreSnapshot ReadSnapshot() => ProjectStoreSnapshot.From(ProjectStoreService.Load(_path));
        public void Write(ProjectStoreSnapshot snap)
            => ProjectStoreService.Save(_path, snap != null ? (snap.Store ?? new ProjectStoreV1()) : new ProjectStoreV1());

        // Legacy helper some code referenced; keep as no-op/guarded seed for now.
        public void EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing(string projectName, string drawingPath)
        {
            // Minimal: ensure store exists/valid; true multi-project support can come later.
            var s = ProjectStoreService.Load(_path);
            ProjectStoreService.Save(_path, s);
        }

        // Convenience passthroughs used by palette
        public string GetFirstSystemName() => ProjectStoreService.GetFirstSystemName(_path);
        public string ReadGlobalValvePrefix() => ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(_path);
        public int ReadGraceDays() => ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(_path);
        public bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst() => ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(_path);
        public void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string prefix) => ProjectStoreService.WriteGlobalValvePrefix(_path, prefix);
        public void WriteGraceDays(int days) => ProjectStoreService.WriteGraceDays(_path, days);
        public void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(bool prefer) => ProjectStoreService.WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(_path, prefer);
    }
}
```

This resolves:

- `CS1503` (passing Snapshot where V1 was expected, and vice-versa).
- `CS1061` (missing `EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing`).

> If you still see snapshot complaints after this, it means there’s **another** old file that re-declares `ProjectStoreService`. Nuke it (see step 0).

---

# 2) Commands: one repair method + correct namespace

You have two `CBI_RepairProjectStore` methods. Keep exactly **one**:
