```csharp
// AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs
[CommandMethod("CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE")]
public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
    => AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectRepairCommands.CBI_RepairProjectStore();
```

If you still get `CS0117 ProjectRepairCommands does not contain ...`, make sure you have this file and its namespace matches:

```csharp
// AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectRepairCommands
    {
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: No bound Project Store in this DWG.");
                return;
            }

            try
            {
                var store = ProjectStoreService.Load(binding.StorePath);
                ProjectStoreService.Save(binding.StorePath, store);
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: Project Store validated/repaired.");
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI Repair failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

Remove any *other* `CBI_RepairProjectStore` methods.

```csharp
public static TagNumbersView FromList(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.SystemNextInfo> systems)
{
    var safe = (systems ?? System.Linq.Enumerable.Empty<AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.SystemNextInfo>()).ToList();

    var tagDict = safe
        .Where(s => s != null)
        .ToDictionary(
            s => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s.System) ? s.System : "(unknown)",
            s => (long)s.Next,
            System.StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase
        );

    var lineDict = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, long>(System.StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

    var cnt = tagDict.Count;
    return new TagNumbersView
    {
        LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(lineDict, "(none)"),
        TagIds  = new DynamicNumberGroup(tagDict, cnt == 0 ? "(none)" : (cnt == 1 ? "1 system" : (cnt.ToString() + " systems")))
    };
}
```

This removes:

- `CS0426` (it now references the namespace-level DTO),
- `CS0019` (“method group == int”) at line ~127.

i dont know why but the nextreader has the class projectstoreservice

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/NextReaders.cs
// Extends ProjectStoreService with “peek / next” helpers used by UI panes.
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // ---------- READ helpers (used by LineTagSettings etc.) ----------------

        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => s?.Config?.GlobalValvePrefix ?? "V";

        public static int ReadGraceDays(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => Math.Max(0, s?.Config?.GraceDays ?? 0);

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => s?.Config?.PreferReleasedLowestFirst ?? false;

        // ---------- WRITE helpers (mirror of the READs above) ------------------

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string prefix)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s));
            s.Config = s.Config ?? new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V" : prefix.Trim();
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, int days)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s));
            s.Config = s.Config ?? new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GraceDays = Math.Max(0, days);
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, bool value)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s));
            s.Config = s.Config ?? new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = value;
        }

        // ------ Path overloads (loads then delegates) ------
        public static IList<SystemNextInfo> GetSystemNextList(string path)
            => GetSystemNextList(Load(path));

        public static string PeekLineTagString(string path, string systemKey = null)
            => PeekLineTagString(Load(path), systemKey);

        public static string PeekValveTagString(string path, string systemKey = null)
            => PeekValveTagString(Load(path), systemKey);

        // ---------- “Next/Peek” helpers ---------------------------------------

        /// <summary>
        /// Returns a list of systems and their current Next/End counters for display.
        /// </summary>
        public static IList<SystemNextInfo> GetSystemNextList(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            var list = new List<SystemNextInfo>();
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return list;

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
            {
                var c = kv.Value ?? new V1.SystemCounterV1();
                var next = Math.Max(c.Start, c.Next);
                list.Add(new SystemNextInfo
                {
                    System = kv.Key,
                    Prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(c.Prefix) ? "X" : c.Prefix,
                    Next = next,
                    End = c.End
                });
            }
            return list.OrderBy(x => x.System, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList();
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Preview the next Line Tag (uses the system’s own Prefix/Pad and Next).
        /// </summary>
        public static string PeekLineTagString(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey = null)
        {
            var sys = ResolveSystem(s, systemKey);
            if (sys == null) return string.Empty;
            return FormatId(sys);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Preview the next Valve Tag (uses Config.GlobalValvePrefix with system Pad/Next).
        /// </summary>
        public static string PeekValveTagString(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey = null)
        {
            var sys = ResolveSystem(s, systemKey);
            if (sys == null) return string.Empty;
            var prefix = ReadGlobalValvePrefix(s);
            return FormatId(sys, prefixOverride: prefix);
        }

        // ---------- internal helpers ------------------------------------------

        private static V1.SystemCounterV1 ResolveSystem(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string key)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) && s.Systems.TryGetValue(key, out var exact) && exact != null)
                return exact;

            // Fallback to first system deterministically
            var first = s.Systems.FirstOrDefault().Value;
            return first ?? new V1.SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "X", Pad = 4, Start = 1, End = 9999, Next = 1 };
        }

        private static string FormatId(V1.SystemCounterV1 sys, string prefixOverride = null, int? numberOverride = null)
        {
            if (sys == null) return string.Empty;
            var pad = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 1;
            var n = numberOverride ?? Math.Max(sys.Start, sys.Next);
            var pfx = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefixOverride) ? prefixOverride.Trim()
                      : (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? sys.Prefix : "X");
            return $"{pfx}-{n.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0')}";
        }
    }
}

Ahh—there’s the culprit. `NextReaders.cs` is declaring **`public static partial class ProjectStoreService`** and re-adding `GetSystemNextList(string)`, `PeekLineTagString(string, …)`, etc. That’s exactly what’s creating the “ambiguous between … and …” errors.

You have two clean options:

## Option A (fastest): remove the file
Just **Exclude/Delete `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/NextReaders.cs`** from the project.  
You already have those path-based methods in the main `ProjectStoreService.cs`.

## Option B (keep logic, no conflicts): replace the file with a non-conflicting helper
Paste this **drop-in** to completely replace `NextReaders.cs`. It moves everything to a separate class, removes the path overloads (which clash), and only exposes **store-based** helpers you can call when you already have a loaded `ProjectStoreV1`.

```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/NextReaders.cs
// Purpose: Non-conflicting helpers for "peek / next" using an in-memory V1 store.
// Target:  C# 7.3
// Notes :  DO NOT declare/extend ProjectStoreService here. No path-based overloads.
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Helpers that operate on an already-loaded ProjectStoreV1 instance.
    /// Use the path-based methods from ProjectStoreService for everything else.
    /// </summary>
    internal static class ProjectStorePeek
    {
        // ----- READ helpers from an in-memory store -----

        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => (s != null && s.Config != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix))
               ? s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix
               : "V";

        public static int ReadGraceDays(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => (s != null && s.Config != null && s.Config.GraceDays > 0) ? s.Config.GraceDays : 7;

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => (s != null && s.Config != null) ? s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst : true;

        // ----- WRITE helpers on an in-memory store -----

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string prefix)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s));
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V" : prefix.Trim();
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, int days)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s));
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GraceDays = days > 0 ? days : 7;
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, bool value)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s));
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = value;
        }

        // ----- “Next/Peek” helpers on an in-memory store -----

        /// <summary>Returns a list of systems and their current counters for display.</summary>
        public static IList<SystemNextInfo> GetSystemNextListFromStore(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            var list = new List<SystemNextInfo>();
            if (s == null || s.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return list;

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
            {
                var c = kv.Value ?? new V1.SystemCounterV1();
                var next = Math.Max(c.Start, c.Next);
                var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(c.Prefix) ? "V" : c.Prefix.Trim();
                var pad = c.Pad > 0 ? c.Pad : 3;
                list.Add(new SystemNextInfo
                {
                    System = kv.Key,
                    Next = next,
                    NextTag = prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0')
                });
            }
            return list.OrderBy(x => x.System, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList();
        }

        /// <summary>Preview the next Line tag for the system (uses system Prefix/Pad/Next).</summary>
        public static string PeekLineTagStringFromStore(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey = null)
        {
            var sys = ResolveSystem(s, systemKey);
            return FormatId(sys);
        }

        /// <summary>Preview the next Valve tag (uses GlobalValvePrefix + system Pad/Next).</summary>
        public static string PeekValveTagStringFromStore(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey = null)
        {
            var sys = ResolveSystem(s, systemKey);
            var prefix = ReadGlobalValvePrefix(s);
            return FormatId(sys, prefix);
        }

        // ----- internals -----

        private static V1.SystemCounterV1 ResolveSystem(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string key)
        {
            if (s == null || s.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) && s.Systems.TryGetValue(key, out var exact) && exact != null)
                return exact;

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Value; // first
            return null;
        }

        private static string FormatId(V1.SystemCounterV1 sys, string prefixOverride = null)
        {
            if (sys == null) return string.Empty;
            var pad = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 3;
            var n = Math.Max(sys.Start, sys.Next);
            var pfx = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefixOverride)
                        ? prefixOverride.Trim()
                        : (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? sys.Prefix.Trim() : "V");
            return pfx + "-" + n.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');
        }
    }
}
```

### Why this fixes your errors
- It **removes** the second `partial ProjectStoreService` that duplicated path-based APIs→ no more *ambiguous between* errors.
- It leaves your main `ProjectStoreService.cs` as the **single source** for `GetSystemNextList(string)`, `Peek*TagString(string, …)`, etc.

```csharp
// AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectRepairCommands
    {
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: No bound Project Store in this DWG.");
                return;
            }

            try
            {
                var store = ProjectStoreService.Load(binding.StorePath); // V1
                ProjectStoreService.Save(binding.StorePath, store);      // V1
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: Project Store validated/repaired.");
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI Repair failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

This fixes:
- CS0117 (@137) and CS1503 in `ProjectRepairCommands.cs` (it now uses V1 only).

---

```csharp
using PStoreNS = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore; // for SystemNextInfo
```

Replace your `Safe<T>` with this 7.3-safe version:
```csharp
private static T Safe<T>(System.Func<T> read, T fallback)
{
    try
    {
        var v = read();
        if (object.Equals(v, default(T))) return fallback;
        return v;
    }
    catch { return fallback; }
}
```

Now update the two places that read the system list (NO `??`, explicit T):

```csharp

              () => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath),

TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList((System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<PStoreNS.SystemNextInfo>)list);

// 2) In RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()
var list2 = Safe<PStoreNS.SystemNextInfo[]>(
               () => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath),
               new PStoreNS.SystemNextInfo[0]);
TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList((System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<PStoreNS.SystemNextInfo>)list2);
```

# D) TagNumbersDynamic.cs (DTO namespace + “method group == int”)

Top of file:
```csharp
using PStoreNS = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
```

Change the signature of `FromList` and body:

```csharp
public static TagNumbersView FromList(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<PStoreNS.SystemNextInfo> systems)
{
    var safe = (systems ?? System.Linq.Enumerable.Empty<PStoreNS.SystemNextInfo>()).ToList();

    var tagDict = safe
        .Where(s => s != null)
        .ToDictionary(
            s => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s.System) ? s.System : "(unknown)",
            s => (long)s.Next,
            System.StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase
        );

    var lineDict = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, long>(System.StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

    // IMPORTANT: use the property Count, store it in a local int first (avoid method group confusion)
    int cnt = tagDict.Count;

    return new TagNumbersView
    {
        LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(lineDict, "(none)"),
        TagIds  = new DynamicNumberGroup(
                      tagDict,
                      cnt == 0 ? "(none)" : (cnt == 1 ? "1 system" : (cnt.ToString() + " systems"))
                  )
    };
}
```

This fixes:
- CS0426 on `SystemNextInfo`,
- CS0019 “method group == int” at ~127 (caused by using a method group in a ternary—now we hoist `cnt` first).

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File   : ProjectRepairCommands.cs
// Target : C# 7.3
// Desc   : Lightweight repair for the JSON "snapshot" file without touching V1 APIs.
//          Uses reflection to avoid compile-time dependency on channel type.
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Reflection;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectRepairCommands
    {
        // Adjust this if you keep a different global path somewhere else
        private static readonly string StorePath = @"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json";

        [CommandMethod("CBI_RepairProjectStore")]
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStoreClass()
        {
            try
            {
                var svc = new ProjectSettingsService(StorePath);
                var snap = svc.Read();

                bool changed = RepairSnapshotInPlace(snap);

                if (changed) svc.Write(snap);

                Application.ShowAlertDialog(
                    "Project store repair completed."
                    + (changed ? " (changes were written)" : " (no changes necessary)"));
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex) // disambiguate AutoCAD vs System.Exception
            {
                Application.ShowAlertDialog("Repair failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }

        // Minimal, schema-agnostic repair that won't require V1 types or concrete channel types
        private static bool RepairSnapshotInPlace(ProjectStoreSnapshot s)
        {
            bool changed = false;
            if (s == null) return false;

            // Ensure maps exist
            if (s.Projects == null) { s.Projects = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>(); changed = true; }

            // Ensure ActiveProjectKey is sane
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.ActiveProjectKey) && !s.Projects.ContainsKey(s.ActiveProjectKey))
            {
                s.ActiveProjectKey = null; // drop invalid pointer
                changed = true;
            }

            // Normalize each project
            foreach (var kv in s.Projects)
            {
                var p = kv.Value;
                if (p == null) { s.Projects[kv.Key] = new ProjectEntry(); changed = true; p = s.Projects[kv.Key]; }

                if (p.Drawings == null) { p.Drawings = new List<DrawingEntry>(); changed = true; }
                if (p.Tagging == null) { p.Tagging = new TaggingEntry(); changed = true; }
                if (p.Blocks == null) { p.Blocks = new BlocksEntry(); changed = true; }

                // Ensure Tagging.Valve exists and has defaults
                changed |= EnsureChannelWithDefaults(p.Tagging, "Valve", defaultPrefix: "V-");

                // Ensure Tagging.Line exists and has defaults
                changed |= EnsureChannelWithDefaults(p.Tagging, "Line", defaultPrefix: "L-");
            }

            return changed;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Ensures TaggingEntry.{channelName} exists, and applies safe defaults to common properties.
        /// Works even if the channel type name is not known at compile time.
        /// </summary>
        private static bool EnsureChannelWithDefaults(TaggingEntry tagging, string channelName, string defaultPrefix)
        {
            bool changed = false;
            if (tagging == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(channelName)) return false;

            // Get the channel property (e.g., "Valve" or "Line")
            var tagType = tagging.GetType();
            var prop = tagType.GetProperty(channelName, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            if (prop == null || !prop.CanRead || !prop.CanWrite) return false;

            var channel = prop.GetValue(tagging, null);
            if (channel == null)
            {
                // Create instance of the unknown channel type via reflection
                var chanType = prop.PropertyType;
                try
                {
                    channel = Activator.CreateInstance(chanType);
                    prop.SetValue(tagging, channel, null);
                    changed = true;
                }
                catch
                {
                    // If we cannot create, we can't set defaults; bail safely
                    return changed;
                }
            }

            // Apply defaults if the properties exist
            changed |= TrySetStringDefault(channel, "Prefix", defaultPrefix);
            changed |= TrySetIntMin(channel, "Padding", 0);
            changed |= TrySetIntMin(channel, "NextNumber", 1);

            return changed;
        }

        private static bool TrySetStringDefault(object obj, string propName, string defaultValue)
        {
            var pi = obj.GetType().GetProperty(propName, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            if (pi == null || !pi.CanRead || !pi.CanWrite || pi.PropertyType != typeof(string)) return false;

            var cur = (string)pi.GetValue(obj, null);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cur))
            {
                pi.SetValue(obj, defaultValue, null);
                return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        private static bool TrySetIntMin(object obj, string propName, int minValue)
        {
            var pi = obj.GetType().GetProperty(propName, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            if (pi == null || !pi.CanRead || !pi.CanWrite) return false;

            // Accept both int and nullable int
            var t = pi.PropertyType;
            bool isInt = t == typeof(int);
            bool isNullableInt = t.IsGenericType && t.GetGenericTypeDefinition() == typeof(Nullable<>) && t.GetGenericArguments()[0] == typeof(int);
            if (!isInt && !isNullableInt) return false;

            int curVal;
            try
            {
                var curObj = pi.GetValue(obj, null);
                curVal = curObj == null ? int.MinValue : Convert.ToInt32(curObj);
            }
            catch
            {
                curVal = int.MinValue;
            }

            if (curVal < minValue)
            {
                pi.SetValue(obj, minValue, null);
                return true;
            }
            return false;
        }
    }
}

```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE")]
public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
    => AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectRepairCommands.CBI_RepairProjectStore();
```

Since your class already has:
```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_RepairProjectStore")]
public static void CBI_RepairProjectStoreClass() { ... }
```

…you’ll end up with **two** commands bound to the same name. Do one of these:

- **Simplest:** Delete the router in `Commands.cs`. Keep your class-level command.
- Or keep the router and **remove the CommandMethod attribute** from the class method, renaming it to a plain helper (e.g., `Run()`).

Either way, ensure there is **only one** `[CommandMethod("CBI_RepairProjectStore")]` in the solution.

---

# Option B — Minimal *v1-only* repair (recommended for alpha)

✅ Good when you want to enforce the canonical **Project Store v1.0** schema, seed defaults, and validate strictly.

**Drop-in file**: `AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File   : AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs
// Purpose: Minimal v1 repair/validate + save for Project_Store.json.
// Target : C# 7.3
// Notes  : Uses the canonical v1 service (no snapshot/reflection).
//          Make sure only one CommandMethod("CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE") exists.
// ============================================================================
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectRepairCommands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE")]
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: No bound Project Store in this DWG.");
                return;
            }

            try
            {
                // Load → seed defaults → validate → save (all v1)
                var v1 = ProjectStoreService.Load(binding.StorePath);
                ProjectStoreService.Save(binding.StorePath, v1);
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: Project Store validated/repaired.");
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI Repair failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

If you choose Option B, **remove** any other `CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE` declarations (including the snapshot CommandMethod) so there’s only this one.

---

## Which should you pick?

- If your goal is **alpha stability with the new v1 contract**, choose **Option B** (v1-only). It aligns with our current plan and avoids the snapshot/v1 split.
- If you still need to **loosely clean old snapshot files** without enforcing v1, keep **Option A** and remove the router in `Commands.cs`.

Either path will clear the earlier “missing definition / duplicate command” noise. After you pick one, rebuild and we’ll tackle any remaining red lines next.

## Response:
11/12/2025, 11:12:47 AM

You’re right—we fixed a bunch and then a few cross-wires popped up. These all come from two things:

- **Snapshot vs V1 mixups** (some files still expect the old “snapshot” shape with `ActiveProjectKey/Projects`).
- **C# 7.3 constraints** (the `Safe<T>` + `??` array and a variable name collision).

Below are **drop-in patches** to clear the specific errors you just posted. I’ve kept them minimal and C# 7.3-safe.

---

# 1) Bring back the lightweight “snapshot” channel for bindings

These compile-only helpers are what your *binding/valve resolver* code is expecting.

## 1a) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectSettingsService.cs` (NEW or replace)

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectSettingsService.cs
// Purpose: Simple "snapshot" channel for palette/binding UI (no V1 types).
// Target : C# 7.3
// Notes  : Coexists with V1 service. This does NOT do allocations; just config.
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    // ------------------ Snapshot Models ------------------

    public sealed class ProjectStoreSnapshot
    {
        public string ActiveProjectKey { get; set; }
        public Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry> Projects { get; set; }
            = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
    }

    public sealed class ProjectEntry
    {
        public string Name { get; set; }
        public List<DrawingEntry> Drawings { get; set; } = new List<DrawingEntry>();
        public TaggingEntry Tagging { get; set; } = new TaggingEntry();
        public BlocksEntry Blocks { get; set; } = new BlocksEntry();
    }

    public sealed class DrawingEntry
    {
        public string Path { get; set; }
    }

    public sealed class TaggingEntry
    {
        public ChannelEntry Valve { get; set; } = new ChannelEntry { Prefix = "V-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 };
        public ChannelEntry Line  { get; set; } = new ChannelEntry { Prefix = "L-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 };
    }

    public sealed class ChannelEntry
    {
        public string Prefix { get; set; }   // include trailing dash for UI (e.g., "V-")
        public int Padding { get; set; }     // e.g., 3
        public int NextNumber { get; set; }  // e.g., 1
    }

    public sealed class BlocksEntry
    {
        // Keep room to map block defs later; fine empty for now
        // public Dictionary<string,string> ValveTypes { get; set; } = new Dictionary<string,string>();
    }

    // ------------------ Service ------------------

    public sealed class ProjectSettingsService
    {
        private readonly string _path;
        public ProjectSettingsService(string path) { _path = path; }

        public ProjectStoreSnapshot Read()
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_path) || !File.Exists(_path))
                return new ProjectStoreSnapshot();
            var json = File.ReadAllText(_path);
            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(json)
                ? new ProjectStoreSnapshot()
                : (JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<ProjectStoreSnapshot>(json) ?? new ProjectStoreSnapshot());
        }

        public void Write(ProjectStoreSnapshot snap)
        {
            if (snap == null) snap = new ProjectStoreSnapshot();
            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(_path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp = _path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(snap, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);
            if (File.Exists(_path)) File.Replace(tmp, _path, null);
            else File.Move(tmp, _path);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Ensures project exists and the drawing is present in its list.
        /// Returns the key used (projectKey).
        /// </summary>
        public string EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing(string projectKey, string projectName, string drawingPath)
        {
            var snap = Read();

            ProjectEntry p;
            if (!snap.Projects.TryGetValue(projectKey, out p) || p == null)
            {
                p = new ProjectEntry { Name = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(projectName) ? projectKey : projectName };
                snap.Projects[projectKey] = p;
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(snap.ActiveProjectKey))
                    snap.ActiveProjectKey = projectKey;
            }

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(drawingPath))
            {
                var exists = false;
                for (int i = 0; i < p.Drawings.Count; i++)
                    if (string.Equals(p.Drawings[i]?.Path, drawingPath, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                    { exists = true; break; }
                if (!exists) p.Drawings.Add(new DrawingEntry { Path = drawingPath });
            }

            Write(snap);
            return projectKey;
        }
    }
}
```

## 1b) Patch `AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs`

Replace the lines raising:
- cannot convert Snapshot↔V1
- missing `EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing`

with this minimal binding:

```csharp
// inside your command method:
var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
if (doc == null) return;
var dwgPath = doc?.Name;

var svc = new AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");

// choose any sensible key/name for alpha; “Default” keeps it simple:
var keyUsed = svc.EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing("Default", "Default Project", dwgPath);

// (optional) show feedback in editor
doc.Editor.WriteMessage($"\nCBI: Bound drawing to project '{keyUsed}'.");
```

That clears the two errors in `BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs`.

---

# 2) Fix `ProjectRepairCommands.cs` (was still mixing V1/Snapshot)

Your current file is fine conceptually; just make sure it stays **snapshot-only**. If you still see:

> cannot convert V1 ↔ Snapshot

make sure this line is **Snapshot**:

```csharp
private static bool RepairSnapshotInPlace(ProjectStoreSnapshot s)
```

and that you’re **not** calling any V1 helpers in this file. The short version I gave you earlier is not truncated—it’s intentionally small so it stays schema-agnostic.

---

# 3) Add tiny V1 wrappers some files still expect

These clear the `SaveAtomic` / `CreateDefault` missing members.

## 3a) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs` (NEW)

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs
// Purpose: Thin wrappers so old callers compile. Calls through to V1 Save/Load.
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.IO;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        public static void SaveAtomic(string path, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            Save(path, store); // Save already uses atomic temp+replace
        }

        public static void CreateDefault(string path)
        {
            var store = new ProjectStoreV1();
            // Seed one system so validator passes
            if (store.Systems == null)
                store.Systems = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            if (store.Systems.Count == 0)
                store.Systems["Default"] = new SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 9999, Next = 1 };
            Save(path, store);
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 4) LineTagSettings.cs — C# 7.3 fixes + variable name collision

You’re seeing `Safe<T>` inference and `??` with generics + **CS0136** (variable named `first`).

### Change these two spots:

```csharp
// BEFORE (broke on C# 7.3 type inference + null-coalescing with T[])
var list = Safe(() => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath), (PSP.SystemNextInfo[])null);
TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list ?? Array.Empty<PSP.SystemNextInfo>());

// AFTER (C# 7.3 friendly: use IList<T>; call Safe<IList<...>>; no null-coalescing on T)
System.Collections.Generic.IList<PSP.SystemNextInfo> list =
    Safe<System.Collections.Generic.IList<PSP.SystemNextInfo>>(
        () => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath),
        new System.Collections.Generic.List<PSP.SystemNextInfo>());

TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list);
```

Do that **in both places** you call `GetSystemNextList(...)` in the file.

### Fix the CS0136 “first” name clash

Find your method where you have:

```csharp
var first = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.GetFirstSystemName(storePath), (string)null);
```

and rename the local to something else (e.g., `firstName`):

```csharp
var firstName = Safe<string>(() => ProjectStoreService.GetFirstSystemName(storePath), null);
if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(firstName)) return firstName;
```

---

# 5) TagNumbersDynamic.cs — fix the method-group comparison

The error:

> Operator '==' cannot be applied to operands of type 'method group' and 'int'

means a `.Count` property/variable was written as the method `.Count(...)`. Replace the footer where we render the label with this:

```csharp
var count = tagDict.Count;
TagIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(
            tagDict,
            count == 0 ? "(none)" : (count == 1 ? "1 system" : (count.ToString() + " systems"))
         );
```

(And keep `LineIds` as the empty group until you implement a real per-system “line id” view.)

---

# 6) Files still reading “ActiveProjectKey/Projects” must use **Snapshot**, not V1

Errors in:

- `ProjectTagStoreAdapter.cs`
- `ValveDefinitionResolver.cs`

…are because V1 intentionally **does not** have `ActiveProjectKey` or `Projects`. Those belong to the snapshot layer.

### Minimal fix pattern (apply in both files):

```csharp
using SNAP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

var svc = new SNAP.ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");
var snap = svc.Read();

var active = snap.ActiveProjectKey;
if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(active) || snap.Projects == null || !snap.Projects.ContainsKey(active))
{
    // handle unbound
    return;
}

var p = snap.Projects[active];
// p.Tagging.Valve.Prefix etc. are available here
```

If those files also do **allocation/counters**, then keep those parts on **V1** (via `ProjectStoreService`), but read UI-ish things (active project, block maps, channel prefixes with dash, drawing lists) from **Snapshot**.

---

# 7) Clear the two ambiguous `GetSystemNextList` / `Peek*` calls

Ambiguity happens if you have **both** “path” and “store” overloads in scope. Easiest fix in `LineTagSettings.cs` is to call the **path** versions via the PSP alias:

```csharp
using PSP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;

// Get list (path overload)
var systemsList =
    Safe<System.Collections.Generic.IList<PSP.SystemNextInfo>>(
        () => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath),
        new System.Collections.Generic.List<PSP.SystemNextInfo>());

// Preview strings (path overloads)
var line  = Safe<string>(() => PSP.PeekLineTagString(storePath, sys),  "—");
var valve = Safe<string>(() => PSP.PeekValveTagString(storePath, sys), "—");
```

(You’ve already got `using PSP = ...` in your snippet—keep using it.)

---

## Recap of what to drop in

- **NEW** `ProjectSettingsService.cs` (snapshot) — section 1a.
- **PATCH** `BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs` — section 1b.
- **KEEP** your short `ProjectRepairCommands.cs` (snapshot only). Don’t call V1 from there.
- **NEW** `ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs` (adds `SaveAtomic`, `CreateDefault`) — section 3a.
- **PATCH** `LineTagSettings.cs` (Safe<T> + rename `first`) — section 4.
- **PATCH** `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` (fix `.Count` bug) — section 5.
- **PATCH** `ProjectTagStoreAdapter.cs` & `ValveDefinitionResolver.cs` to read **Snapshot** for ActiveProject/Projects — section 6.

Do those, rebuild, and we should be down to just any stragglers (if something else still references old members we can stub).

If anything still squawks after this pass, paste the 2–3 remaining error lines and I’ll hand you the next tiny diffs.

11/12/2025, 12:59:43 PM

Absolutely—here are **drop-in files** for every class we changed in the last pass. They’re all **C# 7.3-safe**, avoid the ambiguous overloads, and keep the Snapshot (UI) layer cleanly separated from the V1 store.

---

## 1) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectSettingsService.cs`  ✅ *NEW (Snapshot channel)*

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectSettingsService.cs
// Purpose: Simple "snapshot" channel for palette/binding UI (no V1 types).
// Target : C# 7.3
// Notes  : Coexists with V1 service. This does NOT do allocations; just config.
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    // ------------------ Snapshot Models ------------------

    public sealed class ProjectStoreSnapshot
    {
        public string ActiveProjectKey { get; set; }
        public Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry> Projects { get; set; }
            = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
    }

    public sealed class ProjectEntry
    {
        public string Name { get; set; }
        public List<DrawingEntry> Drawings { get; set; } = new List<DrawingEntry>();
        public TaggingEntry Tagging { get; set; } = new TaggingEntry();
        public BlocksEntry Blocks { get; set; } = new BlocksEntry();
    }

    public sealed class DrawingEntry
    {
        public string Path { get; set; }
    }

    public sealed class TaggingEntry
    {
        public ChannelEntry Valve { get; set; } = new ChannelEntry { Prefix = "V-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 };
        public ChannelEntry Line  { get; set; } = new ChannelEntry { Prefix = "L-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 };
    }

    public sealed class ChannelEntry
    {
        public string Prefix { get; set; }   // include trailing dash for UI (e.g., "V-")
        public int Padding { get; set; }     // e.g., 3
        public int NextNumber { get; set; }  // e.g., 1
    }

    public sealed class BlocksEntry
    {
        // Reserved for future block maps; safe to keep empty for now.
    }

    // ------------------ Service ------------------

    public sealed class ProjectSettingsService
    {
        private readonly string _path;
        public ProjectSettingsService(string path) { _path = path; }

        public ProjectStoreSnapshot Read()
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_path) || !File.Exists(_path))
                return new ProjectStoreSnapshot();
            var json = File.ReadAllText(_path);
            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(json)
                ? new ProjectStoreSnapshot()
                : (JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<ProjectStoreSnapshot>(json) ?? new ProjectStoreSnapshot());
        }

        public void Write(ProjectStoreSnapshot snap)
        {
            if (snap == null) snap = new ProjectStoreSnapshot();
            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(_path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp = _path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(snap, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);
            if (File.Exists(_path)) File.Replace(tmp, _path, null);
            else File.Move(tmp, _path);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Ensures project exists and the drawing is present in its list.
        /// Returns the key used (projectKey).
        /// </summary>
        public string EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing(string projectKey, string projectName, string drawingPath)
        {
            var snap = Read();

            ProjectEntry p;
            if (!snap.Projects.TryGetValue(projectKey, out p) || p == null)
            {
                p = new ProjectEntry { Name = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(projectName) ? projectKey : projectName };
                snap.Projects[projectKey] = p;
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(snap.ActiveProjectKey))
                    snap.ActiveProjectKey = projectKey;
            }

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(drawingPath))
            {
                var exists = false;
                for (int i = 0; i < p.Drawings.Count; i++)
                    if (string.Equals(p.Drawings[i]?.Path, drawingPath, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                    { exists = true; break; }
                if (!exists) p.Drawings.Add(new DrawingEntry { Path = drawingPath });
            }

            Write(snap);
            return projectKey;
        }
    }
}
```

## 2) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs`  ✅ *NEW*

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs
// Purpose: Thin wrappers so old callers compile. Calls through to V1 Save/Load.
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using System;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        public static void SaveAtomic(string path, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            Save(path, store); // Save already does atomic write via temp+replace
        }

        public static void CreateDefault(string path)
        {
            var store = new ProjectStoreV1();
            if (store.Systems == null)
                store.Systems = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            if (store.Systems.Count == 0)
                store.Systems["Default"] = new SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 9999, Next = 1 };
            Save(path, store);
        }
    }
}
```

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs
// Purpose: Bind the current DWG into the Snapshot project list.
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using SNAP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class BindCurrentDrawingCommand
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG")]
        public static void CBI_BindCurrentDwg()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            var ed = doc.Editor;
            var dwgPath = doc.Name;

            var svc = new SNAP.ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");
            // Use a simple default project key/name for alpha
            var keyUsed = svc.EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing("Default", "Default Project", dwgPath);

            ed.WriteMessage($"\nCBI: Bound drawing to project '{keyUsed}'.");
        }
    }
}
```

## 4) `AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/LineTagSettings.cs`  ✅ *DROP-IN (C# 7.3-safe)*

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/LineTagSettings.cs
// Purpose: Palette settings + preview backed by V1 store & Snapshot.
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.IO;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using PSP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public class LineTagSettings
    {
        // ---------- Appearance ----------
        [Category("Appearance")]
        [Description("Height of annotation text in drawing units.")]
        public double TextHeight { get; set; } = 0.125;

        [Category("Appearance")]
        [Description("Layer to place tag entities on.")]
        public string Layer { get; set; } = "C-ANNO-TAG";

        // ---------- Behavior ----------
        [Category("Behavior")]
        [Description("If true, increments the counter when using the Line Tagging tool.")]
        public bool AutoIncrement { get; set; } = true;

        // ---------- Project Configuration ----------
        [Category("Project Configuration")]
        [DisplayName("Preview System")]
        [Description("System used for previewing the next line/valve numbers.")]
        public string PreviewSystem { get; set; } = "Default";

        [Category("Project Configuration")]
        [DisplayName("Global Valve Prefix")]
        [Description("Default valve-tag prefix stored in Project_Store.json (Config.GlobalValvePrefix).")]
        public string GlobalValvePrefix { get; set; } = "V";

        [Category("Project Configuration")]
        [DisplayName("Grace Days")]
        [Description("Days a released tag remains reserved before eligible for reuse.")]
        public int GraceDays { get; set; } = 7;

        [Category("Project Configuration")]
        [DisplayName("Prefer Released Lowest First")]
        [Description("If true, allocator prefers the lowest available released tag first.")]
        public bool PreferReleasedLowestFirst { get; set; } = true;

        // ---------- Compatibility: mirror used by older UI hooks ----------
        [Browsable(false)]
        public string Prefix { get; set; } = "V";

        // (UI convenience)
        [Browsable(false)]
        public string UiValvePrefix
            => (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(GlobalValvePrefix) ? "V" : GlobalValvePrefix.Trim()) + "-";

        [Browsable(false)]
        public TagNumbersView TagNumbers { get; private set; } = new TagNumbersView();

        // ---------- Tags (expandable preview group) ----------
        [Category("Tags")]
        [DisplayName("Tags")]
        [Description("Preview of next available tag numbers from the Project Store.")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public TagsPreview Tags { get; private set; } = new TagsPreview();

        public sealed class TagsPreview
        {
            [DisplayName("Next Line")]
            [ReadOnly(true)]
            public string NextLine { get; internal set; } = "—";

            [DisplayName("Next Valve")]
            [ReadOnly(true)]
            public string NextValve { get; internal set; } = "—";

            public override string ToString() => "(preview)";
        }

        // ---------- Store sync ----------

        public void RefreshFromStore()
        {
            try
            {
                string storePath;
                if (!TryResolveStorePath(out storePath)) { ApplySafeUnboundDefaults(); return; }

                GlobalValvePrefix = Safe<string>(() => PSP.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(storePath), "V");
                GraceDays = Safe<int>(() => PSP.ReadGraceDays(storePath), 7);
                PreferReleasedLowestFirst = Safe<bool>(() => PSP.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(storePath), true);

                Prefix = GlobalValvePrefix;

                // C# 7.3 friendly: avoid T[] with ??; use IList<T>
                System.Collections.Generic.IList<PSP.SystemNextInfo> list =
                    Safe<System.Collections.Generic.IList<PSP.SystemNextInfo>>(
                        () => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath),
                        new System.Collections.Generic.List<PSP.SystemNextInfo>());

                TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list);

                RefreshTagsPreviewFromStore();
            }
            catch
            {
                ApplySafeUnboundDefaults();
            }
        }

        public void LoadProjectConfig()
        {
            try
            {
                string storePath;
                if (!TryResolveStorePath(out storePath)) { ApplySafeUnboundDefaults(); return; }

                GlobalValvePrefix = Safe<string>(() => PSP.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(storePath), "V");
                GraceDays = Safe<int>(() => PSP.ReadGraceDays(storePath), 7);
                PreferReleasedLowestFirst = Safe<bool>(() => PSP.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(storePath), true);

                Prefix = GlobalValvePrefix;
            }
            catch
            {
                ApplySafeUnboundDefaults();
            }
        }

        public void SaveProjectConfig()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBinding binding;
            if (!AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
                return;

            try
            {
                PSP.WriteGlobalValvePrefix(binding.StorePath, GlobalValvePrefix);
                PSP.WriteGraceDays(binding.StorePath, GraceDays);
                PSP.WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(binding.StorePath, PreferReleasedLowestFirst);

                Prefix = GlobalValvePrefix;
            }
            catch { /* keep UI alive */ }
        }

        public void RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()
        {
            try
            {
                string storePath;
                if (!TryResolveStorePath(out storePath)) { TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView(); return; }

                System.Collections.Generic.IList<PSP.SystemNextInfo> list =
                    Safe<System.Collections.Generic.IList<PSP.SystemNextInfo>>(
                        () => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath),
                        new System.Collections.Generic.List<PSP.SystemNextInfo>());

                TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list);
            }
            catch
            {
                TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView();
            }
        }

        private void RefreshTagsPreviewFromStore()
        {
            try
            {
                string storePath;
                if (!TryResolveStorePath(out storePath)) { Tags = new TagsPreview(); return; }

                var sys = ResolvePreviewSystem(storePath, PreviewSystem);
                var line = Safe<string>(() => PSP.PeekLineTagString(storePath, sys), "—");
                var valve = Safe<string>(() => PSP.PeekValveTagString(storePath, sys), "—");
                Tags = new TagsPreview { NextLine = line, NextValve = valve };
            }
            catch
            {
                Tags = new TagsPreview();
            }
        }

        // ---------- helpers ----------

        private static T Safe<T>(Func<T> read, T fallback)
        {
            try { return read(); } catch { return fallback; }
        }

        private static bool TryResolveStorePath(out string storePath)
        {
            storePath = null;
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return false;

            AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBinding binding;
            if (!AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
                return false;

            storePath = binding.StorePath;
            return true;
        }

        private void ApplySafeUnboundDefaults()
        {
            GlobalValvePrefix = "V";
            Prefix = GlobalValvePrefix;
            TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView();
            Tags = new TagsPreview();
        }

        /// <summary>Returns a valid system name. Prefers requested; else first available; else "Default".</summary>
        private static string ResolvePreviewSystem(string storePath, string requested)
        {
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(requested))
            {
                var ok = Safe<bool>(() => PSP.SystemExists(storePath, requested), false);
                if (ok) return requested;
            }

            var firstName = Safe<string>(() => PSP.GetFirstSystemName(storePath), null);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(firstName)) return firstName;

            return "Default";
        }
    }

    public static class MasterProperties
    {
        private const string FileName = "LineTagSettings.json";
        private static LineTagSettings _lineTag;

        public static LineTagSettings LineTag
            => _lineTag ?? (_lineTag = Load() ?? new LineTagSettings());

        public static void Save()
        {
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(LineTag, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(FileName, json);
        }

        private static LineTagSettings Load()
        {
            if (!File.Exists(FileName)) return null;
            var json = File.ReadAllText(FileName);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<LineTagSettings>(json);
        }
    }
}
```

## 5) `AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/TagNumbersDynamic.cs`  ✅ *DROP-IN*

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/TagNumbersDynamic.cs
// Purpose: Dynamic, expandable groups for PropertyGrid
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Linq;
using PSP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
    public sealed class DynamicNumberGroup : ICustomTypeDescriptor
    {
        private readonly Dictionary<string, long> _values;
        private readonly string _display;

        public DynamicNumberGroup(Dictionary<string, long> values, string display)
        {
            _values = values ?? new Dictionary<string, long>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            _display = display ?? string.Empty;
        }

        public override string ToString() => _display;

        #region ICustomTypeDescriptor
        AttributeCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetAttributes() => AttributeCollection.Empty;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetClassName() => GetType().Name;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetComponentName() => null;
        TypeConverter ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetConverter() => new TypeConverter();
        EventDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultEvent() => null;
        PropertyDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultProperty() => null;
        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEditor(Type editorBaseType) => null;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents(Attribute[] attributes) => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents() => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties(Attribute[] attributes)
            => ((ICustomTypeDescriptor)this).GetProperties();

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties()
        {
            var props = _values.Keys
                .OrderBy(k => k, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                .Select(k => new NumberPropDescriptor(k, _values[k]))
                .ToArray();

            return new PropertyDescriptorCollection(props, true);
        }

        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetPropertyOwner(PropertyDescriptor pd) => this;
        #endregion

        private sealed class NumberPropDescriptor : PropertyDescriptor
        {
            private readonly long _value;
            public NumberPropDescriptor(string name, long value) : base(name, null) { _value = value; }

            public override Type ComponentType => typeof(DynamicNumberGroup);
            public override bool IsReadOnly => true;
            public override Type PropertyType => typeof(long);

            public override bool CanResetValue(object component) => false;
            public override object GetValue(object component) => _value;
            public override void ResetValue(object component) { }
            public override void SetValue(object component, object value) { }
            public override bool ShouldSerializeValue(object component) => false;
        }
    }

    public sealed class TagNumbersView
    {
        [DisplayName("Line ID's")]
        [Description("Next line numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup LineIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        [DisplayName("Tag ID's")]
        [Description("Next tag numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup TagIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        public static TagNumbersView FromList(IEnumerable<PSP.SystemNextInfo> systems)
        {
            var safe = (systems ?? Enumerable.Empty<PSP.SystemNextInfo>()).ToList();

            var tagDict = new Dictionary<string, long>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            foreach (var s in safe)
            {
                if (s == null) continue;
                var name = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s.System) ? s.System : "(unknown)";
                tagDict[name] = s.Next;
            }

            var count = tagDict.Count;

            var view = new TagNumbersView
            {
                LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(new Dictionary<string, long>(), "(none)"),
                TagIds  = new DynamicNumberGroup(
                              tagDict,
                              count == 0 ? "(none)" : (count == 1 ? "1 system" : (count.ToString() + " systems")))
            };

            return view;
        }
    }
}
```

## 6) `AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs`  ✅ *DROP-IN (Snapshot-only)*

```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File   : AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs
// Target : C# 7.3
// Desc   : Lightweight repair for the Snapshot JSON. No V1 dependencies.
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Reflection;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectRepairCommands
    {
        private static readonly string StorePath = @"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json";

        [CommandMethod("CBI_RepairProjectStore")]
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
        {
            try
            {
                var svc = new ProjectSettingsService(StorePath);
                var snap = svc.Read();

                bool changed = RepairSnapshotInPlace(snap);
                if (changed) svc.Write(snap);

                Application.ShowAlertDialog(
                    "Project store repair completed."
                    + (changed ? " (changes were written)" : " (no changes necessary)"));
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                Application.ShowAlertDialog("Repair failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }

        private static bool RepairSnapshotInPlace(ProjectStoreSnapshot s)
        {
            bool changed = false;
            if (s == null) return false;

            if (s.Projects == null) { s.Projects = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>(); changed = true; }

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.ActiveProjectKey) && !s.Projects.ContainsKey(s.ActiveProjectKey))
            { s.ActiveProjectKey = null; changed = true; }

            foreach (var kv in s.Projects)
            {
                var p = kv.Value;
                if (p == null) { s.Projects[kv.Key] = new ProjectEntry(); changed = true; p = s.Projects[kv.Key]; }

                if (p.Drawings == null) { p.Drawings = new List<DrawingEntry>(); changed = true; }
                if (p.Tagging == null) { p.Tagging = new TaggingEntry(); changed = true; }
                if (p.Blocks == null)   { p.Blocks = new BlocksEntry();   changed = true; }

                changed |= EnsureChannelWithDefaults(p.Tagging, "Valve", "V-");
                changed |= EnsureChannelWithDefaults(p.Tagging, "Line" , "L-");
            }
            return changed;
        }

        private static bool EnsureChannelWithDefaults(TaggingEntry tagging, string channelName, string defaultPrefix)
        {
            bool changed = false;
            if (tagging == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(channelName)) return false;

            var tagType = tagging.GetType();
            var prop = tagType.GetProperty(channelName, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            if (prop == null || !prop.CanRead || !prop.CanWrite) return false;

            var channel = prop.GetValue(tagging, null);
            if (channel == null)
            {
                var chanType = prop.PropertyType;
                try
                {
                    channel = Activator.CreateInstance(chanType);
                    prop.SetValue(tagging, channel, null);
                    changed = true;
                }
                catch { return changed; }
            }

            changed |= TrySetStringDefault(channel, "Prefix", defaultPrefix);
            changed |= TrySetIntMin(channel, "Padding", 0);
            changed |= TrySetIntMin(channel, "NextNumber", 1);

            return changed;
        }

        private static bool TrySetStringDefault(object obj, string propName, string defaultValue)
        {
            var pi = obj.GetType().GetProperty(propName, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            if (pi == null || !pi.CanRead || !pi.CanWrite || pi.PropertyType != typeof(string)) return false;

            var cur = (string)pi.GetValue(obj, null);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cur))
            {
                pi.SetValue(obj, defaultValue, null);
                return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        private static bool TrySetIntMin(object obj, string propName, int minValue)
        {
            var pi = obj.GetType().GetProperty(propName, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            if (pi == null || !pi.CanRead || !pi.CanWrite) return false;

            var t = pi.PropertyType;
            bool isInt = t == typeof(int);
            bool isNullableInt = t.IsGenericType && t.GetGenericTypeDefinition() == typeof(Nullable<>) && t.GetGenericArguments()[0] == typeof(int);
            if (!isInt && !isNullableInt) return false;

            int curVal;
            try { var curObj = pi.GetValue(obj, null); curVal = curObj == null ? int.MinValue : Convert.ToInt32(curObj); }
            catch { curVal = int.MinValue; }

            if (curVal < minValue) { pi.SetValue(obj, minValue, null); return true; }
            return false;
        }
    }
}
```

### 7a) `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/ProjectTagStoreAdapter.cs`  ✅ *DROP-IN*

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/ProjectTagStoreAdapter.cs
// Purpose: Read-only adapter to Snapshot for active project/channel prefixes.
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using SNAP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public sealed class ProjectTagStoreAdapter
    {
        private readonly SNAP.ProjectSettingsService _svc;
        private readonly SNAP.ProjectStoreSnapshot _snap;

        public ProjectTagStoreAdapter(string storePath)
        {
            _svc = new SNAP.ProjectSettingsService(storePath);
            _snap = _svc.Read();
        }

        public string ActiveProjectKey
            => _snap != null ? _snap.ActiveProjectKey : null;

        public SNAP.ProjectEntry ActiveProject
        {
            get
            {
                if (_snap == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_snap.ActiveProjectKey) || _snap.Projects == null)
                    return null;
                SNAP.ProjectEntry p;
                return _snap.Projects.TryGetValue(_snap.ActiveProjectKey, out p) ? p : null;
            }
        }

        public string ValvePrefixUi
        {
            get
            {
                var p = ActiveProject;
                var prefix = p?.Tagging?.Valve?.Prefix;
                return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V-" : prefix;
            }
        }

        public string LinePrefixUi
        {
            get
            {
                var p = ActiveProject;
                var prefix = p?.Tagging?.Line?.Prefix;
                return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "L-" : prefix;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### 7b) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/Valves/ValveDefinitionResolver.cs`  ✅ *DROP-IN (Snapshot-based read)*

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/Valves/ValveDefinitionResolver.cs
// Purpose: Resolve UI valve prefix from Snapshot (no V1 dependencies).
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using SNAP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Valves
{
    public static class ValveDefinitionResolver
    {
        public static string GetActiveValvePrefixUi(string storePath)
        {
            var svc = new SNAP.ProjectSettingsService(storePath);
            var snap = svc.Read();
            if (snap == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(snap.ActiveProjectKey) || snap.Projects == null)
                return "V-";

            SNAP.ProjectEntry p;
            if (!snap.Projects.TryGetValue(snap.ActiveProjectKey, out p) || p == null)
                return "V-";

            var prefix = p.Tagging != null && p.Tagging.Valve != null ? p.Tagging.Valve.Prefix : null;
            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V-" : prefix;
        }
    }
}
```
