## Response:
11/12/2025, 1:11:37 PM

Nice catch—those “Ambiguity between … and …” errors tell us two things:

1) Your **Snapshot models** (`ProjectStoreSnapshot`, `ProjectEntry`, etc.) exist **twice in the same namespace**, and  
2) `ProjectStoreService` doesn’t expose a single, consistent set of helpers (`ReadGlobalValvePrefix`, `SystemNextInfo`, etc.) across its partial files.

Below are **drop-in, C# 7.3-safe** files that resolve all of that by:

- Moving the **Snapshot channel** into its own namespace: `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Snapshot`
- Making all callers of snapshot models point to that namespace.
- Centralizing **V1 helpers** in `ProjectStoreService.Core.cs` and the **compat shims** (`CreateDefault`, `SaveAtomic`) in `ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs`.
- Defining a single `SystemNextInfo` model in `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs`.
- Fixing `NextReaders.cs` so it references the shared `SystemNextInfo` and adds missing **path overloads** (`Read*/Write*`) used by `LineTagSettings`.
- Updating the few consuming classes to the correct namespaces/aliases.

> Replace the contents of the files below exactly as shown. If you still have an older copy of the snapshot models under `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore` (same namespace), **delete/rename** that file or replace it with the version below so there’s only one definition (now under `.Snapshot`).

---

# 1) V1 Core (central helpers)

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.Core.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.Core.cs
// Purpose: V1 load/save + core helpers used by UI wrappers.
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // ---- IO primitives ---------------------------------------------------

        public static T ReadJson<T>(string path)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path) || !File.Exists(path)) return default(T);
            var json = File.ReadAllText(path);
            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(json) ? default(T) : JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<T>(json);
        }

        public static void AtomicWrite<T>(string path, T obj)
        {
            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp = path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(obj ?? Activator.CreateInstance<T>(), Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);
            if (File.Exists(path)) File.Replace(tmp, path, null);
            else File.Move(tmp, path);
        }

        // ---- V1 root ---------------------------------------------------------

        public static V1.ProjectStoreV1 Load(string path)
        {
            var s = ReadJson<V1.ProjectStoreV1>(path);
            return s ?? new V1.ProjectStoreV1();
        }

        public static void Save(string path, V1.ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            if (store == null) store = new V1.ProjectStoreV1();
            AtomicWrite(path, store);
        }

        // ---- V1 querying helpers --------------------------------------------

        public static string GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            if (s == null || s.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;
            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Key; // deterministic enough for UI
            return null;
        }

        public static string GetFirstSystemName(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s);
        }

        public static V1.SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey)
        {
            if (s == null) return null;
            if (s.Systems != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(systemKey))
            {
                V1.SystemCounterV1 exact;
                if (s.Systems.TryGetValue(systemKey, out exact) && exact != null) return exact;
            }
            if (s.Systems == null) return null;
            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Value;
            return null;
        }

        public static V1.SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrNull(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey)
        {
            if (s == null || s.Systems == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(systemKey)) return null;
            V1.SystemCounterV1 val;
            return s.Systems.TryGetValue(systemKey, out val) ? val : null;
        }

        public static bool EnsureSystemExists(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey)
        {
            if (s == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(systemKey)) return false;
            if (s.Systems == null) s.Systems = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, V1.SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            if (!s.Systems.ContainsKey(systemKey))
            {
                s.Systems[systemKey] = new V1.SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "X", Pad = 4, Start = 1, End = 9999, Next = 1 };
                return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        public static bool SystemExists(string path, string systemKey)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s == null || s.Systems == null) return false;
            return s.Systems.ContainsKey(systemKey);
        }

        // ---- Config (path overloads) -----------------------------------------

        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s != null && s.Config != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix))
                ? s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix.Trim()
                : "V";
        }

        public static int ReadGraceDays(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return Math.Max(0, s != null && s.Config != null ? s.Config.GraceDays : 0);
        }

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return s != null && s.Config != null && s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst;
        }

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string path, string prefix)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V" : prefix.Trim();
            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(string path, int days)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GraceDays = Math.Max(0, days);
            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path, bool value)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = value;
            Save(path, s);
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 2) V1 Models for “next” rows

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.Models.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.Models.cs
// Purpose: Shared model for UI "SystemNext" rows.
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        public sealed class SystemNextInfo
        {
            public string System { get; set; }
            public string Prefix { get; set; }
            public int Next { get; set; }
            public int End { get; set; }
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 3) V1 Legacy shims

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs
// Purpose: Back-compat helpers used by older callers.
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using System;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        public static void SaveAtomic(string path, V1.ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            Save(path, store);
        }

        public static void CreateDefault(string path)
        {
            var store = new V1.ProjectStoreV1();
            if (store.Systems == null)
                store.Systems = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, V1.SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            if (store.Systems.Count == 0)
                store.Systems["Default"] = new V1.SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 9999, Next = 1 };

            Save(path, store);
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 4) “Peek / Next” readers (and path overloads)

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/NextReaders.cs`

```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/NextReaders.cs
// Extends ProjectStoreService with “peek / next” helpers used by UI panes.
// Target : C# 7.3
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // ---------- READ CONFIG (V1 object overloads) -------------------------

        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => s != null && s.Config != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix)
                    ? s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix.Trim() : "V";

        public static int ReadGraceDays(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => Math.Max(0, s != null && s.Config != null ? s.Config.GraceDays : 0);

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => s != null && s.Config != null && s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst;

        // ---------- WRITE CONFIG (V1 object overloads) ------------------------

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string prefix)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s));
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V" : prefix.Trim();
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, int days)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s));
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GraceDays = Math.Max(0, days);
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, bool value)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s));
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = value;
        }

        // ---------- PATH overloads for the above (used by LineTagSettings) ----

        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(string path) => ReadGlobalValvePrefix(Load(path));
        public static int    ReadGraceDays(string path)        => ReadGraceDays(Load(path));
        public static bool   ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path) => ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(Load(path));

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string path, string prefix)
        { var s = Load(path); WriteGlobalValvePrefix(s, prefix); Save(path, s); }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(string path, int days)
        { var s = Load(path); WriteGraceDays(s, days); Save(path, s); }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path, bool value)
        { var s = Load(path); WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(s, value); Save(path, s); }

        // ---------- “Next” list for UI ---------------------------------------

        public static IList<SystemNextInfo> GetSystemNextList(string path)
            => GetSystemNextList(Load(path));

        public static IList<SystemNextInfo> GetSystemNextList(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            var list = new List<SystemNextInfo>();
            if (s == null || s.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return list;

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
            {
                var c = kv.Value ?? new V1.SystemCounterV1();
                var next = Math.Max(c.Start, c.Next);
                list.Add(new SystemNextInfo
                {
                    System = kv.Key,
                    Prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(c.Prefix) ? "X" : c.Prefix,
                    Next   = next,
                    End    = c.End
                });
            }

            // Note: provide explicit type args so C# 7.3 can infer correctly
            return list
                .OrderBy<SystemNextInfo, string>(x => x.System, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                .ToList();
        }

        // ---------- “Peek next” strings --------------------------------------

        public static string PeekLineTagString(string path, string systemKey = null)
            => PeekLineTagString(Load(path), systemKey);

        public static string PeekValveTagString(string path, string systemKey = null)
            => PeekValveTagString(Load(path), systemKey);

        public static string PeekLineTagString(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey = null)
        {
            var sys = ResolveSystem(s, systemKey);
            if (sys == null) return string.Empty;
            return FormatId(sys);
        }

        public static string PeekValveTagString(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey = null)
        {
            var sys = ResolveSystem(s, systemKey);
            if (sys == null) return string.Empty;
            var prefix = ReadGlobalValvePrefix(s);
            return FormatId(sys, prefixOverride: prefix);
        }

        // ---------- Internal helpers -----------------------------------------

        private static V1.SystemCounterV1 ResolveSystem(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string key)
        {
            if (s == null || s.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key))
            {
                V1.SystemCounterV1 exact;
                if (s.Systems.TryGetValue(key, out exact) && exact != null) return exact;
            }

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Value; // first
            return new V1.SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "X", Pad = 4, Start = 1, End = 9999, Next = 1 };
        }

        private static string FormatId(V1.SystemCounterV1 sys, string prefixOverride = null, int? numberOverride = null)
        {
            if (sys == null) return string.Empty;
            var pad = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 1;
            var n = numberOverride ?? Math.Max(sys.Start, sys.Next);
            var pfx = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefixOverride) ? prefixOverride.Trim()
                      : (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? sys.Prefix : "X");
            return pfx + "-" + n.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');
        }
    }
}
```

# 5) Snapshot channel (moved to its own namespace)

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/Snapshot/ProjectSettingsService.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/Snapshot/ProjectSettingsService.cs
// Purpose: Snapshot models + service (separate namespace to avoid clashes).
// Target : C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Snapshot
{
    public sealed class ProjectStoreSnapshot
    {
        public string ActiveProjectKey { get; set; }
        public Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry> Projects { get; set; }
            = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
    }

    public sealed class ProjectEntry
    {
        public string Name { get; set; }
        public List<DrawingEntry> Drawings { get; set; } = new List<DrawingEntry>();
        public TaggingEntry Tagging { get; set; } = new TaggingEntry();
        public BlocksEntry Blocks { get; set; } = new BlocksEntry();
    }

    public sealed class DrawingEntry
    {
        public string Path { get; set; }
    }

    public sealed class TaggingEntry
    {
        public ChannelEntry Valve { get; set; } = new ChannelEntry { Prefix = "V-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 };
        public ChannelEntry Line  { get; set; } = new ChannelEntry { Prefix = "L-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 };
    }

    public sealed class ChannelEntry
    {
        public string Prefix { get; set; }
        public int Padding { get; set; }
        public int NextNumber { get; set; }
    }

    public sealed class BlocksEntry { }

    public sealed class ProjectSettingsService
    {
        private readonly string _path;
        public ProjectSettingsService(string path) { _path = path; }

        public ProjectStoreSnapshot Read()
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_path) || !File.Exists(_path))
                return new ProjectStoreSnapshot();
            var json = File.ReadAllText(_path);
            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(json)
                ? new ProjectStoreSnapshot()
                : (JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<ProjectStoreSnapshot>(json) ?? new ProjectStoreSnapshot());
        }

        public void Write(ProjectStoreSnapshot snap)
        {
            if (snap == null) snap = new ProjectStoreSnapshot();
            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(_path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp = _path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(snap, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);
            if (File.Exists(_path)) File.Replace(tmp, _path, null);
            else File.Move(tmp, _path);
        }

        public string EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing(string projectKey, string projectName, string drawingPath)
        {
            var snap = Read();

            ProjectEntry p;
            if (!snap.Projects.TryGetValue(projectKey, out p) || p == null)
            {
                p = new ProjectEntry { Name = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(projectName) ? projectKey : projectName };
                snap.Projects[projectKey] = p;
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(snap.ActiveProjectKey))
                    snap.ActiveProjectKey = projectKey;
            }

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(drawingPath))
            {
                var exists = false;
                for (int i = 0; i < p.Drawings.Count; i++)
                {
                    var d = p.Drawings[i];
                    if (d != null && string.Equals(d.Path, drawingPath, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                    { exists = true; break; }
                }
                if (!exists) p.Drawings.Add(new DrawingEntry { Path = drawingPath });
            }

            Write(snap);
            return projectKey;
        }
    }
}
```

using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using SNAP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Snapshot;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class BindCurrentDrawingCommand
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG")]
        public static void CBI_BindCurrentDwg()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            var ed = doc.Editor;
            var dwgPath = doc.Name;

            var svc = new SNAP.ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");
            var key = svc.EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing("Default", "Default Project", dwgPath);

            ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: Bound drawing to project '" + key + "'.");
        }
    }
}

## 6b) Snapshot repair command

**File:** `AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs`

```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File   : AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs
// Target : C# 7.3
// Desc   : Snapshot repair only (no V1 dependency)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using SNAP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Snapshot;
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Reflection;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectRepairCommands
    {
        private static readonly string StorePath = @"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json";

        [CommandMethod("CBI_RepairProjectStore")]
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
        {
            try
            {
                var svc = new SNAP.ProjectSettingsService(StorePath);
                var snap = svc.Read();

                bool changed = RepairSnapshotInPlace(snap);
                if (changed) svc.Write(snap);

                Application.ShowAlertDialog("Project store repair completed." + (changed ? " (changes were written)" : " (no changes necessary)"));
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                Application.ShowAlertDialog("Repair failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }

        private static bool RepairSnapshotInPlace(SNAP.ProjectStoreSnapshot s)
        {
            bool changed = false;
            if (s == null) return false;

            if (s.Projects == null) { s.Projects = new Dictionary<string, SNAP.ProjectEntry>(); changed = true; }

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.ActiveProjectKey) && !s.Projects.ContainsKey(s.ActiveProjectKey))
            { s.ActiveProjectKey = null; changed = true; }

            foreach (var kv in s.Projects)
            {
                var p = kv.Value;
                if (p == null) { s.Projects[kv.Key] = new SNAP.ProjectEntry(); changed = true; p = s.Projects[kv.Key]; }

                if (p.Drawings == null) { p.Drawings = new List<SNAP.DrawingEntry>(); changed = true; }
                if (p.Tagging == null)  { p.Tagging  = new SNAP.TaggingEntry();       changed = true; }
                if (p.Blocks == null)   { p.Blocks   = new SNAP.BlocksEntry();        changed = true; }

                changed |= EnsureChannelWithDefaults(p.Tagging, "Valve", "V-");
                changed |= EnsureChannelWithDefaults(p.Tagging, "Line",  "L-");
            }
            return changed;
        }

        private static bool EnsureChannelWithDefaults(SNAP.TaggingEntry tagging, string channelName, string defaultPrefix)
        {
            bool changed = false;
            if (tagging == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(channelName)) return false;

            var prop = tagging.GetType().GetProperty(channelName, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            if (prop == null || !prop.CanRead || !prop.CanWrite) return false;

            var channel = prop.GetValue(tagging, null);
            if (channel == null)
            {
                try { channel = Activator.CreateInstance(prop.PropertyType); prop.SetValue(tagging, channel, null); changed = true; }
                catch { return changed; }
            }

            changed |= TrySetStringDefault(channel, "Prefix", defaultPrefix);
            changed |= TrySetIntMin(channel, "Padding", 0);
            changed |= TrySetIntMin(channel, "NextNumber", 1);
            return changed;
        }

        private static bool TrySetStringDefault(object obj, string propName, string defaultValue)
        {
            var pi = obj.GetType().GetProperty(propName, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            if (pi == null || !pi.CanRead || !pi.CanWrite || pi.PropertyType != typeof(string)) return false;

            var cur = (string)pi.GetValue(obj, null);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cur)) { pi.SetValue(obj, defaultValue, null); return true; }
            return false;
        }

        private static bool TrySetIntMin(object obj, string propName, int minValue)
        {
            var pi = obj.GetType().GetProperty(propName, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            if (pi == null || !pi.CanRead || !pi.CanWrite) return false;

            var t = pi.PropertyType;
            bool isInt = t == typeof(int);
            bool isNullableInt = t.IsGenericType && t.GetGenericTypeDefinition() == typeof(Nullable<>) && t.GetGenericArguments()[0] == typeof(int);
            if (!isInt && !isNullableInt) return false;

            int curVal;
            try { var curObj = pi.GetValue(obj, null); curVal = curObj == null ? int.MinValue : Convert.ToInt32(curObj); }
            catch { curVal = int.MinValue; }

            if (curVal < minValue) { pi.SetValue(obj, minValue, null); return true; }
            return false;
        }
    }
}
```

## 6c) Snapshot adapter for Tagging services

**File:** `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/ProjectTagStoreAdapter.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/ProjectTagStoreAdapter.cs
// Target: C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using SNAP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Snapshot;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public sealed class ProjectTagStoreAdapter
    {
        private readonly SNAP.ProjectSettingsService _svc;
        private readonly SNAP.ProjectStoreSnapshot _snap;

        public ProjectTagStoreAdapter(string storePath)
        {
            _svc = new SNAP.ProjectSettingsService(storePath);
            _snap = _svc.Read();
        }

        public string ActiveProjectKey => _snap != null ? _snap.ActiveProjectKey : null;

        public SNAP.ProjectEntry ActiveProject
        {
            get
            {
                if (_snap == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_snap.ActiveProjectKey) || _snap.Projects == null)
                    return null;
                SNAP.ProjectEntry p;
                return _snap.Projects.TryGetValue(_snap.ActiveProjectKey, out p) ? p : null;
            }
        }

        public string ValvePrefixUi
        {
            get
            {
                var p = ActiveProject;
                var prefix = p != null && p.Tagging != null && p.Tagging.Valve != null ? p.Tagging.Valve.Prefix : null;
                return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V-" : prefix;
            }
        }

        public string LinePrefixUi
        {
            get
            {
                var p = ActiveProject;
                var prefix = p != null && p.Tagging != null && p.Tagging.Line != null ? p.Tagging.Line.Prefix : null;
                return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "L-" : prefix;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## 6d) Valve prefix resolver (Snapshot)

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/Valves/ValveDefinitionResolver.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/Valves/ValveDefinitionResolver.cs
// Target: C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using SNAP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Snapshot;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Valves
{
    public static class ValveDefinitionResolver
    {
        public static string GetActiveValvePrefixUi(string storePath)
        {
            var svc  = new SNAP.ProjectSettingsService(storePath);
            var snap = svc.Read();
            if (snap == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(snap.ActiveProjectKey) || snap.Projects == null)
                return "V-";

            SNAP.ProjectEntry p;
            if (!snap.Projects.TryGetValue(snap.ActiveProjectKey, out p) || p == null)
                return "V-";

            var prefix = p.Tagging != null && p.Tagging.Valve != null ? p.Tagging.Valve.Prefix : null;
            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V-" : prefix;
        }
    }
}
```

# 7) PropertyGrid dynamic numbers (just ensure PSP resolves)

**File:** `AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/TagNumbersDynamic.cs`

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/TagNumbersDynamic.cs
// Target: C# 7.3
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Linq;
using PSP = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
    public sealed class DynamicNumberGroup : ICustomTypeDescriptor
    {
        private readonly Dictionary<string, long> _values;
        private readonly string _display;

        public DynamicNumberGroup(Dictionary<string, long> values, string display)
        {
            _values = values ?? new Dictionary<string, long>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            _display = display ?? string.Empty;
        }

        public override string ToString() => _display;

        #region ICustomTypeDescriptor
        AttributeCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetAttributes() => AttributeCollection.Empty;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetClassName() => GetType().Name;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetComponentName() => null;
        TypeConverter ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetConverter() => new TypeConverter();
        EventDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultEvent() => null;
        PropertyDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultProperty() => null;
        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEditor(Type editorBaseType) => null;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents(Attribute[] attributes) => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents() => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties(Attribute[] attributes)
            => ((ICustomTypeDescriptor)this).GetProperties();

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties()
        {
            var props = _values.Keys
                .OrderBy(k => k, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                .Select(k => new NumberPropDescriptor(k, _values[k]))
                .ToArray();

            return new PropertyDescriptorCollection(props, true);
        }

        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetPropertyOwner(PropertyDescriptor pd) => this;
        #endregion

        private sealed class NumberPropDescriptor : PropertyDescriptor
        {
            private readonly long _value;
            public NumberPropDescriptor(string name, long value) : base(name, null) { _value = value; }

            public override Type ComponentType => typeof(DynamicNumberGroup);
            public override bool IsReadOnly => true;
            public override Type PropertyType => typeof(long);

            public override bool CanResetValue(object component) => false;
            public override object GetValue(object component) => _value;
            public override void ResetValue(object component) { }
            public override void SetValue(object component, object value) { }
            public override bool ShouldSerializeValue(object component) => false;
        }
    }

    public sealed class TagNumbersView
    {
        [DisplayName("Line ID's")]
        [Description("Next line numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup LineIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        [DisplayName("Tag ID's")]
        [Description("Next tag numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup TagIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        public static TagNumbersView FromList(IEnumerable<PSP.SystemNextInfo> systems)
        {
            var safe = (systems ?? Enumerable.Empty<PSP.SystemNextInfo>()).ToList();

            var tagDict = new Dictionary<string, long>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            foreach (var s in safe)
            {
                if (s == null) continue;
                var name = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s.System) ? s.System : "(unknown)";
                tagDict[name] = s.Next;
            }

            var count = tagDict.Count;

            return new TagNumbersView
            {
                LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(new Dictionary<string, long>(), "(none)"),
                TagIds  = new DynamicNumberGroup(
                              tagDict,
                              count == 0 ? "(none)" : (count == 1 ? "1 system" : (count.ToString() + " systems")))
            };
        }
    }
}
```

## Final quick checklist

- ✅ **Delete/replace** any older file that defined `ProjectStoreSnapshot`, `ProjectEntry`, etc. **under** the `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore` namespace. The new snapshot models live in `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Snapshot`.
- ✅ Ensure **all four** partials for `ProjectStoreService` share the exact namespace `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore`.
- ✅ Make sure the **LegacyCompat** file is included in the project so `CreateDefault` and `SaveAtomic` resolve.
- ✅ Rebuild.

If anything still errors after these drops, paste the remaining 3–5 messages and I’ll hand you tiny patches.

## Prompt:
11/12/2025, 1:31:25 PM

What is this
AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.Models.cs 

is it a new file models.cs or is referencing the models function in ProjectStoreService?

## Response:
11/12/2025, 1:31:28 PM

Great question—sorry for the naming whiplash!

It’s a **new file** you add to the shared project:

**Path:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.Models.cs`  
**Purpose:** define the small UI helper model `SystemNextInfo` **inside** the same `ProjectStoreService` partial class.

So it’s **not** referencing some external “models” class; it simply contributes one tiny type to the existing partial:

- Namespace: `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore`
- Class: `public static partial class ProjectStoreService`
- Type inside it: `public sealed class SystemNextInfo { ... }`

That way, all partials of `ProjectStoreService` (Core, LegacyCompat, NextReaders) can use `ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo` without ambiguity, and files like `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` can reference it as `PSP.SystemNextInfo`.

Quick checklist:
- ✅ Add the file exactly once at that path.
- ✅ Keep the same namespace as the other `ProjectStoreService` partials.
- ✅ Do **not** confuse it with the **Snapshot models** (`ProjectStoreSnapshot`, `ProjectEntry`, etc.), which live under the separate namespace `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.Snapshot`.

If you want, I can paste the exact minimal contents again, but it’s the 15-line class defining `System`, `Prefix`, `Next`, and `End` inside the partial `ProjectStoreService`.

## Prompt:
11/12/2025, 1:34:40 PM

Ok so this explains a few things I was over writing the entire code in ProjectStoreService not knowing i needed a new file called ProjectStoreService.Models.cs the dot confuses me because I have seen formatting describe a function in a class representing in that fashion

## Response:
11/12/2025, 1:34:40 PM

Exactly 👏 — you’ve nailed it.  
That dot in `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs` **isn’t** referring to a *method or property*; it’s just a **naming convention** we use in C# projects to show that a file *extends* or *belongs to* a particular class or namespace — especially when we’re splitting a large class into multiple `partial` files.

Here’s the distinction clearly:

| **What it looks like** | **Meaning** | **Where it lives** |
|--------------------------|-------------|--------------------|
| `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs` | ✅ A *separate file name* that adds members to the `ProjectStoreService` partial class | Physical file in your project folder |
| `ProjectStoreService.Models` (no `.cs`) | ❌ Would mean “a property or nested class named `Models` inside ProjectStoreService`” if seen *in code* | Logical member name inside code |

So, in Visual Studio:
```
AutoCAD_Shared_Project/
 └── ProjectStore/
      ├── ProjectStoreService.cs
      ├── ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs
      ├── ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs
      ├── ProjectStoreService.Models.cs   ← new file
```

All four share:
```csharp
namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // each file contributes methods or nested types here
    }
}
```

That’s why the dot naming helps *humans* see the relationship but doesn’t imply anything special to the compiler — it’s purely organizational.

Would you like me to give you a clean version of the `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs` file next (the `SystemNextInfo` class definition) so you can drop it in?

## Response:
11/12/2025, 1:36:55 PM

Perfect — thank you for catching that, Brian. 👍  
You’re exactly right: the “dot-named” files (like `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs`, `ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs`, etc.) are **partials** that *extend* the same parent class, not replacements for it.  

So let’s cleanly reset you with a **full, correct parent base file** for the main class:

---

### 🧩 File:  
`AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs`

### ✅ Purpose:
This is the *core parent partial* — it holds the class definition, main helpers (`Load`, `SaveAtomic`, `CreateDefault`), and any core constants.  
All the other files (`.LegacyCompat.cs`, `.Models.cs`, `.NextReaders.cs`) simply plug into this via `partial class ProjectStoreService`.

---

### ✅ Drop-in Code:
```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs
// Desc: Core Project Store static service - handles load, save, and initialization.
//       Other partials extend this class with helpers (Models, Readers, LegacyCompat).
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Text.Json;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        private static readonly JsonSerializerOptions JsonOptions = new JsonSerializerOptions
        {
            WriteIndented = true,
            PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
            AllowTrailingCommas = true
        };

        /// <summary>
        /// Loads a ProjectStoreSnapshot from disk.
        /// </summary>
        public static ProjectStoreSnapshot Load(string path)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path))
                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(path));

            if (!File.Exists(path))
                throw new FileNotFoundException("Project Store not found", path);

            string json = File.ReadAllText(path);
            return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ProjectStoreSnapshot>(json, JsonOptions)
                   ?? new ProjectStoreSnapshot();
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Saves a ProjectStoreSnapshot atomically.
        /// </summary>
        public static void SaveAtomic(string path, ProjectStoreSnapshot snapshot)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path))
                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(path));

            if (snapshot == null)
                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(snapshot));

            string tmpPath = path + ".tmp";
            string json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(snapshot, JsonOptions);
            File.WriteAllText(tmpPath, json);
            File.Copy(tmpPath, path, overwrite: true);
            File.Delete(tmpPath);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Creates a minimal default ProjectStoreSnapshot structure.
        /// </summary>
        public static ProjectStoreSnapshot CreateDefault()
        {
            return new ProjectStoreSnapshot
            {
                Version = "1.0",
                ActiveProjectKey = null,
                Projects = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>()
            };
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Returns true if the file exists and is readable as a ProjectStoreSnapshot.
        /// </summary>
        public static bool ExistsAndValid(string path)
        {
            try
            {
                var s = Load(path);
                return s != null && s.Projects != null;
            }
            catch
            {
                return false;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

      ├── ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs     ← V1 bridges (read/write)
      ├── ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs      ← Peek/Next helpers

## Response:
11/12/2025, 1:43:18 PM

Short answer: yeah—this got tangled because the main file was overwritten when I meant to add **partials**. The error pattern you pasted is exactly what I’d expect when a parent file gets replaced and then new partials are added on top.

Here’s why your errors scream “duplication/overwrite”:

- **CS0229 Ambiguity** on things like `ProjectStoreSnapshot.ActiveProjectKey`, `Projects`, `Tagging`, etc. → there are **two definitions** of the same members in the same namespace (typically happens when the original class still exists somewhere *and* you added another copy of the same class/members in a different file).  
- **CS0101 The namespace … already contains a definition for ‘X’** → duplicate class/type files (e.g., you now have two `ProjectStoreSnapshot`, two `ProjectEntry`, etc.).  
- **CS0111 Type ‘…’ already defines a member ‘…’** → same method implemented more than once because we split/duplicated the base vs partials.  
- **CS0117 ‘ProjectStoreService’ does not contain …** + **CS0103 name ‘Save’ does not exist** → the “base” that originally provided `Load/Save/SaveAtomic/CreateDefault` got overwritten, so the partials that reference those methods can’t find them.  
- **CS0426 The type name ‘SystemNextInfo’ does not exist in the type ‘ProjectStoreService’** → the DTO ended up either missing or defined in a different namespace/file than the partials expect.

So yes: the last couple chats got harder because I tried to “add” split files (partials) while the **parent** had already been overwritten. That made everything look like contradictions/dupes.

### Fastest recovery plan (surgical + safe)

1) **Back up** your current `ProjectStore` folder.

2) In `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/` keep exactly these files (and only these) for the service:
   - `ProjectStoreService.cs`  ← the **base** you now have (with `Load`, `SaveAtomic`, `CreateDefault`, etc.)
   - `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs`  ← defines **only** DTOs like `SystemNextInfo` (no duplicates of Snapshot/ProjectEntry/etc.)
   - `ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs`  ← the V1 bridge helpers (read/write config, etc.)
   - `ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs`  ← the peek/next list helpers

   Everything else in that folder that defines **another** `ProjectStoreSnapshot`, `ProjectEntry`, `DrawingEntry`, `TaggingEntry`, `BlocksEntry` should be removed (you had those re-declared in `ProjectSettingsService.cs`—that’s what triggered the CS0101 dupe errors). Those snapshot models should live in **one** place only.

3) Open each of the four files above and confirm they all share **exactly** this:
   - `namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore`
   - `public static partial class ProjectStoreService` (for the three partials)
   - No duplicate class names across files.

4) In `LineTagSettings.cs` fix the C#7.3 generics issue by avoiding `??` with unconstrained `T` and by **specifying the type** when calling `Safe<T>`:
   - Change calls like:
     ```csharp
     var list = Safe(() => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath), (PSP.SystemNextInfo[])null);
     ```
     to:
     ```csharp
     PSP.SystemNextInfo[] list = Safe<PSP.SystemNextInfo[]>(() => PSP.GetSystemNextList(storePath), null);
     ```
   - If you previously used `list ?? Array.Empty<PSP.SystemNextInfo>()`, replace with:
     ```csharp
     var safeList = (list != null) ? list : Array.Empty<PSP.SystemNextInfo>();
     ```

5) In any code that still calls **V1** methods with **Snapshot** (or vice-versa), align the types:
   - `ProjectRepairCommands` should use **Snapshot** (`ProjectSettingsService` read/write).
   - The palette/preview helpers (`Peek*/GetSystemNextList`) should use the **V1** model via `ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs`.
   - If a file expects `SaveAtomic`/`CreateDefault` on **Snapshot**, keep those calls in the Snapshot layer; don’t point them at V1.

6) Rebuild. Remaining errors will point to any **stragglers**:
   - If you see **“already contains a definition for ‘X’”** → delete the duplicate file that re-declares that class.
   - If you see **“does not contain a definition for ‘X’”** → that method lives in the base; confirm the base file is present and in the same namespace/partial class.

If you want, paste me the **current file list** under `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/` with filenames; I’ll mark which to keep, which to delete, and I can regenerate clean copies of `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs`, `ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs`, and `ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs` to match your base exactly.

11/12/2025, 1:54:24 PM

You didn’t mess it up—this is a super common tangle when partials get introduced after an overwrite. Let’s un-tangle it cleanly.

Below are **drop-in, C# 7.3-compatible** files to restore a sane layout. Create these **as separate files** under the shown paths/names. Then delete any duplicate class definitions that collide with these (I call those out at the end).

---

# 1) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs`
_Base (V1 JSON load/save + shared helpers used by the partials)._
```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: ProjectStoreService.cs  (BASE)
// Target: C# 7.3
// Desc : V1 store load/save + shared helpers (no UI / no snapshot).
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // ---------- Core load/save for V1 store ----------

        public static V1.ProjectStoreV1 Load(string path)
        {
            var s = ReadJson<V1.ProjectStoreV1>(path);
            if (s == null) s = CreateDefault();
            SeedIfMissing(s);
            return s;
        }

        public static void Save(string path, V1.ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            if (store == null) store = CreateDefault();
            SeedIfMissing(store);
            AtomicWrite(path, store);
        }

        // ---------- Defaults / seed ----------

        public static V1.ProjectStoreV1 CreateDefault()
        {
            return new V1.ProjectStoreV1
            {
                Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1
                {
                    GlobalValvePrefix = "V",
                    GraceDays = 7,
                    PreferReleasedLowestFirst = true
                },
                Systems = new Dictionary<string, V1.SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                {
                    ["Default"] = new V1.SystemCounterV1
                    {
                        Prefix = "V",
                        Pad = 3,
                        Start = 1,
                        End = 999,
                        Next = 1
                    }
                }
            };
        }

        private static void SeedIfMissing(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix))
                s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = "V";

            if (s.Systems == null)
                s.Systems = new Dictionary<string, V1.SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            if (s.Systems.Count == 0)
                s.Systems["Default"] = new V1.SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 999, Next = 1 };

            foreach (var k in new List<string>(s.Systems.Keys))
                if (s.Systems[k] == null)
                    s.Systems[k] = new V1.SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 999, Next = 1 };
        }

        // ---------- JSON I/O ----------

        internal static T ReadJson<T>(string path)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path) || !File.Exists(path)) return default(T);
            var json = File.ReadAllText(path);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<T>(json);
        }

        internal static void AtomicWrite<T>(string path, T data)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path)) return;

            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp = path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(data, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);

            if (File.Exists(path)) File.Replace(tmp, path, null);
            else File.Move(tmp, path);
        }

        // ---------- helpers used by partials ----------

        internal static int Clamp(int next, int start, int end)
        {
            if (start <= 0) start = 1;
            if (end < start) end = start;
            if (next < start) next = start;
            if (next > end) next = end;
            return next;
        }

        internal static V1.SystemCounterV1 GetSystemOrFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;

            V1.SystemCounterV1 sys;
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name) && s.Systems.TryGetValue(name, out sys) && sys != null)
                return sys;

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Value; // first
            return null;
        }

        internal static string GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return null;
            foreach (var kv in s.Systems) return kv.Key; // first
            return null;
        }

        internal static void EnsureSystemExists(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string name)
        {
            if (s.Systems == null)
                s.Systems = new Dictionary<string, V1.SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            if (!s.Systems.ContainsKey(name))
                s.Systems[name] = new V1.SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 999, Next = 1 };
        }

        internal static string FormatTag(V1.SystemCounterV1 sys, string prefixOverride = null)
        {
            if (sys == null) return "V-000";
            var prefix = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefixOverride)
                         ? prefixOverride.Trim()
                         : (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix) ? sys.Prefix.Trim() : "V");
            var pad = sys.Pad > 0 ? sys.Pad : 3;
            var next = Clamp(sys.Next, sys.Start, sys.End);
            return prefix + "-" + next.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');
        }
    }
}
```

_DTOs only (no snapshots here)._

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public sealed class SystemNextInfo
    {
        public string System { get; set; }
        public string Prefix { get; set; }
        public int Next { get; set; }
        public int End { get; set; }
        public string NextTag { get; set; } // optional convenience
    }
}

# 3) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs`
_V1 config read/write helpers + simple queries._
```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs
// Desc: V1 config reads/writes + simple queries (path overloads).
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using System;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return s.Config != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix)
                 ? s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix.Trim()
                 : "V";
        }

        public static int ReadGraceDays(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return (s.Config != null && s.Config.GraceDays > 0) ? s.Config.GraceDays : 7;
        }

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return s.Config != null ? s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst : true;
        }

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string path, string prefix)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "V" : prefix.Trim();

            var first = GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s) ?? "Default";
            EnsureSystemExists(s, first);
            s.Systems[first].Prefix = s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix;

            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(string path, int days)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.GraceDays = days > 0 ? days : 7;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(string path, bool preferLowest)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            if (s.Config == null) s.Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1();
            s.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = preferLowest;
            Save(path, s);
        }

        public static bool SystemExists(string path, string name)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return s.Systems != null && s.Systems.ContainsKey(name);
        }

        public static string GetFirstSystemName(string path)
        {
            var s = Load(path);
            return GetFirstSystemNameFromStore(s);
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 4) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs`
_List/peek helpers for the palette & property grid (path + V1 overloads)._
```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs
// Desc: “peek / next” helpers used by UI (property grid, palette).
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        public static IList<SystemNextInfo> GetSystemNextList(string path)
            => GetSystemNextList(Load(path));

        public static string PeekLineTagString(string path, string systemKey = null)
            => PeekLineTagString(Load(path), systemKey);

        public static string PeekValveTagString(string path, string systemKey = null)
            => PeekValveTagString(Load(path), systemKey);

        public static IList<SystemNextInfo> GetSystemNextList(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            var list = new List<SystemNextInfo>();
            if (s?.Systems == null || s.Systems.Count == 0) return list;

            foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
            {
                var c = kv.Value ?? new V1.SystemCounterV1();
                var next = Math.Max(c.Start, c.Next);
                var end = c.End < c.Start ? c.Start : c.End;

                list.Add(new SystemNextInfo
                {
                    System = kv.Key,
                    Prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(c.Prefix) ? "V" : c.Prefix.Trim(),
                    Next = next,
                    End = end,
                    NextTag = FormatTag(c)
                });
            }

            return list.OrderBy(x => x.System, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList();
        }

        public static string PeekLineTagString(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey = null)
        {
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemKey);
            return FormatTag(sys);
        }

        public static string PeekValveTagString(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string systemKey = null)
        {
            var sys = GetSystemOrFirst(s, systemKey);
            var prefix = (s?.Config != null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix))
                        ? s.Config.GlobalValvePrefix.Trim()
                        : "V";
            return FormatTag(sys, prefix);
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 5) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectSnapshot.Models.cs`
_This holds the **Snapshot** models used by your “repair” and “bind drawing” flows._
```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: ProjectSnapshot.Models.cs
// Desc: Snapshot models (separate from V1). One authoritative copy only.
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using System.Collections.Generic;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public sealed class ProjectStoreSnapshot
    {
        public string ActiveProjectKey { get; set; }
        public Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry> Projects { get; set; }
    }

    public sealed class ProjectEntry
    {
        public string Name { get; set; }
        public List<DrawingEntry> Drawings { get; set; }
        public TaggingEntry Tagging { get; set; }
        public BlocksEntry Blocks { get; set; }
    }

    public sealed class DrawingEntry
    {
        public string Path { get; set; }
    }

    public sealed class TaggingEntry
    {
        // Channels (dynamic types in your repair tool, but simple POCOs here)
        public ValveChannel Valve { get; set; }
        public LineChannel Line { get; set; }
    }

    public sealed class BlocksEntry
    {
        public Dictionary<string, string> Types { get; set; } // simple map (name -> path) if needed
    }

    public sealed class ValveChannel
    {
        public string Prefix { get; set; }
        public int? Padding { get; set; }
        public int? NextNumber { get; set; }
    }

    public sealed class LineChannel
    {
        public string Prefix { get; set; }
        public int? Padding { get; set; }
        public int? NextNumber { get; set; }
    }
}
```

# 6) `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectSettingsService.cs`
_Snapshot reader/writer + helper to ensure a project and add a drawing._
```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: ProjectSettingsService.cs
// Desc: Read/Write snapshot + EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing helper.
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public sealed class ProjectSettingsService
    {
        private readonly string _path;

        public ProjectSettingsService(string path)
        {
            _path = path ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(path));
        }

        public ProjectStoreSnapshot Read()
        {
            if (!File.Exists(_path)) return CreateDefaultSnapshot();
            var json = File.ReadAllText(_path);
            var s = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<ProjectStoreSnapshot>(json) ?? CreateDefaultSnapshot();
            if (s.Projects == null) s.Projects = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            return s;
        }

        public void Write(ProjectStoreSnapshot s)
        {
            if (s == null) s = CreateDefaultSnapshot();
            if (s.Projects == null) s.Projects = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(_path);
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);

            var tmp = _path + ".tmp";
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(s, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);

            if (File.Exists(_path)) File.Replace(tmp, _path, null);
            else File.Move(tmp, _path);
        }

        public void EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing(string projectKey, string drawingPath)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(projectKey)) projectKey = "Default";
            var s = Read();

            ProjectEntry p;
            if (!s.Projects.TryGetValue(projectKey, out p) || p == null)
            {
                p = new ProjectEntry
                {
                    Name = projectKey,
                    Drawings = new List<DrawingEntry>(),
                    Tagging = new TaggingEntry
                    {
                        Valve = new ValveChannel { Prefix = "V-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 },
                        Line  = new LineChannel  { Prefix = "L-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 }
                    },
                    Blocks = new BlocksEntry { Types = new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase) }
                };
                s.Projects[projectKey] = p;
            }

            if (p.Drawings == null) p.Drawings = new List<DrawingEntry>();
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(drawingPath) &&
                p.Drawings.Find(d => string.Equals(d.Path, drawingPath, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) == null)
            {
                p.Drawings.Add(new DrawingEntry { Path = drawingPath });
            }

            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.ActiveProjectKey))
                s.ActiveProjectKey = projectKey;

            Write(s);
        }

        private static ProjectStoreSnapshot CreateDefaultSnapshot()
        {
            return new ProjectStoreSnapshot
            {
                ActiveProjectKey = "Default",
                Projects = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                {
                    ["Default"] = new ProjectEntry
                    {
                        Name = "Default",
                        Drawings = new List<DrawingEntry>(),
                        Tagging = new TaggingEntry
                        {
                            Valve = new ValveChannel { Prefix = "V-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 },
                            Line  = new LineChannel  { Prefix = "L-", Padding = 3, NextNumber = 1 }
                        },
                        Blocks = new BlocksEntry { Types = new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase) }
                    }
                }
            };
        }
    }
}
```

---

## What to delete (the “duplicates”)
Delete (or comment out) any files that re-declare these types **again** in the same namespace `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore`:

- `ProjectStoreSnapshot`
- `ProjectEntry`
- `DrawingEntry`
- `TaggingEntry`
- `BlocksEntry`
- `SystemNextInfo`

You mentioned some of these were inside your old `ProjectSettingsService.cs`—remove those model declarations from there. With the layout above, **models live only** in `ProjectSnapshot.Models.cs` and `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs`.

Also remove any earlier “partial” files that accidentally included base logic (e.g., JSON I/O) or redefined methods like `Load`, `Save`, `AtomicWrite`, `CreateDefault`. Those belong only in **ProjectStoreService.cs (BASE)** now.

---

## Small fixes you’ll still need in your other code

- **LineTagSettings.cs** (C# 7.3 generic `??` issue): specify the generic on `Safe<T>` and avoid null-coalescing with unconstrained T:
  ```csharp
  // Old (problem in C# 7.3):
  // var list = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList(storePath), (ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo[])null);

  // New:
  var list = Safe<SystemNextInfo[]>(() => AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService
                                           .GetSystemNextList(storePath)
                                           .ToArray(),
                                    null);
  var safeList = (list != null) ? list : new SystemNextInfo[0];
  ```

- **BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs**: it should use the **Snapshot** service:
  ```csharp
  var psvc = new ProjectSettingsService(storePath);
  psvc.EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing(projectKey, currentDwgPath);
  ```

- **ProjectRepairCommands.cs**: keep it pointed at `ProjectSettingsService` (Snapshot). If it references V1 types anywhere, switch them to Snapshot equivalents.

- **Any references to `SaveAtomic` on `ProjectStoreService`**: that was a Snapshot-side convenience. If you still call it, change those call sites to use `new ProjectSettingsService(path).Write(snapshot)` instead.

---

## Why this works
- We restored a **single, authoritative** base for V1 with partials layered on top.
- We separated **Snapshot** models/services from the V1 store so your repair/binding tools don’t collide with palette/tag preview logic.
- We removed the duplication that caused `CS0101/CS0111/CS0229`.
- Everything is C# 7.3-friendly.

If you paste me the **current file list** under `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/` (just the filenames), I’ll mark keep/delete and check for any stragglers.

> **Reviewing file organization and duplicates**
>
> I need to guide on which files to keep, delete, or rename to address file duplication. It seems the critical files can be kept while removing duplicates like `NextReaders.cs` (duplicate name in root), `ProjectStoreDefaults.cs`, `ProjectStoreModels.cs`, `ProjectStorePaths.cs`, and `ProjectStoreService.Core.cs`. Mark these for deletion or archiving due to class duplications or method ambiguities.
>
> **File management guidance**
>
> I’m recommending to keep the following files for core functionality: `V1/ProjectStoreV1.cs, ProjectStoreService.cs, ProjectStoreService.Models.cs, ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs, ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs, ProjectSnapshot.Models.cs, ProjectSettingsService.cs`.
> 
> To delete or archive, I suggest files that conflict or duplicate others: `NextReaders.cs (root), ProjectStoreDefaults.cs, ProjectStoreModels.cs, ProjectStorePaths.cs, ProjectStoreService.Core.cs` and some optional files like `ProjectStoreValidator.cs`, `StoreGuards.cs`, `TagStoreV1.cs`, `NewProjectBuilder.cs`, and the Valves folder (review the updates).
>
> Thought for 36s

You’re good—this is fixable. The “what to delete” meant **whole files** that re-declare the same classes/methods (not just methods inside a file). Based on your screenshot, here’s exactly what to **KEEP vs ARCHIVE** so we get back to a clean, non-duplicated layout.

# What to KEEP (authoritative)
These are the files I gave you (or their equivalents). Leave them as-is.

- **V1/**
  - `ProjectStoreV1.cs`  ✅  (the V1 schema)
- **ProjectStore/**
  - `ProjectStoreService.cs`  ✅  (BASE: Load/Save/AtomicWrite/Clamp/FormatTag/etc.)
  - `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs`  ✅  (`SystemNextInfo` only)
  - `ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs`  ✅  (Read/Write settings, SystemExists, GetFirstSystemName)
  - `ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs`  ✅  (GetSystemNextList/Peek… path+V1 overloads)
  - `ProjectSnapshot.Models.cs`  ✅  (Snapshot: ProjectStoreSnapshot/ProjectEntry/etc.)
  - `ProjectSettingsService.cs`  ✅  (Snapshot reader/writer + EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing)

# What to ARCHIVE (move to `ProjectStore/_archive/` or delete)
These are the usual culprits causing **CS0101/CS0111/CS0229** (duplicate type/member) and the “method is ambiguous” errors. They either re-declare models or re-implement logic now owned by the KEEP files.

- `NextReaders.cs`  🔧 (conflicts with `ProjectStoreService.NextReaders.cs`; archive it)
- `ProjectStoreDefaults.cs`  🔧 (usually re-defines defaults/CreateDefault; archive)
- `ProjectStoreModels.cs`  🔧 (**likely** re-declares `ProjectStoreSnapshot/ProjectEntry/...`; archive)
- `ProjectStorePaths.cs`  🔧 (if it only has constants you need, you can keep—but if it also defines Load/Save or models, archive; safest: archive for now)
- `ProjectStoreService.Core.cs`  🔧 (duplicate of base service methods; archive)
- `ProjectStoreValidator.cs`  🟨 (optional – if not referenced, archive now to reduce noise)
- `StoreGuards.cs`  🟨 (optional – archive if unused)
- `TagStoreV1.cs`  🟨 (if it duplicates V1 counters/config or wraps V1 with overlapping APIs, archive)
- `NewProjectBuilder.cs`  🟨 (tooling; archive if unused during compile)
- **Valves/** folder: keep the *files you actually use* (e.g., `ValveDefinitionResolver.cs`). If any file in here re-declares snapshot models or service methods, archive *that* file. Otherwise keep.

> Rule of thumb: after the clean, there should be **one and only one** place that defines  
> - `ProjectStoreSnapshot/ProjectEntry/...` → `ProjectSnapshot.Models.cs`  
> - `SystemNextInfo` → `ProjectStoreService.Models.cs`  
> - `Load/Save/CreateDefault/AtomicWrite/FormatTag/Clamp/...` → `ProjectStoreService.cs` (BASE)

---

## Quick fixes to clear the remaining compiler errors

1) **CS0229 “Ambiguity … Snapshot/Projects/ActiveProjectKey”**  
This is exactly from duplicate model declarations. After archiving the files above (especially `ProjectStoreModels.cs` and any other file that re-declares snapshot DTOs), these go away.

2) **`SaveAtomic` missing**  
Replace those calls with the snapshot writer:
```csharp
var svc = new ProjectSettingsService(storePath);
svc.Write(snapshot);
```
(If you were passing V1 store, use `ProjectStoreService.Save(path, v1Store)` instead.)

3) **`CreateDefault` missing**  
That’s provided by `ProjectStoreService.cs` (BASE). If you still see the error after the archive step, you still have a duplicate “core” file shadowing it. Archive `ProjectStoreService.Core.cs` / `ProjectStoreDefaults.cs`.

4) **`EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing` missing**  
That’s in `ProjectSettingsService.cs` (snapshot). In your callers:
```csharp
var psvc = new ProjectSettingsService(storePath);
psvc.EnsureProjectAndAddDrawing(projectKey, currentDwgPath);
```

5) **`LineTagSettings.cs` generic `??` issue (C# 7.3)**  
Don’t use unconstrained `T ??`. Do this instead:
```csharp
var arr = Safe<SystemNextInfo[]>(
             () => AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService
                      .GetSystemNextList(storePath)
                      .ToArray(),
             null);
var safeArr = (arr != null) ? arr : new SystemNextInfo[0];
```

6) **`ValveDefinitionResolver.cs` & friends**  
Make sure they target the **Snapshot** when they need projects/drawings:
```csharp
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore; // Snapshot models here

// expect: ProjectStoreSnapshot, ProjectEntry, etc. from ProjectSnapshot.Models.cs
```
If those files were previously pointing at V1 for project/drawing access, switch them to Snapshot or update their logic accordingly.

---

ProjectStore/

  ProjectSettingsService.cs

## Response:
11/12/2025, 2:54:44 PM

You didn’t mess it up—these 7 are all “last mile” fixes. Do the quick edits below and they’ll clear.

---

# 1) ProjectRepairCommands.cs (use **Snapshot** API only)
Replace the whole command body with this (it uses `ProjectSettingsService`, not V1):

```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_RepairProjectStore")]
public static void CBI_RepairProjectStoreClass()
{
    try
    {
        // If you keep a central path constant, use it here instead of hardcoding.
        var svc = new ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");
        var snap = svc.Read();                       // SNAPSHOT
        bool changed = RepairSnapshotInPlace(snap);  // SNAPSHOT

        if (changed) svc.Write(snap);                // SNAPSHOT

        Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.ShowAlertDialog(
            "Project store repair completed." + (changed ? " (changes were written)" : " (no changes necessary)"));
    }
    catch (System.Exception ex)
    {
        Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.ShowAlertDialog("Repair failed: " + ex.Message);
    }
}
```

This removes the “convert V1 → Snapshot” error.

---

# 2) LineTagSettings.cs (CS0136 “first” already used)
You’ve declared `first` twice in the same method. In `ResolvePreviewSystem(...)`, just rename the *second* one:

```csharp
// first available
var firstName = Safe(() => ProjectStoreService.GetFirstSystemName(storePath), (string)null);
if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(firstName)) return firstName;
```

(Leave the earlier local named `first` alone.)

---

# 3) TagAddService.cs (SaveAtomic → Save)
Find the line calling `ProjectStoreService.SaveAtomic(...)` and replace with:

```csharp
ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, storeV1);
```

(If your variable isn’t named `storeV1`, pass whatever `V1.ProjectStoreV1` instance you have.)

---

# 4) TagManagerService.cs (CreateDefault missing)
Two options—pick **A** (fastest):

**A) Add these tiny shims** to **`ProjectStoreService.LegacyCompat.cs`** (or the base `ProjectStoreService.cs`):

```csharp
// --- convenience shims for legacy call sites ---
public static void SaveAtomic(string path, V1.ProjectStoreV1 store) => Save(path, store);

public static V1.ProjectStoreV1 CreateDefault()
{
    return new V1.ProjectStoreV1
    {
        Config = new V1.StoreConfigV1
        {
            GlobalValvePrefix = "V",
            GraceDays = 7,
            PreferReleasedLowestFirst = true
        },
        Systems = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, V1.SystemCounterV1>
        {
            ["Default"] = new V1.SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "V", Pad = 3, Start = 1, End = 999, Next = 1 }
        }
    };
}
```

Now your existing call to `CreateDefault()` compiles.

**B) Or** change the call site to build a default directly (no shim). If you want this route, I can give the exact replacement snippet for that line.

---

# 5) ReconcileCurrentDrawing.cs (ProjectStorePaths & TagStoreV1)
Since we archived those helpers, replace them:

- At the top of the file, add a local constant (or use your central constant):

```csharp
const string DefaultStorePath = @"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json";
```

- Replace any `ProjectStorePaths.Whatever` with `DefaultStorePath`.
- Replace `TagStoreV1.Load(path)` with `ProjectStoreService.Load(path)`.
- Replace `ProjectStoreService.SaveAtomic(path, store)` with `ProjectStoreService.Save(path, store)`.

That resolves all three errors in this file.

---

# 6) (Optional but handy) Add a tiny “paths” helper back
If you prefer not to hardcode the path in multiple files, add a **new** file:
**`ProjectStore/ProjectStorePaths.cs`**

```csharp
namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectStorePaths
    {
        public const string DefaultStorePath = @"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json";
    }
}
```

Then use `ProjectStorePaths.DefaultStorePath` instead of the literal.

---

If anything still complains, paste the **new** error list and I’ll knock out whatever remains.
