11/12/2025, 6:38:41 PM

Instead of assuming on item 5 here is my code.

// v1.0.1 — TagAddService wired to ProjectStore v1.0 (C# 7.3, Newtonsoft.Json)
// No external XData helpers required; bubble step is a no-op for now.
using System;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD2020.NOD_X_Data;                       // ProjectStoreNod
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;          // ProjectStoreService
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public static class TagAddService
    {
        private const string RegApp = "CBI_TAG_LINKS";
        private const string KeyValveId = "VALVE_TAG_ID";

        public static void Run()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            var ed = doc.Editor;
            var db = doc.Database;

            // 1) Resolve ProjectStore JSON
            var storePath = ProjectStoreNod.GetPathOrPrompt(doc);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nNo ProjectStore selected. Command cancelled.");
                return;
            }

            // 2) Pick valve block
            var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect valve block:");
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a block reference.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(BlockReference), false);
            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var br = (BlockReference)tr.GetObject(per.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                var handleHex = br.Handle.ToString();
                var drawing = doc.Name ?? "(unsaved)";

                // 3) Load store
                var store = ProjectStoreService.Load(storePath);

                // 4) Reuse existing tag if present, else allocate
                string tagId = TryGetPrimaryValveId(tr, br);
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(tagId))
                {
                    var systemKey = "GENERAL"; // TODO: derive from palette/settings
                    var rec = TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(store, systemKey, drawing, handleHex);
                    tagId = rec.Id;

                    // Persist JSON
                    ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1.ProjectStoreV1);
                }

                // 5) Stamp XData and (optionally) bubble
                br.UpgradeOpen();
                SetPrimaryValveId(tr, br, tagId);
                EnsureOrSyncBubblePlaceholder(tr, br, tagId);

                tr.Commit();
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nTag assigned: {tagId}");
            }
        }

        // -------------------------
        // Minimal XData utilities
        // -------------------------

        private static void EnsureRegApp(Transaction tr, Database db, string appName)
        {
            var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!rat.Has(appName))
            {
                rat.UpgradeOpen();
                var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                rat.Add(rec);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
            }
        }

        private static string TryGetPrimaryValveId(Transaction tr, BlockReference br)
        {
            var db = br.Database;
            EnsureRegApp(tr, db, RegApp);

            var rb = br.XData;
            if (rb == null) return null;

            string lastKey = null;
            foreach (TypedValue tv in rb)
            {
                if (tv.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName) continue;

                if (tv.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString)
                {
                    var s = tv.Value as string;
                    if (lastKey == KeyValveId) return s;
                    lastKey = s; // treat previous as key
                }
            }
            return null;
        }

        private static void SetPrimaryValveId(Transaction tr, BlockReference br, string id)
        {
            var db = br.Database;
            EnsureRegApp(tr, db, RegApp);

            // Rebuild a simple [1001 RegApp][1000 Key][1000 Value] buffer,
            // while preserving any non-our entries if present.
            var newBuf = new ResultBuffer(
                new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, RegApp),
                new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, KeyValveId),
                new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, id ?? string.Empty)
            );

            // If existing buffer has other apps, we could merge; to keep it simple and safe
            // we replace only our app block, leaving other apps intact by re-adding them.
            var old = br.XData;
            if (old != null)
            {
                var merged = new ResultBuffer();
                bool inOurApp = false;
                foreach (TypedValue tv in old)
                {
                    if (tv.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName)
                    {
                        // starting a new app run
                        inOurApp = string.Equals(tv.Value as string, RegApp, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
                        if (!inOurApp)
                            merged.Add(tv);
                        else
                        {
                            // skip our old run; add new one instead
                            foreach (TypedValue ntv in newBuf)
                                merged.Add(ntv);
                        }
                        continue;
                    }
                    if (!inOurApp) merged.Add(tv);
                }
                // If our app wasn't present at all, append it now
                bool hadOurApp = old.AsArray().Length != merged.AsArray().Length;
                if (!hadOurApp)
                {
                    foreach (TypedValue ntv in newBuf)
                        merged.Add(ntv);
                }
                br.XData = merged;
            }
            else
            {
                br.XData = newBuf;
            }
        }

        // Placeholder until your bubble code is wired in.
        private static void EnsureOrSyncBubblePlaceholder(Transaction tr, BlockReference br, string id)
        {
            // TODO: call your real bubble insert/sync here.
            // For now, do nothing so this compiles/runs without BubbleGraphics.cs.
        }
    }
}

## Response:
11/12/2025, 6:38:48 PM

You’re right to stop and verify—thanks for pasting the exact file. Here’s a **clean drop-in** for `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAddService.cs` that fixes the issues I see in your current version:

- Uses the correct `TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(...)` signature (adds `reason` and `by`).
- Saves the **actual** `store` object (`ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store)`), not a type.
- Keeps everything C# 7.3 friendly and leaves your NOD resolver + minimal XData intact.
- Leaves a TODO hook for your bubble sync.

Replace your file with this:

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File   : AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAddService.cs
// Version: v1.0.2 (C# 7.3)
// Notes  :
//  - Fixes AllocateNext(...) call to include reason/by
//  - Fixes Save(...) to pass the loaded store instance
//  - Leaves your NOD resolver and minimal XDATA utilities intact
// ============================================================================
using System;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD2020.NOD_X_Data;                       // ProjectStoreNod (GetPathOrPrompt)
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;          // ProjectStoreService, TagStoreV1
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public static class TagAddService
    {
        private const string RegApp   = "CBI_TAG_LINKS";
        private const string KeyValveId = "VALVE_TAG_ID";

        public static void Run()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed  = doc.Editor;
            var db  = doc.Database;

            // 1) Resolve ProjectStore JSON
            var storePath = ProjectStoreNod.GetPathOrPrompt(doc);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nNo ProjectStore selected. Command cancelled.");
                return;
            }

            // 2) Pick valve block
            var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect valve block:");
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a block reference.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(BlockReference), false);
            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var br        = (BlockReference)tr.GetObject(per.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                var handleHex = br.Handle.ToString();
                var drawing   = doc.Name ?? "(unsaved)";

                // 3) Load store
                var store = ProjectStoreService.Load(storePath);

                // 4) Reuse existing tag if present, else allocate
                string tagId = TryGetPrimaryValveId(tr, br);
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(tagId))
                {
                    // TODO: derive from palette/settings; keep GENERAL for now
                    var systemKey = "GENERAL";
                    var rec = TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(
                        store,
                        systemKey,
                        drawing,
                        handleHex,
                        reason: "Tag allocation",
                        by: Environment.UserName ?? "unknown");

                    tagId = rec.Id;

                    // Persist JSON (write the instance we just updated)
                    ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
                }

                // 5) Stamp XData and (optionally) bubble
                br.UpgradeOpen();
                SetPrimaryValveId(tr, br, tagId);
                EnsureOrSyncBubblePlaceholder(tr, br, tagId);

                tr.Commit();
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nTag assigned: {tagId}");
            }
        }

        // -------------------------
        // Minimal XData utilities
        // -------------------------

        private static void EnsureRegApp(Transaction tr, Database db, string appName)
        {
            var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!rat.Has(appName))
            {
                rat.UpgradeOpen();
                var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                rat.Add(rec);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
            }
        }

        private static string TryGetPrimaryValveId(Transaction tr, BlockReference br)
        {
            var db = br.Database;
            EnsureRegApp(tr, db, RegApp);

            var rb = br.XData;
            if (rb == null) return null;

            string lastKey = null;
            foreach (TypedValue tv in rb)
            {
                if (tv.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName) continue;

                if (tv.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString)
                {
                    var s = tv.Value as string;
                    if (lastKey == KeyValveId) return s;
                    lastKey = s; // treat previous as key
                }
            }
            return null;
        }

        private static void SetPrimaryValveId(Transaction tr, BlockReference br, string id)
        {
            var db = br.Database;
            EnsureRegApp(tr, db, RegApp);

            var newBuf = new ResultBuffer(
                new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, RegApp),
                new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, KeyValveId),
                new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, id ?? string.Empty)
            );

            var old = br.XData;
            if (old != null)
            {
                var merged = new ResultBuffer();
                bool inOurApp = false;
                bool wroteOurApp = false;

                foreach (TypedValue tv in old)
                {
                    if (tv.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName)
                    {
                        // starting a new app block
                        inOurApp = string.Equals(tv.Value as string, RegApp, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
                        if (!inOurApp)
                        {
                            merged.Add(tv);
                        }
                        else
                        {
                            // skip old run; write new one
                            foreach (TypedValue ntv in newBuf) merged.Add(ntv);
                            wroteOurApp = true;
                        }
                        continue;
                    }
                    if (!inOurApp) merged.Add(tv);
                }

                if (!wroteOurApp)
                {
                    foreach (TypedValue ntv in newBuf) merged.Add(ntv);
                }

                br.XData = merged;
            }
            else
            {
                br.XData = newBuf;
            }
        }

        // Placeholder until your bubble code is wired in.
        private static void EnsureOrSyncBubblePlaceholder(Transaction tr, BlockReference br, string id)
        {
            // TODO: hook your bubble insert/sync logic here.
        }
    }
}
```

If you still see errors from this file after pasting, tell me the exact line numbers and messages and I’ll adjust immediately.

## Prompt:
11/12/2025, 6:41:41 PM

I dont understand item 6 build sequence. I only build the project solution not the individual projects.

## Response:
11/12/2025, 6:41:45 PM

Totally fair question—here’s what “build sequence” means for your setup:

### What to build (and what not to)
- **AutoCAD_Shared_Project** is a **Shared Project**. It **doesn’t build to a DLL** by itself. It’s compiled **into** whatever “real” project references it.
- **AutoCAD2020** (and any other concrete class library/exe projects) are the ones that actually **build**.

### One-time setup (so VS knows the right order)
1. In VS: **Build → Configuration Manager…**
   - Ensure **AutoCAD2020** is **checked** to build.
   - The **Shared Project** will not have a build checkbox (that’s normal).
2. In VS: **Project → Project Dependencies…**
   - Select **AutoCAD2020** in the dropdown.
   - Ensure it **depends on** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project`. (This just guarantees compile order; VS usually infers it from references.)
3. Make sure **AutoCAD2020** **references** the Shared Project (Right-click **AutoCAD2020 → Add → Reference… → Shared Projects**).

### Clean → Build sequence (the practical steps)
1. **Build → Clean Solution**
2. **Build → Rebuild Solution** (or **Build → Build Solution**)
   - VS will first compile the shared code *as part of* **AutoCAD2020**’s build.
   - You do **not** build the Shared Project separately.

### When you have lots of red squiggles
- If errors appear in `AutoCAD2020` and refer to types from the Shared Project, it still means: **fix the errors and rebuild the Solution** (not the Shared Project).
- If you temporarily need to isolate a subset, you can:
  - Right-click projects that aren’t needed right now → **Unload Project**, or
  - Use **Configuration Manager** to uncheck build for other (non-shared) projects.

### Quick sanity checks (if build order still feels wrong)
- In **AutoCAD2020.csproj**, confirm there’s an `<Import Project="..\AutoCAD_Shared_Project\AutoCAD_Shared_Project.shproj" />` or a Shared Projects reference entry (VS manages this for you).
- In **Solution Explorer**, the Shared Project shows the “shared” glyph and sits under **Shared Projects**—that’s expected.

If you want, paste your **Configuration Manager** grid (a screenshot) and the **Project Dependencies** dialog for **AutoCAD2020**, and I’ll confirm everything’s wired correctly.

![Image](https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/estuary/content?id=file_000000004d9471f78c2ecbe2b4181163&ts=495743&p=fs&cid=1&sig=08335ce40f21768dadb92a408a0c1d29ab1c8b3a75a8442fceec050ea726a498&v=0)

## Response:
11/12/2025, 6:46:05 PM

Perfect — your screenshots are actually **really helpful** 👍  
Let’s go through this carefully — you’re in good shape.  

---

### ✅  What You’re Seeing
You have a **Shared Project** (`AutoCAD_Shared_Project`) and a **main project** (`AutoCAD2020`).  
That means:
- The **shared project doesn’t appear in Configuration Manager** (normal).
- The **shared project builds automatically** as part of `AutoCAD2020`.  
- You **don’t** manually build it. You just **build the solution** (as you’re doing).

So far, everything is correct in your screenshots.

---

### 🔍  Why You Don’t See “Project Dependencies”
Visual Studio only shows **“Project Dependencies”** when:
- You have **2 or more buildable projects** (e.g., two `.csproj` files that both produce DLLs or EXEs).
- But since a **Shared Project (`.shproj`)** is not compiled separately, VS hides that option.

So again:  
> ✅ You do **not** need to manually set project dependencies.

`AutoCAD2020` automatically pulls in and compiles everything under `AutoCAD_Shared_Project`.

---

### 🧭  Here’s What You Should Do From Here

1. **Confirm the Shared Project is properly referenced:**
   - In **Solution Explorer**, right-click `AutoCAD2020 → Add → Reference…`
   - Go to the **Shared Projects** tab.
   - Ensure **AutoCAD_Shared_Project** is checked.

2. **Then, build the entire solution:**
   - Top menu: `Build → Rebuild Solution`

3. **Watch the Output window:**
   - If you see messages about compiling files from both projects, it’s working as expected.
   - If not, we’ll fix the `.csproj` reference line manually.

---

### 🧩  If for some reason Visual Studio lost the link:
You can check the reference manually:
1. Open your file:
   ```
   AutoCAD2020\AutoCAD2020.csproj
   ```
2. Near the bottom, you should see a line like:
   ```xml
   <Import Project="..\AutoCAD_Shared_Project\AutoCAD_Shared_Project.shproj" Label="Shared" />
   ```
3. If it’s missing, that’s why the build can’t see shared classes — and we’ll add it back.

---

Would you please open the `AutoCAD2020.csproj` in Notepad (or Visual Studio → right-click → *Edit Project File*) and show me the last 10 lines or so?

That’ll let me confirm the shared link is wired correctly before we try the next build.

## Response:
11/12/2025, 6:56:51 PM

Totally fair—let me untangle that. There are **two separate things** in `Commands.cs`:

## A) Tag Manager routes (stay on `TagManagerService`)
These three commands must call **TagManagerService**:
```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans")]
public static void CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans()
    => Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.SweepAutoReleaseOrphans();

[CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve")]
public static void CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve()
    => Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve();

[CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_AddToValve")]
public static void CBI_Tag_AddToValve()
    => Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.AddTagToSelectedValve();
```
Make sure the file has:
```csharp
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;
```
…and that the class lives in:
```csharp
namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services { public static class TagManagerService { … } }
```

## B) Valve **placement/tag** routes (go to your **Services** folder, not `Valves.*`)
These are unrelated to TagManagerService and should call your existing **ValveInsertService** / **ValveTagService**:
```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve", CommandFlags.Modal | CommandFlags.UsePickSet)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve()
    => Services.ValveInsertService.Run();

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Ball", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Ball()
    => Services.ValveInsertService.Run();

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly()
    => Services.ValveInsertService.Run();

[CommandMethod("CBI_ValveTag")]
public static void CBI_ValveTag()
    => Services.ValveTagService.Run();
```

So:  
- If you get “TagManagerService doesn’t exist,” fix the **namespace/class name** for TagManagerService (A).  
- If you get “`AutoCAD2020.Valves.InsertValveCommand` doesn’t exist,” change those commands to call **Services.ValveInsertService** / **ValveTagService** (B) as above.

That’s the only reason I mentioned both—they fix **different** error groups in the same file.

## Response:
11/12/2025, 6:58:48 PM

Excellent — that screenshot clarifies **exactly** what’s happening. 👍  

Here’s the breakdown and the fix:

---

## ✅ What’s happening

Your class **ValveInsertService** is in the namespace:
```csharp
namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
```

But in **Commands.cs**, the calls are written as:
```csharp
Services.ValveInsertService.Run();
```

…and Visual Studio complains:
> “`AutoCAD2020.Services` does not exist…”

That’s **100% correct**, because the real path is `AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services`, not `AutoCAD2020.Services`.

---

## ✅ Two clean options to fix it

### **Option 1 (Recommended): Add a `using`**
At the very top of **Commands.cs**, make sure this line is there:
```csharp
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;
```

Then your calls stay simple:
```csharp
ValveInsertService.Run();
ValveTagService.Run();
```

This is cleaner and aligns with how we route everything else in that file.

---

### **Option 2: Fully qualify**
If you prefer not to add another `using`, just change each line to:
```csharp
AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services.ValveInsertService.Run();
AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services.ValveTagService.Run();
```

But Option 1 is the better convention.

---

## 🧩 So your valve section in **Commands.cs** should look like this:
```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve", CommandFlags.Modal | CommandFlags.UsePickSet)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve()
    => ValveInsertService.Run();

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Ball", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Ball()
    => ValveInsertService.Run();

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly()
    => ValveInsertService.Run();

[CommandMethod("CBI_ValveTag")]
public static void CBI_ValveTag()
    => ValveTagService.Run();
```

…and make sure at the top of the file you have:
```csharp
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;
```

---

After you save that, **rebuild the solution**.  
That one fix should immediately remove all 4 valve command errors you’re seeing (`CS0234`).

Once you confirm that’s clean, we’ll tackle the remaining few (`StoreConfigV1` and TagManagerService ones) — they’re separate issues but much smaller.

## Response:
11/12/2025, 7:02:07 PM

You’re right—`.Run()` doesn’t exist on your `ValveInsertService`, so Commands can’t call it. Let’s make the routing crystal-clear and fix it in two tiny edits.

## 1) Add public entry points to `ValveInsertService`
Open **AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/ValveInsertService.cs** and add these wrappers (keep your existing logic as-is; just append these methods inside the class):

```csharp
// Put these inside: namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services { internal static class ValveInsertService { ... } }
public static void Run()
{
    // call your existing interactive insert (whatever its name is)
    // if your method is named differently, point to it here:
    PlaceValveInteractive();
}

public static void RunBall()
{
    // call your existing insert with a fixed “Ball” option
    PlaceValveInteractive("Ball");
}

public static void RunButterfly()
{
    // call your existing insert with a fixed “Butterfly” option
    PlaceValveInteractive("Butterfly");
}
```

> If your core method is named something else (e.g., `PlaceValveInteractive`, `InsertValveInteractive`, etc.), just update the calls above to match. These wrappers give Commands a stable surface.

## 2) Point Commands at those methods
At the **top** of **Commands.cs** ensure you have:
```csharp
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;
```

Then update the valve commands:

```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve", CommandFlags.Modal | CommandFlags.UsePickSet)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve()
    => ValveInsertService.Run();

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Ball", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Ball()
    => ValveInsertService.RunBall();

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly()
    => ValveInsertService.RunButterfly();
```

That removes the “`.Run` not found” and the namespace confusion in one shot.

---

## One more quick fix you’ll likely hit next
Your Error List shows `StoreConfigV1` “does not contain Start/End”. Those properties belong to **system counters**, not the top-level config. If you see code like:

```csharp
StoreConfigV1.Start
StoreConfigV1.End
```

replace it with the system entry:

```csharp
var sys = store.Systems[systemKey]; // SystemCounterV1
// use sys.Start, sys.End, sys.Next, sys.Prefix, sys.Pad
```

(Top-level `StoreConfigV1` should only have things like `GlobalValvePrefix`, `GraceDays`, `PreferReleasedLowestFirst`.)

---

Do those two edits (Valve wrappers + Commands.cs), build again, and tell me the **new top 5 errors**—we’ll keep shaving them down methodically. You’re not alone; we’ll get this compiling.

// File: ValveInsertService.cs
// Project: AutoCAD2020
// Namespace: AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
// LanguageVersion: 9.0 (C# 7.3 target ok)
// SemVer: 0.1.0
//
// Summary:
//   Single-document, interactive valve insertion flow.
//   - Optional: select polyline for line context (XData keys under "CBI").
//   - Insert block "CBI_VALVE" at picked point (and rotation).
//   - Allocate next Valve Tag by scope (AREA-SERVICE) via NOD sequence.
//   - Write XData (CBI_UID, CBI_KIND=Valve, mirror props) to block.
//   - Persist cross-ref to NOD: CBI/ProjectStore/Valves/{UID} as XRecord.
//   - Select the newly inserted block so the CBI Properties Palette shows it.
//
// Integration points (non-breaking):
//   - Uses existing NOD helpers if available (NODWriter, etc.) but contains safe fallbacks.
//   - Emits a simple ChangeHistory hook (no-throw) you can wire into your existing manager.
//
// Notes:
//   - Attribute names assumed: TAG, LINE_NO, SERVICE, SIZE, SPEC, AREA, UNIT, CBI_UID
//     (extra attributes in the block are ignored; missing ones are skipped).
//   - Block name assumed: "CBI_VALVE" (change in Constants if needed).

using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    internal static class ValveInsertService
    {
        private static class Constants
        {
            public const string BlockName = "CBI_VALVE";

            // XData
            public const string AppName = "CBI";
            public const string XdKindKey = "CBI_KIND";
            public const string XdKindValve = "Valve";
            public const string XdUidKey = "CBI_UID";

            // Common property keys
            public const string KeyTag = "TAG";
            public const string KeyLineNo = "LINE_NO";
            public const string KeyService = "SERVICE";
            public const string KeySize = "SIZE";
            public const string KeySpec = "SPEC";
            public const string KeyArea = "AREA";
            public const string KeyUnit = "UNIT";

            // NOD tree
            public const string NodRoot = "CBI";
            public const string NodProjectStore = "ProjectStore";
            public const string NodValves = "Valves";
            public const string NodSequences = "Sequences";
            public const string NodValveSeq = "ValveTag";
        }

        // Add near the top of the class:
        internal sealed class ValveInsertOptions
        {
            public string BlockName { get; set; } = "INST_BALL-VALVE"; // default
            public string LayerName { get; set; } = "";          // optional
                                                                 // future: symbol scale, attr map overrides, etc.
        }

        internal static class BlockLoadConfig
        {
            // Add/adjust to your environment
            public static readonly string[] BlockSearchDirs = new[]
            {
                @"C:\CBICAD\AutoCAD\Blocks\Instrumentation",
                @"C:\CbiCAD\Blocks",
                @"C:\CBICAD" // fallback
            };
        }

        public static void run()
        {
            PlaceValveInteractive();
        }

        public static void RunBall()
        {
            PlaceValveInteractive("Ball");
        }

        // --- ONLY ONE no-arg wrapper (keeps old callers working) ---
        public static void PlaceValveInteractive()
        {
            PlaceValveInteractive(new ValveInsertOptions()); // defaults to CBI_VALVE
        }

        // --- NEW overload that accepts options (Ball, Butterfly, etc.) ---
        public static void PlaceValveInteractive(ValveInsertOptions options)
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var db = doc.Database;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            // 1) Optional: pick a polyline to inherit context
            var context = TryGetLineContextFromPick(ed, db);

            // 2) Ask for insertion point
            var ppr = ed.GetPoint("\nSpecify valve insertion point: ");
            if (ppr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;
            var insPt = ppr.Value;

            // 3) Ask for rotation (relative to current UCS)
            var par = ed.GetAngle("\nSpecify rotation angle <0>: ");
            var rotation = (par.Status == PromptStatus.OK) ? par.Value : 0.0;

            // 4) Place valve (NOTE: PlaceValveCore must have the new options param)
            PlaceValveCore(db, ed, insPt, rotation, context, options);
        }

        private static IDictionary<string, string> TryGetLineContextFromPick(Editor ed, Database db)
        {
            var result = new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect source polyline for context or <Enter> to skip: ");
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a polyline.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline), exactMatch: false);

            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return result;

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var pline = tr.GetObject(per.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (pline != null)
                {
                    var xdata = ReadXDataPairs(pline, Constants.AppName);
                    CopyIfPresent(xdata, Constants.KeyLineNo, result);
                    CopyIfPresent(xdata, Constants.KeyService, result);
                    CopyIfPresent(xdata, Constants.KeySize, result);
                    CopyIfPresent(xdata, Constants.KeySpec, result);
                    CopyIfPresent(xdata, Constants.KeyArea, result);
                    CopyIfPresent(xdata, Constants.KeyUnit, result);
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }

            return result;

}

        //private static void PlaceValveCore(Database db, Editor ed, Point3d insPt, double rotation, IDictionary<string, string> context)
        private static void PlaceValveCore(Database db, Editor ed, Point3d insPt, double rotation, IDictionary<string, string> context, ValveInsertOptions options)
        {
            {
                using (var docLock = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument.LockDocument())
                using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    // Ensure RegApp exists for XData
                    EnsureRegAppRecord(tr, db, Constants.AppName);

                    // --- Determine which block to use ---
                    var blockName = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(options?.BlockName)
                        ? Constants.BlockName
                        : options.BlockName;

                    // --- Ensure definition exists (load from drawing or external folder) ---
                    if (!EnsureBlockDefinition(db, blockName, ed))
                    {
                        tr.Abort();
                        return; // bail if we still couldn’t load it
                    }

                    // --- Re-open tables after possible insert (after EnsureBlockDefinition) ---
                    BlockTable bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                    BlockTableRecord btr = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[blockName], OpenMode.ForRead);

                    // Compose valve metadata
                    var uid = Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N").ToUpperInvariant();

                    // Determine sequence scope and get next tag
                    var scope = ComputeValveScope(context);
                    var tag = NodSequenceManager.GetNext(tr, db, Constants.NodValveSeq, scope);

                    // Build final property bag (context + computed tag + identity)
                    var meta = new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                    {
                        [Constants.KeyTag] = tag,
                        [Constants.KeyLineNo] = context.TryGetValue(Constants.KeyLineNo, out var ln) ? ln : string.Empty,
                        [Constants.KeyService] = context.TryGetValue(Constants.KeyService, out var sv) ? sv : string.Empty,
                        [Constants.KeySize] = context.TryGetValue(Constants.KeySize, out var sz) ? sz : string.Empty,
                        [Constants.KeySpec] = context.TryGetValue(Constants.KeySpec, out var sp) ? sp : string.Empty,
                        [Constants.KeyArea] = context.TryGetValue(Constants.KeyArea, out var ar) ? ar : string.Empty,
                        [Constants.KeyUnit] = context.TryGetValue(Constants.KeyUnit, out var un) ? un : string.Empty,
                        [Constants.XdUidKey] = uid
                    };

// --- Insert the block reference ---
                    ObjectId msId = SymbolUtilityServices.GetBlockModelSpaceId(db);
                    BlockTableRecord ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(msId, OpenMode.ForWrite);

                    using (var br = new BlockReference(insPt, btr.ObjectId))
                    {
                        br.Rotation = rotation;
                        br.SetDatabaseDefaults();
                        var brId = ms.AppendEntity(br);
                        tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(br, true);

                        // --- Create attribute references for any matching attributes ---
                        if (btr.HasAttributeDefinitions)
                        {
                            foreach (ObjectId id in btr)
                            {
                                var obj = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead);
                                var ad = obj as AttributeDefinition;
                                if (ad != null && !ad.Constant)
                                {
                                    using (var attRef = new AttributeReference())
                                    {
                                        attRef.SetAttributeFromBlock(ad, br.BlockTransform);

                                        var key = ad.Tag != null ? ad.Tag.Trim() : null;
                                        if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(key) && meta.TryGetValue(key, out var value))
                                            attRef.TextString = value ?? string.Empty;

                                        br.AttributeCollection.AppendAttribute(attRef);
                                        tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(attRef, true);
                                    }
                                }
                            }
                        }

                        // Write XData to the block (CBI_KIND, CBI_UID + mirrored props)
                        WriteXDataPairs(br, Constants.AppName, meta, tr);

                        // Ensure must-have identity keys present as XData scalars
                        AppendXDataScalar(br, Constants.AppName, Constants.XdKindKey, Constants.XdKindValve, tr);
                        AppendXDataScalar(br, Constants.AppName, Constants.XdUidKey, uid, tr);

                        // Persist cross-ref in NOD (Valves/{UID})
                        NodValveRegistry.UpsertValve(tr, db, uid, br, meta);

                        //// Select it so Properties Palette updates
                        //ed.SetImpliedSelection(new[] { brId });

                        // ChangeHistory hook (no-throw)
                        ChangeHistoryHook.TryRecordInsert(uid, br.Handle, meta);
                    }

                    tr.Commit();
                    try
                    {
                        // If you previously selected the new valve, consider not doing that anymore.
                        // Either way, reset implied selection so the palette’s reactors see the next click.
                        ed.SetImpliedSelection(Array.Empty<ObjectId>());
                        ed.Regen();
                    }
                    catch { /* ignore */ }
                }
            }
        }

        private static void CopyIfPresent(IDictionary<string, string> src, string key, IDictionary<string, string> dst)
        {
            if (src != null && dst != null && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(key))
            {
                string v;
                if (src.TryGetValue(key, out v) && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(v))
                    dst[key] = v;
            }
        }

        private static string ComputeValveScope(IDictionary<string, string> ctx)
        {
            var area = ctx.TryGetValue(Constants.KeyArea, out var a) ? a : string.Empty;
            var service = ctx.TryGetValue(Constants.KeyService, out var s) ? s : string.Empty;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(area) && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(service))
                return $"{area}-{service}";

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(area))
                return area;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(service))
                return service;

            return "GLOBAL";
        }

        #region XData helpers

        private static void EnsureRegAppRecord(Transaction tr, Database db, string appName)
        {
            var reg = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!reg.Has(appName))
            {
                reg.UpgradeOpen();
                var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                reg.Add(rec);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, add: true);
            }
        }

        private static IDictionary<string, string> ReadXDataPairs(Entity ent, string appName)
        {
            var result = new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(appName);
            if (rb == null) return result;

            var tv = rb.AsArray();
            // Expected format: (app name), then repeated (key, value) pairs as strings.
            for (int i = 1; i + 1 < tv.Length; i += 2)
            {
                var key = tv[i].Value as string;
                var val = tv[i + 1].Value as string;
                if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key))
                    result[key] = val ?? string.Empty;
            }
            return result;
        }

        private static void WriteXDataPairs(Entity ent, string appName, IDictionary<string, string> kv, Transaction tr)
        {
            var rb = new ResultBuffer();
            rb.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, appName));
            foreach (var pair in kv)
            {
                rb.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, pair.Key));
                rb.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, pair.Value ?? string.Empty));
            }
            ent.XData = rb;
        }

        private static void AppendXDataScalar(Entity ent, string appName, string key, string value, Transaction tr)
        {
            var existing = ReadXDataPairs(ent, appName);
            existing[key] = value ?? string.Empty;
            WriteXDataPairs(ent, appName, existing, tr);
        }

        #endregion

        #region NOD helpers (Sequences + Valve registry)

        /// <summary>
        /// Simple sequence manager stored under NOD: CBI/ProjectStore/Sequences/{seqName}/{scope}
        /// </summary>
        private static class NodSequenceManager
        {
            public static string GetNext(Transaction tr, Database db, string seqName, string scope)
            {
                // Fallback starts at 1; stored as XRecord with a single int.
                var (dict, created) = EnsureNestedDict(tr, db, new[]
                {
                    Constants.NodRoot, Constants.NodProjectStore, Constants.NodSequences, seqName
                });

                var scopeKey = scope ?? "GLOBAL";
                var nextVal = 1;

                if (dict.Contains(scopeKey))
                {
                    var xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(dict.GetAt(scopeKey), OpenMode.ForWrite);
                    var vals = xr.Data?.AsArray();
                    if (vals != null && vals.Length > 0 && vals[0].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.Int32)
                        nextVal = (int)vals[0].Value + 1;

                    xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Int32, nextVal));
                }
                else
                {
                    dict.UpgradeOpen();
                    var xr = new Xrecord
                    {
                        Data = new ResultBuffer(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Int32, nextVal))
                    };
                    dict.SetAt(scopeKey, xr);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(xr, true);
                }

                // Return a formatted tag string; change format to your standard if needed.
                return $"{scopeKey}-{nextVal:000}";
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Stores a cross-reference for each valve under: CBI/ProjectStore/Valves/{UID}
        /// Data: flattened key/value strings + Handle.
        /// </summary>
        private static class NodValveRegistry
        {
            public static void UpsertValve(Transaction tr, Database db, string uid, BlockReference br, IDictionary<string, string> meta)
            {
                var (dict, _) = EnsureNestedDict(tr, db, new[]
                {
                    Constants.NodRoot, Constants.NodProjectStore, Constants.NodValves
                });

                dict.UpgradeOpen();
                var key = uid;

                Xrecord xr;
                if (dict.Contains(key))
                {
                    xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(dict.GetAt(key), OpenMode.ForWrite);
                }
                else
                {
                    xr = new Xrecord();
                    dict.SetAt(key, xr);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(xr, true);
                }

                var flat = new List<TypedValue>
                {
                    new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, "HANDLE"),
                    new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, br.Handle.ToString())
                };

                foreach (var kv in meta)
                {
                    flat.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, kv.Key));
                    flat.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, kv.Value ?? string.Empty));
                }

                xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(flat.ToArray());
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Ensures a chain of nested dictionaries exists beneath NamedObjectsDictionary. Returns the leaf.
        /// </summary>
        private static (DBDictionary dict, bool created) EnsureNestedDict(Transaction tr, Database db, string[] path)
        {
            var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            DBDictionary current = nod;
            bool createdAny = false;

            foreach (var name in path)
            {
                if (!current.Contains(name))
                {
                    current.UpgradeOpen();
                    var child = new DBDictionary();
                    current.SetAt(name, child);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(child, true);
                    current = child;
                    createdAny = true;
                }
                else
                {
                    current = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(current.GetAt(name), OpenMode.ForRead);
                }
            }
            return (current, createdAny);
        }

        #endregion

        private static class ChangeHistoryHook
        {
            // Non-throwing placeholder; wire to your existing ChangeHistoryManager if present.
            public static void TryRecordInsert(string uid, Handle handle, IDictionary<string, string> meta)
            {
                try
                {
                    // Example pseudo-call (adjust to your manager):
                    // ChangeHistoryManager.Record("InsertValve", new { UID = uid, Handle = handle.ToString(), Meta = meta });
                }
                catch
                {
                    // Intentionally swallow to avoid impacting production work.
                }
            }
        }

        private static bool EnsureBlockDefinition(Database db, string blockName, Editor ed)
        {
            // Already present?
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (bt.Has(blockName)) { tr.Commit(); return true; }
                tr.Commit();
            }

            // Try AutoCAD support paths first
            string dwgPath = null;
            try
            {
                dwgPath = HostApplicationServices.Current.FindFile(blockName + ".dwg", db, FindFileHint.Default);
            }
            catch { /* ignore */ }

            // If not found, try our custom directories
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(dwgPath))
            {
                foreach (var dir in BlockLoadConfig.BlockSearchDirs)
                {
                    var cand = Path.Combine(dir, blockName + ".dwg");
                    if (File.Exists(cand)) { dwgPath = cand; break; }
                }
            }

            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(dwgPath))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nBlock \"{blockName}\" not found in drawing or search paths. " +
                                $"Add it to a support path or to one of: {string.Join("; ", BlockLoadConfig.BlockSearchDirs)}");
                return false;
            }

            // Import the block definition from DWG
            using (var srcDb = new Database(false, true))
            {
                srcDb.ReadDwgFile(dwgPath, FileOpenMode.OpenForReadAndAllShare, true, "");
                // This imports/merges the definition named blockName into current db
                db.Insert(blockName, srcDb, false);
            }
            return true;
        }
    }
}

> **Clarifying method usage**
>
> It looks like the issue is with calling the wrong methods. The correct commands should call `ValveInsertService.run()`, `ValveInsertService.RunBall()`, and `ValveInsertService.PlaceValveInteractive()`, not `Run()`, which doesn’t exist as written. We might need to adapt the existing methods or create new ones where needed, like adding `RunButterfly()` for butterfly handling.
>
> **Proposing code edits**
>
> To fix the issue, we need to adjust the `ValveInsertService`: replace `RunBall` to use options, add `RunButterfly()`, and ensure methods are public static. For `Commands.cs`, import `AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services` and call the updated methods like `ValveInsertService.RunBall()` and `ValveInsertService.RunButterfly()`. Also, update `PlaceValveInteractive` to accept a string for specific valve types like "Ball" or "Butterfly" mapped to constants.
>
> Thought for 19s

You’re right—the problem isn’t you. Your `ValveInsertService` doesn’t have the members Commands is calling. Let’s fix it **surgically** with exact edits (no guessing).

### Do these 3 tiny edits in `ValveInsertService.cs`

1) **Rename `run()` → `Run()`** (capital “R”, so Commands can call it)
```csharp
// was: public static void run()
public static void Run()
{
    PlaceValveInteractive();
}
```

2) **Fix `RunBall()`** to use your options overload (you don’t have a `PlaceValveInteractive(string)`):
```csharp
public static void RunBall()
{
    PlaceValveInteractive(new ValveInsertOptions { BlockName = "INST_BALL-VALVE" });
}
```

3) **Add a butterfly entry point** (you call it from Commands):
```csharp
public static void RunButterfly()
{
    PlaceValveInteractive(new ValveInsertOptions { BlockName = "INST_BFLY-VALVE" });
}
```

> You already have `ValveInsertOptions` and the `PlaceValveInteractive(ValveInsertOptions options)` overload, so these will compile cleanly.

---

### Then update the three commands in `Commands.cs`

Make sure you have:
```csharp
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;
```

And route the commands to the fixed entry points:
```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve", CommandFlags.Modal | CommandFlags.UsePickSet)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve()
    => ValveInsertService.Run();

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Ball", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Ball()
    => ValveInsertService.RunBall();

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly()
    => ValveInsertService.RunButterfly();
```

That’s it. This removes the `.Run` not found error and the bad `"Ball"` string overload. If you still see valve-related errors after this, paste the **next 3 errors** and I’ll knock those out too.

You’re not crazy. This **worked before** because all the moving pieces *happened* to line up: filenames matched class names, namespaces were consistent, and Commands were calling the exact method signatures that existed then. Over the last few sessions we’ve had:

- a couple of files overwritten (e.g., TagManagerService.cs),
- deletes/restores (TagStoreV1 / StoreGuards),
- method renames or casing changes (`run` vs `Run`),
- duplicate/renamed classes (two TagReconcileService’s),
- namespace drift (Valves vs Tagging.Services),
- and a few signature changes (e.g., `AllocateNext(...)`).

Any one of those is survivable. Together, they scramble the “public surface” your Commands bind to—so your build explodes even if 95% of the code is fine.

Here’s the fastest way to get you compiling again (zero guessing, minimal surgery):

I'll provide concrete steps for freezing, like using a "compile-first" approach and searching for files labeled "TEMP STUB" to replace later.
>
> **Providing steps to resolve build errors**
>
> I’ll suggest using “Build only AutoCAD2020” and removing shared changes for simplicity. They should enter “triage mode” by commenting out broken service routes. I’ll emphasize “no deletes, only disables.” Then, I’ll remind them to revert all changes via GitHub Desktop or back up the solution folder. I’ll outline steps to restore the missing code directly, if needed, and suggest isolating the build errors.
>
> **Providing stabilization protocol**
>
> To stabilize, I'll suggest they comment out TagManagerService and ValveTag commands in Commands.cs using `#if CBI_MINIMAL`. They can define this at the top of the file. Then, I’ll take ownership of the situation, offer a six-step protocol with focus on minimal surface changes. They’ll freeze changes and enable tools gradually. I'll advise using GitHub Desktop to revert commits and confirm only the affected files are worked on. Also, for compile targets, they'll use the “Error List” and “Build Order.”
>
> Thought for 21s

You’re right to be frustrated. I own that I made changes too broadly and that broke your trust. Let’s stop the churn now and switch to a **stabilize-only** mode that **doesn’t guess**, **doesn’t delete**, and gets you back to a compiling DLL first—then we re-enable features one by one.

Here’s the plan I’ll stick to:

# Stabilization protocol (no more guessing)
1) **Snapshot what you have now**  
   Copy the whole solution folder to `…\_backup_stabilize_2025-11-13`. We will not delete or move files—only *disable* code paths safely.

2) **Freeze the public surface**  
   We only touch:
   - `Commands.cs` (to temporarily *disable* routes that point to missing classes), and
   - Add tiny stubs if a command must exist for compile (clearly marked `// TEMP STUB`).

3) **Do not edit any existing working services**  
   No refactors, no renames, no “maybe this will help”. If a class is missing, we *stub* it; if it exists but is wrong, we *disable the call* from Commands until we have the real file.

4) **Compile-first strategy**  
   Goal is “Build Succeeds”. Functionality can be temporarily disabled behind preprocessor flags so the rest of your tools run.

---

## Step A — Make Commands safe without deleting anything
At the very top of `Commands.cs`, add:

```csharp
#define CBI_MINIMAL_SURFACE
```

Now wrap only the routes that are currently erroring behind flags. Keep the ones you know compile (Line tags, ValveInsertService.Run/RunBall, ProjectStore binds, palettes, etc.). Example:

```csharp
// Tag Manager (temporarily disabled until TagManagerService is restored)
#if !CBI_MINIMAL_SURFACE
[CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans")]
public static void CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans()
    => Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.SweepAutoReleaseOrphans();

[CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve")]
public static void CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve()
    => Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve();

[CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_AddToValve")]
public static void CBI_Tag_AddToValve()
    => Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.AddTagToSelectedValve();
#endif
```

Do the same for anything else raising “type not found / method not found” errors (e.g., ValveTagService, ValveLabelService, ProjectRepairCommands). Don’t delete them—just wrap.

If you prefer not to use `#define`, you can comment those methods out as a block.

---

## Step B — Provide minimal stubs only where absolutely required
If any command **must** exist for your ribbon/menu to load, drop a 10-line no-op stub so the **public name exists** but does nothing. Example:

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectRepairCommands.cs
// TEMP STUB – keep until real implementation is reattached
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectRepairCommands
    {
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStoreClass()
        {
            Application.ShowAlertDialog("[CBI] RepairProjectStore (stub)");
        }
    }
}
```

Same approach for `TagManagerService` if your UI references those commands. (Only add if build keeps complaining *after* Step A.)

---

## Step C — Keep the valve routes strict to what you already have
You shared `ValveInsertService` with these entry points:
- `public static void Run()`
- `public static void RunBall()`
- `public static void PlaceValveInteractive()` (and the options version)

So in `Commands.cs`, use only these (no `.run`, no `InsertValveCommand`):

```csharp
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve", CommandFlags.Modal | CommandFlags.UsePickSet)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve() => ValveInsertService.Run();

[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Ball", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Ball() => ValveInsertService.RunBall();

// If you don’t have butterfly yet, disable it for now:
#if !CBI_MINIMAL_SURFACE
[CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly", CommandFlags.Modal)]
public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly() => ValveInsertService.RunButterfly();
#endif
```

If `RunButterfly()` doesn’t exist, keep it wrapped or comment that command out.

---

## Step D — Build
- Build the **solution** as you’ve been doing.  
- If you still see errors, don’t hunt them—**only** wrap the offending command routes in `Commands.cs` (or drop a tiny stub) until the compile is clean.

Target outcome of this pass: **0 build errors** with a minimal, safe set of commands active.

---

## What this achieves
- No more deletes or wide edits.  
- You get a compiled DLL again so you can run what still works.  
- The “broken” commands are clearly flagged and won’t crash the build.  
- We can then re-enable one tool at a time by replacing stubs with *your* original files (not new code), verifying compile each time.

---

## My commitment going forward
- I won’t guess or rename anything.  
- I’ll only suggest: (a) wrapping a call, or (b) adding a tiny stub, or (c) dropping back in **your** original file verbatim when you provide it.  
- We’ll keep a running list of what’s stubbed so nothing is lost.

If you want, I can write a 1-page **“stabilization checklist”** you can keep in the repo (so future work follows the same non-destructive process).

## Response:
11/13/2025, 10:42:00 AM

You didn’t ruin anything by owning it—resetting to your repo was the right call. Let’s zoom back out to the **original big-picture plan** we’d been building toward and then pick a safe, bite-size order to bring pieces online.

# The big picture (what we were aiming for)
1) **Project Store v1 (single source of truth)**
   - Canonical JSON file: `Project_Store.json` (schema v1.0).
   - DWGs point to it via NOD key: `CBI/ProjectStore/Path`.
   - All tools read/write through **ProjectStoreService** (atomic save), never direct-edit.

2) **Command Registry Standard**
   - All AutoCAD commands are thin one-liners in `Commands.cs` that route to real classes.
   - No business logic in `Commands.cs`.

3) **Tagging system (line & valve)**
   - **ValveInsertService** inserts a valve block and seeds props/XData.
   - **Tag allocation** is centralized (either NOD sequence or Project Store “Systems”).
   - **Tag Manager** palette/service can: allocate, link/unlink, sweep orphans, reconcile DWG↔Store.
   - **Reconcile pass** marks missing handles as `Orphan` in the store.

4) **Linked labels**
   - **LabelLinkService** creates/refreshes labels from the entity’s XData/Project Store.
   - **Refresh All** command cleans up formatting/rotation.

5) **Sheet Set & batch**
   - Sheet Set Editor palette (separate tool) for multi-DWG selection/batch operations later.

6) **QA/QC**
   - XData sanity tools (repair, sanitize) and NOD inspector for debugging.

7) **Scope Guard**
   - Every tool stays inside its folder/namespace; cross-tool changes go through the store/service contracts only.

---

# Suggested bring-up order (one tool at a time)
Each step ends when you can build + run a **single test command**. No other files touched.

## 1) Project Store “binding”
**Goal:** active DWG can bind to a store and remember the path (NOD pointer).
- Commands:
  - `CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG` → BindCurrentDrawingCommand (writes NOD path).
  - `CBI_OPEN_SUPPORT_FOLDER` (optional).
- Definition of done:
  - You can bind a DWG to `Project_Store.json`.
  - Reopen DWG → NOD pointer still correct.

## 2) Valve insert (minimal)
**Goal:** insert a valve block with attributes/XData; no tag manager yet.
- Class: `ValveInsertService` (you already have this).
- Commands:
  - `CBI_PlaceValve` → `ValveInsertService.Run()`
  - (If you have it) `CBI_PlaceValve_Ball` → `RunBall()`
- DoD:
  - Block inserts, attrs populated from context, XData written, no exceptions.

## 3) Tag add (one-shot)
**Goal:** select a valve and assign a new tag (from NOD seq or simple counter) + stamp XData.
- Class: `TagAddService` (your file).
- Command:
  - `CBI_Tag_AddToValve` → adds tag to selected valve.
- DoD:
  - Tag appears in attr/XData and (if wired) persisted to store/NOD.

## 4) Reconcile & orphan sweep
**Goal:** DWG scan to mark missing handles as `Orphan` in the store.
- Class: `TagReconcileService`.
- Commands:
  - `CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans` (or `CBI_TAG_RECONCILE`).
- DoD:
  - Deleting a labeled entity then running reconcile updates status to Orphan.

## 5) Tag Manager (lite)
**Goal:** expose three actions via service/palette, but keep scope small.
- Class: `TagManagerService` (restore your original).
- Commands:
  - `CBI_Tag_Manager` (palette)
  - `CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve`
  - `CBI_Tag_AddToValve` (reuse from step 3)
- DoD:
  - You can release a tag from the selected valve and re-assign it.

## 6) Linked labels
**Goal:** create and refresh labels reliably.
- Class: `LabelLinkService`
- Commands:
  - `CBI_MAKE_LABEL`
  - `CBI_LABEL_REFRESHALL`
- DoD:
  - Make/refresh works; rotation/orientation correct for your standard.

## 7) Optional tools (stage later)
- **ValveTagCommand**, **ValveLabelService**, reports.
- **Sheet Set Editor** palette.
- **QA/QC** utilities.

---

# Working style so we don’t spiral again
- We only touch the **current step’s files**.
- If a command routes to something not in scope yet, we **comment out the one-liner** temporarily (no deletes).
- After each step: **commit** (e.g., `feat(store): bind current DWG`) so you always have a good checkpoint.
- If you want, create a branch `stabilize/2025-11` and merge each step when it’s green.

---

# What I need from you to start Step 1
Tell me which of these you want to bring up **first** (I recommend Project Store binding), and confirm the filenames/namespaces you have for it. If you’d like, paste your current `BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs` and `ProjectStoreNod`/`ProjectStoreService` signatures and I’ll align the command routing exactly—no guesses.

We’ll go one tile at a time. You lead the order; I’ll keep it tight and surgical.

## Response:
11/13/2025, 10:53:19 AM

> **Guidance on architecture preservation**
>
> To ensure architecture preservation across threads, I suggest documenting key components like solution maps, folder structures, namespaces, and contracts. You can use tools like Visual Studio’s "Generate Code Map", Roslyn analyzers, and dependency graphs (DGML). For a "context pack", include basic info, and consider generating class dependencies automatically via templates or scripts. Store documentation in your repo’s docs folder for easy access.
>
> **Architecture tracking toolbox**
>
> To track class relationships, you can use Visual Studio’s DGML maps, NDepend (paid), or open-source tools like Roslyn analyzers and PlantUML for visualizations. For a concrete solution, use `dotnet tool install --global cs2plantuml` or `Graphviz` for graphs. I recommend creating a "New Thread Bootstrap Kit" containing key architecture details like project contracts and class lists.
>
> **Architecture tracking tools**
>
> To track dependencies, you can use Visual Studio’s Code Map (DGML) and Solution Explorer for dependency validation. For CLI, try `dotnet uml` or `cs2plantuml` for PlantUML diagrams. Or, use a simple PowerShell script with regex to generate a basic map. I recommend an "Architecture Snapshot Template" for consistency.
>
> Thought for 17s

You’re not crazy—keeping architecture “in your head” across fresh threads is hard. The fix is to make a **portable architecture snapshot** you can paste into any new chat and also regenerate from the code when things change.

Here’s a pragmatic plan that works well with Visual Studio and your repo.

# A) Make a tiny “Architecture Snapshot” you can paste into new threads
Create `docs/Architecture_Snapshot.md` in your repo. Paste this **template** and keep it current. In any new chat, paste only this file’s contents first—now I have the exact map.

```
# CBI Tools – Architecture Snapshot (vYYYY-MM-DD)

## Solution & Projects
- Solution: CBI_Tools.sln
- Projects:
  - AutoCAD2020 (targets AutoCAD 2020; UI + Commands + DWG ops)
  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project (shared logic, no AutoCAD refs; JSON, helpers)

## Global Contracts
- ProjectStore v1.0 (JSON) — path is stored in NOD key: `CBI/ProjectStore/Path`
  Keys: Version:"1.0", Config{ GlobalValvePrefix, GraceDays, PreferReleasedLowestFirst }, Systems{ Name:{ Prefix, Pad, Start, End, Next } }, Tags[], History[]

## Command Routing Standard
- All commands in `AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs` are one-liners routed to services.
- Command name format: CBI_*; no business logic in Commands.cs.

## Namespaces & Folders (authoritative)
- AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
  - V1.Models.cs ................ v1 DTOs (ProjectStoreV1, TagRecordV1, enums)
  - ProjectStoreService.cs ...... Load/Save + Read*/Write*/Peek*/GetSystemNextList
  - TagStoreV1.cs ............... AllocateNext/Release/MarkOrphan/GetAll
  - StoreGuards.cs .............. AutoReleaseAllEligible()
  - ProjectStoreValidator.cs .... EnsureValid()

- AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
  - BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs .... writes NOD path
  - OpenSupportFolderCommand.cs ..... opens folder
  - (optional) ProjectRepairCommands.cs

- AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
  - ValveInsertService.cs ......... inserts valve blocks (attrs + XData)
  - TagAddService.cs .............. adds tag to selected valve
  - TagReconcileService.cs ........ mark missing handles Orphan
  - TagManagerService.cs .......... Add/Release/Sweep public entrypoints

- AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Commands
  - TagManagerCommands.cs ......... (palette, if present)
  - LineTagCommands.cs ............ line ID labeling

- AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
  - LineTagSettings.cs ............ palette settings + preview

## Canonical Commands (and their entrypoints)
- CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG  -> ProjectStore.BindCurrentDrawingCommand.CBI_BindCurrentDwg()
- CBI_PlaceValve        -> Tagging.Services.ValveInsertService.Run()
- CBI_Tag_AddToValve    -> Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.AddTagToSelectedValve()
- CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve -> Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve()
- CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans   -> Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.SweepAutoReleaseOrphans()
- CBI_MAKE_LABEL        -> Tagging.Services.LabelLinkService.CreateLinkedLabel()   (if present)
- CBI_LABEL_REFRESHALL  -> Tagging.Services.LabelLinkService.RefreshAllInCurrentSpace()

## Cross-Project Dependencies (high level)
- AutoCAD2020 -> AutoCAD_Shared_Project
- AutoCAD_Shared_Project (no references to AutoCAD DLLs)

## Invariants
- Never edit Project Store directly in multiple places; always via ProjectStoreService.
- Don’t move files across namespaces without updating Commands routes.
```

> Tip: keep this snapshot short, exact, and **authoritative**. If a class moves or is renamed, update the snapshot first.

---

# B) Generate a real graph from Visual Studio (so it isn’t just manual)
**If you have VS Enterprise**:  
`Architecture > Generate Code Map for Solution` → this produces a **DGML** graph of project/class dependencies. Save it under `docs/maps/CBI_Tools_CodeMap.dgml` and export a PNG. Commit both. Update it when you make big refactors.

**If you don’t have Enterprise**, use a lightweight, repeatable option:
- **PlantUML class diagram generator** (e.g., `cs2plantuml`, open-source). It scans the solution and emits `.puml`. Then render to PNG/SVG. Keep the output at `docs/maps/CBI_Tools_ClassDiagram.puml/png`.
- Or a **Roslyn-based console** in your repo that walks symbols and outputs a CSV edge list (`FromType,ToType,Relation`). Super small, no GUI. (I can draft this console if you want it.)

The point: you’ll always have a **machine-generated** snapshot to complement the human snapshot.

---

# C) Add a “Scope Guard Header” for each tool
For any focused thread (e.g., Valve Insert), paste a tiny header so we don’t bleed into other tools:

```
# Scope Guard — Valve Insert Tool
- Active files: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/ValveInsertService.cs (and only what it calls)
- Commands touching this tool: CBI_PlaceValve, CBI_PlaceValve_Ball
- External contracts: reads NOD (optionally), writes XData keys [CBI_KIND, CBI_UID], optional attrs
- Out of scope: Tag Manager, Line Labels, Sheet Sets, Project Repairs
```

This keeps the conversation fenced. If we must cross the fence, we say it explicitly and update the scope header.

---

# D) Minimal “Contract Files” that shouldn’t drift
There are 3–5 files that define “how the world works” for your tools. Keep them stable, reviewed, and versioned:

1. `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/V1.Models.cs`  ← schema types  
2. `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs`  ← Load/Save API  
3. `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/TagStoreV1.cs`  ← allocate/release/orphan API  
4. `AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs`  ← command registry (routes ONLY)  
5. `docs/Architecture_Snapshot.md`  ← the paste-me-first file for new chats

When we open any new thread, you paste #5 first. If we ever change #1–#4, we bump a tiny version note in that file header so the snapshot stays true.

---

# E) Build-order guardrail (so solution compiles reliably)
- Set **project dependencies** so `AutoCAD2020` depends on `AutoCAD_Shared_Project` (you already did).
- If a not-yet-restored tool causes errors, **comment out** its single command line in `Commands.cs` temporarily (do not delete files). Keep solution building.

---

# F) How we’ll work in new threads
1. You paste `docs/Architecture_Snapshot.md`.  
2. You say which *one* tool we’re touching. Paste only the 1–2 files for that tool.  
3. I reply only with diffs for those files (plus **very** small stubs if necessary).  
4. You build. If it fails due to outside references, we **comment out** the offending command line for now, note it in the scope header, and proceed.

This gives you continuity across threads and a way to “re-hydrate” the whole architecture in a few seconds without me guessing.

If you want, I can generate a starter `Architecture_Snapshot.md` right now based on what we’ve stabilized (short and precise), and a small Roslyn console that emits a CSV of type-to-type dependencies you can run occasionally to refresh diagrams.
